## P.G. 216-19: Public Access Defibrillation Program

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Revision:** I.O. 104 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To account for and properly document the use of Automated External Defibrillators (AEDs) in assigned commands, and to provide for training and certification of uniformed, civilian, and auxiliary members of the service in their use.

### Key Definitions

**AUTOMATED EXTERNAL DEFIBRILLATOR (AED)** — An electronic device used to defibrillate the heart by applying an electric shock to it.

**UTILIZATION OF AED** — Once an AED is **placed on an aided**, the Department considers that as utilized, **even if a shock is not administered**. When the AED pads are placed on an aided, the machine will perform an analysis and advise what actions should be taken.

> **Exam Alert:** "Utilized" means pads are placed on the patient — a shock does NOT need to be delivered for it to count as utilized.

**CARDIO PULMONARY RESUSCITATION (CPR) ADMINISTERED** — The Department considers CPR administered once a member performs rapid chest compressions or mouth-to-mouth ventilation on an aided.

### AED Kit Contents

> **Exam Alert:** Memorize the kit contents — this is frequently tested.

| Item | Quantity |
|------|----------|
| Operational AED device | 1 |
| Electrode pads with wire leads (sealed packages, each containing two pads) | 2 packages |
| Pocket mask | 1 |
| Gloves | 1 pair |
| Cloth/gauze compress | 1 |
| Razor | 1 |

### Procedure — Desk Officer (Steps 1-6)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Account for **all AED kits** assigned to command at the **beginning of each tour** |
| 2 | Assign an AED at start of each tour to a member trained and certified as AED qualified |
| 3 | Notify radio dispatcher of the unit(s) assigned an AED kit |
| 4 | Make a **Command Log entry** accounting for all AED kits and notification to radio dispatcher |
| 4a | Note assignment of AED kits by their serial numbers, with prefix "AED" (e.g., AED 1234) |
| 5 | Ensure assignment of AED kits are noted on the roll call |
| 6 | Secure AED kits not in use in appropriate location (e.g., radio room, property room) |

> **Sergeant Focus:** AED accountability happens **every tour** — the desk officer must account for all kits, assign them, notify dispatch, and make Command Log entries.

### Member of the Service — Kit Inspection (Steps 7-8)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7 | Inspect AED kit to ensure all items present |
| 8 | Notify desk officer of any deficiencies/missing equipment; make digital Activity Log entries |

### Upon Arriving at Scene of Cardiac Aided Case (Steps 9-13)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 9 | Advise radio dispatcher of aided's condition and request EMS response |
| 10 | Utilize AED in compliance with Department guidelines/training |
| 10a | AED will **only** be attached to an **unconscious adult patient** after confirming: **not breathing** and **no pulse** |
| 10b | Provide care until relieved by advanced-level emergency medical personnel |
| 11 | Brief responding EMS crew of: (a) condition when found, (b) nature of care provided (rescue breathing, CPR, defibrillation, etc.) |
| 12 | Prepare AIDED REPORT; note in "Details" if AED utilized or CPR administered and results achieved; include AED serial number if utilized |
| 12b | If CPR administered, check appropriate caption on AIDED REPORT |
| 13 | Submit AIDED REPORT immediately to desk officer after returning to command |

> **Exam Alert:** AED is **only** for unconscious adults with **no breathing** and **no pulse**. All three conditions must be confirmed.

### Prisoner AED/CPR Use

> **Exam Alert:** If AED/CPR is administered on a **prisoner** in custody, a **MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER (PD244-150)** form will be completed **instead of** an AIDED REPORT.

### Desk Officer — Post-Use (Steps 14-15)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 14 | Telephone ESU desk to obtain an **AED/CPR Log number**; enter on AIDED REPORT or MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER form |
| 14a | Forward copy of completed form to ESU-AED Coordinator via fax and Department mail to ESU, Attention: EMT School |
| 14b | Ensure AED is secured until delivery and is **not reassigned** |
| 15 | Schedule appointment; ensure AED is delivered to ESU-AED Coordinator for downloading of information after each use |

### Training — "BLASTT" Course

> **Exam Alert:** AED training is a one-day course at the **Police Academy** called **"BLASTT"** — Basic Life Support and Trauma Treatment CPR-AED-Naloxone-BTK.

- The commanding officer designates the command's **training sergeant** as the AED liaison
- The command training sergeant schedules members via **T.A.C.T.I.C.S.** (Training Attendance Certification Transcript Integrated Collection System)

### Loss or Theft

If an AED kit or its contents (except cloth/gauze compress and plastic gloves) are lost or stolen, comply with P.G. 219-20, "Loss or Theft of Department Property."

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| AIDED REPORT | (Electronic) |
| MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER | PD244-150 |

---

## P.G. 216-20: Handling Shelter Clients with Tuberculosis

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Revision:** I.O. 99 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To assist the Department of Health and Mental Hygiene in the removal of recalcitrant patients sheltered in Human Resources Administration facilities, who have tuberculosis and are refusing treatment.

### Procedure

#### Operations Unit Personnel (Steps 1-2)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Upon notification by DOHMH that a removal and detention order has been issued: (a) ascertain location of removal, (b) enter in Command Log the name of DOHMH personnel and pedigree information of subject |
| 2 | Notify Communications Section to assign a patrol supervisor, an RMP unit, and ESU to respond |

#### Patrol Supervisor (Steps 3-6)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 3 | Respond to scene and assess the situation |
| 4 | Determine if services of ESU are necessary; cancel ESU if not required |
| 5 | Confirm that the **Removal and Detention Order** is possessed by the DOHMH physician/peace officer at the shelter site |
| 6 | Assign uniformed member to accompany subject and DOHMH physician/peace officer in DOHMH transportation vehicle to the pre-designated hospital |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The patrol supervisor must **confirm the Removal and Detention Order** before proceeding. This is a legal document that authorizes the removal.

### Department's Role — Critical Limitations

> **Exam Alert:** The Department's primary responsibility is to **assist in the arrest and preserve the peace**. Members of the service are:

| Allowed | NOT Allowed |
|---------|-------------|
| Use physical force to restrain subject until hospital delivery (only extent necessary) | Personally assist in **forcing entry** to premises |
| Accompany patient to hospital | **Safeguard property** at location |
| Wear protective equipment | **Secure premises** afterward |

> **Exam Alert:** The Department does NOT force entry, safeguard property, or secure premises — those duties are performed by other agencies at the direction of the DOHMH physician/peace officer.

### Protective Equipment

Responding members will take appropriate precautions including wearing **plastic gloves and facial masks**. Facial masks will be provided at the shelter site by DOHMH.

### Transportation

- DOHMH provides the vehicle
- If HRA cannot provide a vehicle, an ambulance will be requested through Communications
- **Except in extreme cases**, Department vehicles will NOT be used for transportation
- Transport **only** to the designated HHC facility (even if subject is arrested for other conduct)

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 7-10)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7 | Accompany patient along with DOHMH physician/peace officer to pre-designated hospital |
| 8 | Enter details of removal and transport in digital Activity Log |
| 9 | Ensure DOHMH physician/peace officer name and patient name are recorded in Activity Log and Department forms |
| 10 | Comply with A.G. 330-09, "Exposure of Members of the Service to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials" |

### If Patient Becomes Violent or Barricades

Comply with P.G. 221-13, "Mentally Ill or Emotionally Disturbed Persons" and P.G. 221-14, "Hostage/Barricaded Person(s)," as appropriate.

---

## P.G. 216-21: Response to Carbon Monoxide Alarms

**Date Issued:** 10/18/16 | **Pages:** 2

### Key Definition

**CARBON MONOXIDE** — A **colorless and odorless** gas that interferes with the delivery of oxygen to the cells of the body.

| Signs and Symptoms |
|--------------------|
| Headache |
| Nausea |
| Vomiting |
| Confusion and disorientation |
| Cherry red skin (severe cases) |

Carbon monoxide is created by incomplete combustion of burning fuels (gasoline, oil, wood, paper).

> **Exam Alert:** CO is **colorless and odorless**. Cherry red skin indicates a **severe** case.

### Procedure — Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-8)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Promptly respond; identify type of alarm (residential, central station monitored, EMS call) |
| 2 | Interview persons with information regarding location of alarm |
| 3 | **Refrain from entering location** |
| 4 | Request response of **patrol supervisor**, if alarm confirmed |
| 5 | Establish frozen area |
| 6 | Render reasonable aid to victims, if present |
| 7 | Request EMS to respond if necessary |
| 8 | Observe affected individuals for signs/symptoms of CO poisoning; direct them to EMS for treatment |

> **Exam Alert:** Members **DO NOT enter** the location. This is critical — CO is invisible and can incapacitate quickly.

### Patrol Supervisor (Steps 9-11)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 9 | Respond to location |
| 10 | Evaluate situation upon arrival |
| 11 | Maintain frozen area and extend if necessary, until hazard is removed |

> **Sergeant Focus:** Patrol supervisors should be guided by P.G. 212-37, "Hazardous Materials," when responding to CO alarms and setting up frozen areas.

### Patrol Supervisor — Continued (Steps 12-16)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 12 | Establish crime scene, if necessary |
| 13 | Request response of **ESU personnel** |
| 14 | Prevent all non-ESU police personnel from entering location |
| 15 | Notify duty captain and precinct desk officer |
| 16 | Direct Communications to notify Department of Buildings if location is City-owned |

### Emergency Service Unit Member (Steps 17-19)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 17 | Respond to location and determine whether CO gases are present |
| 18 | Advise patrol supervisor on appropriate frozen area boundaries |
| 19 | **Determine whether location is safe for re-entry** |

> **Exam Alert:** Only **ESU** determines whether the location is safe for re-entry. Not the patrol supervisor, not FDNY — ESU.

### After ESU Assessment (Steps 20-25)

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 20 | MOS | Remain outside location until ESU deems location habitable |
| 21 | MOS | Assist EMS with removal of aided(s) if location properly vented and ESU advises safe to enter |
| 22 | MOS | Comply with P.G. 216-01, "Aided Cases General Procedure" |
| 23 | P.S. | Direct preparation of AIDED REPORT and COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET (PD313-152A), if appropriate |
| 24 | P.S. | Confer with ESU to determine if location is safe for occupants to re-enter prior to allowing re-entry |
| 25 | P.S. | Comply with P.G. 212-37, "Hazardous Materials," if a continuing CO threat exists |

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| AIDED REPORT | (Electronic) |
| COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET | PD313-152A |

---

## P.G. 216-22: Mobile Crisis Outreach Teams and Assertive Community Treatment Teams

**Date Effective:** 06/11/24 | **Revision:** R.O. 39 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To safeguard and remove mentally ill or emotionally disturbed persons at the direction of Mobile Crisis Outreach or Assertive Community Treatment Teams pursuant to **section 9.58 of the New York State Mental Hygiene Law**.

### Key Definitions

**MOBILE CRISIS OUTREACH TEAMS** — Independently operated by local hospitals or community-based mental health organizations; usually funded by DOHMH; may be attached to NYS-licensed "comprehensive emergency programs." Goal: assess, stabilize, and rapidly refer patients to longer-term providers.

**ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT TEAMS (ACT)** — Similar to Mobile Crisis Outreach Teams but are "hospitals on wheels," more richly staffed by mental health workers. Provide a wide range of ongoing services. May order involuntary transport of patients to hospital ERs. Receive contract funding from DOHMH and are licensed by the NYS Office of Mental Health.

**QUALIFIED MENTAL HEALTH PROFESSIONAL (QMHP)** — Members of a Mobile Crisis Outreach or ACT Team who are:

| QMHP Eligible Professionals |
|------------------------------|
| Licensed psychologists |
| Certified social workers |
| Registered professional nurses |

> **Exam Alert:** Only these three types of professionals qualify as QMHPs eligible to direct a removal under Section 9.58.

### Procedure — Uniformed Member of the Service

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Comply with P.G. 221-13, "Mentally Ill or Emotionally Disturbed Persons," as required |
| 2 | Request the physician or QMHP on scene to present **section 9.58 identification card** along with signed and completed **OMH 482 form** (Mobile Crisis Outreach Team Authorization for Transport) |
| 2a | Record pertinent information in digital Activity Log and AIDED REPORT, including identity of physician/QMHP ordering removal and length of time aided was in police custody |
| 3 | Inform communications dispatcher that the removal is involuntary, when appropriate |
| 4 | Prepare AIDED REPORT and include: |
| 4a | **Upload photographs** of section 9.58 identification card and OMH 482 form to AIDED REPORT |
| 4b | Note that individual was removed pursuant to section 9.58 of NYS Mental Hygiene Law |
| 4c | Name of QMHP who directed the removal and the mobile crisis team name |

> **Exam Alert:** Two documents must be presented: (1) **Section 9.58 ID card** and (2) **OMH 482 form**. Both must be photographed and uploaded to the AIDED REPORT.

### AIDED REPORT Coding

To accurately record section 9.58 removals, select:
- **'Emotionally Disturbed'** in the 'Aided Person' section
- **'Mobile Crisis Team Removal? — Yes'** in 'Additional Information' section
- **'Involuntary Removal? — Yes'** in 'Additional Information' section

### Additional Data

**Transportation:**
- Officer will request an ambulance to provide transportation
- Officer **rides in the body of the ambulance** with the patient

**Frisk Authority:**
- Officer may frisk individual's person for weapons when transporting an EDP to the hospital
- If individual has a bag or similar item, officer may only frisk the bag for a weapon or safety concern **if the bag is in a grabbable area**
- If the officer feels what appears to be a weapon during the frisk, the officer may search the bag for the weapon

**Hospital Responsibility:**
- The mobile crisis team member ordering the removal is responsible to communicate details to hospital staff and provide required documentation
- Once individual is removed to hospital and registered as a patient, uniformed member presence is **no longer required** unless the patient is exhibiting violent behavior

**Property:**
- Mobile Crisis Outreach or ACT Teams ordering the removal will safeguard all personal property (other than contraband) removed from the patient

**Legal Concerns:**
- Contact the **Legal Bureau** if the validity of a removal is in question

> **Exam Alert:** If the validity of a Section 9.58 removal is questioned, contact the **Legal Bureau** — not IAB, not the district attorney.

---

# Section 216 — Special Aided Cases

## P.G. 216-07: Firearm Safety Stations at Psychiatric Wards and Admitting Areas

**Date Effective:** 07/01/20 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To utilize firearms safety stations, when available, for the safe unloading and reloading of firearms at psychiatric wards or psychiatric admitting areas of city hospitals.

### Procedure — Uniformed Member of the Service

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Escort mentally ill or emotionally disturbed person to designated patient waiting area |
| 2 | Sign in and complete entries under captions in **Psychiatric Admitting Log** maintained by hospital security police |
| 3 | Proceed to designated firearms safety station, when available, and **unload firearm(s)** |
| 3a | Replace unloaded firearm(s) in holster and put loose ammunition, and/or magazine containing ammunition, **into pocket** |
| 4 | Confer with psychiatric admitting staff and provide necessary information |
| 5 | Safeguard patient at hospital until examined by a psychiatrist |
| 6 | Inform psychiatrist of circumstances which brought patient into police custody |
| 7 | Enter details in digital Activity Log and prepare AIDED REPORT (indicate name of psychiatrist and admission number) |
| 8 | Proceed to designated firearms safety station and **reload firearm(s)** prior to leaving hospital facility |
| 9 | Complete "time-out" entry in Psychiatric Admitting Log |
| 10 | Submit AIDED REPORT to desk officer |

> **Exam Alert:** If the psychiatrist **refers a patient to the hospital emergency room** for medical evaluation and clearance, the member shall **reload** firearm(s) at the safety station before leaving the psychiatric area, and **unload upon return**.

> **Exam Alert:** Interim entries must be made in "Remarks" column of the Psychiatric Admitting Log for each unload/reload cycle.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Patrol supervisors/integrity control officers shall comply with unloading procedures and make a **visit entry** in the Psychiatric Admitting Log when supervising subordinates.

### Relieving Member

If safeguarding extends beyond expiration of tour, the relieving uniformed member will:
- Comply with sign-in and firearms unloading procedures
- Inform psychiatrist of circumstances regarding aided

### DOC Forensic Psychiatry

When a mentally ill or emotionally disturbed prisoner is escorted to a Department of Correction Forensic Psychiatry Ward, comply with the regulations of the Department of Correction regarding unloading and safeguarding firearms.

### HHC Facilities for Emergency Psychiatric Admission

| Hospital |
|----------|
| Bellevue Hospital Center |
| Bronx Municipal Hospital Center |
| City Hospital Center at Elmhurst |
| Coney Island Hospital |
| Harlem Hospital Center |
| Kings County Hospital Center |
| Lincoln Hospital |
| Metropolitan Hospital Center |
| North Central Bronx Hospital |
| Queens Hospital Center |
| Woodhull Hospital |

---

## P.G. 216-08: Persons Overcome by Gas, Drugs, or Other Poisonous Substances

**Date Effective:** 07/01/20 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To secure special assistance and obtain samples of poisonous substances in appropriate cases.

### Procedure — Uniformed Member of the Service

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Call an ambulance |
| 2 | Request Emergency Service Unit, if oxygen required |
| 3 | Give ambulance attendant information regarding the nature of the poisoning |
| 4 | Permit doctor or attendant to take the substance, prescription, or container, **if not required as evidence** |
| 4a | If evidence, give **sample** to doctor/attendant and deliver remainder to desk officer or detective |
| 5 | Notify and make samples available to Poison Control Center of the Health Department, if required |
| 6 | Obtain a receipt for such materials |
| 7 | Make digital Activity Log entry of facts and time notifications were made |
| 8 | Prepare AIDED REPORT |
| 9 | Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET (PD313-152A), if necessary |

### Desk Officer Notification

The desk officer will notify the **Operations Unit** in:
- Poisoned or infected food cases
- Wood or denatured alcohol poisoning cases

> **Exam Alert:** The **Operations Unit** (not Poison Control) is notified for poisoned food and denatured alcohol cases.

### Additional Guidelines for Contaminated Food/Drug Samples

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Spoiled/contaminated food from restaurants | Refer to Dept. of Health, Food Poisoning Investigation Unit |
| Person treated at hospital for poisoning (sample available) | Take sample to DOH Laboratories, 455 First Avenue, Manhattan, with REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT (PD521-168) |
| Pattern of several separate poisoning incidents | Notify Poison Control Center; comply with instructions received |
| Mass removal of potentially dangerous product | Handled case by case; **NEVER** remove items from retail establishment to stationhouse without consultation with city/state/federal agencies via Operations Unit |
| Evidence with criminal sanctions | Deliver to Police Laboratory with REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION attached |
| Non-evidence materials | Invoice and forward to Property Clerk |
| Citizen-delivered substance (no public health/criminal allegation) | **Do NOT accept**; refer to private laboratories for civil cases |

> **Exam Alert:** Mass product removal is handled case by case. Under **no circumstances** will items be removed from retail shelves and brought to a stationhouse before consultation with agencies via the Operations Unit.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| AIDED REPORT | (Electronic) |
| COMPLAINT REPORT | PD313-152 |
| REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT | PD521-168 |

---

## P.G. 216-09: Animal Bites

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Revision:** I.O. 110 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To notify the NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene of human exposure to a rabid animal, an animal suspected of being rabid, and/or an animal that has bitten, scratched, or abraded a human being and to provide for observation of the animal involved.

### Key Definition

**HUMAN EXPOSURE TO RABIES** — A bite or a scratch from a rabid animal, an animal suspected of being rabid, or contamination of an open or fresh wound (having bled within the last twenty-four hours) or mucous membranes (eyes, nose, mouth) with infectious material (saliva, nervous tissue) from a rabid or suspected rabid animal.

### Procedure — Uniformed Member of the Service

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Render reasonable aid and prepare AIDED REPORT, if applicable |
| 2 | Comply with P.G. 219-17, "Use of Patrol Kits" |
| 3 | Advise person to thoroughly **wash affected area with soap and water** and seek medical attention as quickly as possible (even if not serious) |
| 4 | Ascertain owner of animal; obtain description and attempt to locate for examination |
| 4b | If animal is located, comply with P.G. 214-33, "Care and Disposition of Animals" |
| 5 | Prepare **DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT (PD311-152)** in ALL instances involving an animal bite and/or dangerous animal; include ICAD Incident number |
| 5a | If the animal is gone upon arrival, a DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT **must still be prepared**, regardless of whether a person was injured |
| 6 | Deliver DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT and submit AIDED REPORT to desk officer |

> **Exam Alert:** The DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT must be prepared in **ALL instances** — even if the animal is gone and no person was injured.

### Desk Officer (Steps 7-13)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7-8 | Review and approve AIDED REPORT; process per P.G. 216-02 |
| 9 | Review and sign DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT; distribute as delineated |
| 10 | Notify DOHMH by telephone of animal bite/exposure |
| 11 | Forward DOHMH copy of report via Mail and Distribution Unit |
| 12 | Notify DOHMH if animal/owner is located after report forwarded; ensure supplementary info recorded on AIDED REPORT in FORMS |
| 13 | File command copy of all DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORTS |

### DOHMH Contact Hours

| Time | Contact |
|------|---------|
| 0900-1700, Monday-Friday | Dept. of Health and Mental Hygiene, **Animal Bite Unit** |
| After hours | **Poison Control Center** of DOHMH |

> **Exam Alert:** After-hours animal bite notifications go to **Poison Control**, not the Animal Bite Unit.

### Critical Additional Data

**Raccoons:** Raccoons are primarily **nocturnal** animals; any raccoon seen active during the **daytime** should be considered rabid until proven otherwise. Any raccoon acting friendly or aggressively should be considered rabid.

> **Exam Alert:** Daytime raccoon = presumed rabid.

**Firearms and Animals:** Uniformed members shall **not discharge their firearms** at a dog or other animal **except** to protect themselves or another person from physical injury and there is **no other reasonable means** to eliminate the threat.

**Snakebite:** Requires notification to the Poison Control Center of DOHMH. Enter notification on AIDED REPORT.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| AIDED REPORT | (Electronic) |
| DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT | PD311-152 |

---

## P.G. 216-10: Taxicab Drivers

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To report certain aided cases involving taxicab drivers to the NYC Taxi and Limousine Commission.

### Procedure

When a taxicab is involved and the driver has epilepsy, vertigo, heart disease, or other condition making him/her unfit to operate a taxi:

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | MOS | Follow normal aided case procedures |
| 2 | MOS | Obtain driver's credentials and give **RECEIPT FOR CREDENTIALS (PD616-011)** |
| 3 | Desk Officer | Forward credentials and facts on **REPORT OF VIOLATION (PD672-151)** to NYC Taxi and Limousine Commission |
| 4 | Desk Officer | Note on REPORT OF VIOLATION if unable to obtain credentials |

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| RECEIPT FOR CREDENTIALS | PD616-011 |
| REPORT OF VIOLATION | PD672-151 |

---

## P.G. 216-11: Communicable Disease

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Revision:** I.O. 109 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To assist in the orderly removal of a person with a communicable disease.

### Definition

**COMMUNICABLE DISEASE** — An infectious disease capable of being passed to another by direct contact with an infected individual or his/her discharges.

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Desk Officer | Assign a uniformed member to assist in the removal of patient |
| 2 | MOS | Comply with A.G. 330-09, "Exposure of Members of the Service to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials" |
| 3 | MOS | Accompany patient to the hospital when requested by attendant or physician |

### Confidentiality Rules

> **Exam Alert:** Information concerning a prisoner's or victim's communicable disease status must be kept **CONFIDENTIAL**.

- Generally should **NOT** be released to: the public, the media, the person's family and friends, or other prisoners
- Any release may only be made with the **written consent of the Deputy Commissioner — Legal Matters**

> **Exam Alert:** Only the **Deputy Commissioner — Legal Matters** can authorize release of communicable disease information (with written consent).

### Prisoner Segregation

- Prisoners who **volunteer** their positive status indicating the need for segregation or medical treatment should be placed in **isolation cells**, if available
- This information may be recorded on a Prisoner Movement Slip or on form **MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER (PD244-150)** to alert Corrections, DA's offices, or courts

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER | PD244-150 |

---

## P.G. 216-12

*(Title and text unavailable in source PDF — review original Patrol Guide)*

---

# Section 216 — Key Terms, Forms, and Critical Numbers

## Definitions

| Term | Definition | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Aided Case | Any occurrence requiring a person OTHER THAN A PRISONER to receive medical aid or assistance (sick/injured, dead, lost, mentally ill, abandoned child, runaway, homeless) | P.G. 216-01 |
| Routine Sick at Home | Aided at residence who is conscious, identified, no dependent adults/uncared children, no other police service needed — requires only Activity Log entry | P.G. 216-01 |
| Routine Sick in Nursing Home | Uniformed members WILL NOT respond; exceptions: injury, cardiac, D.O.A., etc. | P.G. 216-01 |
| Human Exposure to Rabies | Bite/scratch from rabid or suspected rabid animal, or contamination of open wound (bled within 24 hours) or mucous membranes with infectious material | P.G. 216-09 |
| Communicable Disease | Infectious disease capable of being passed by direct contact with an infected individual or their discharges | P.G. 216-11 |
| Line of Duty (Auxiliary) | After "sign-in" on Auxiliary Police Roll Call (APS #10) until sign-out; NO off-duty designation | P.G. 216-13 |
| Carbon Monoxide | Colorless, odorless gas; symptoms include headache, nausea, vomiting, confusion; severe cases show cherry red skin | P.G. 216-21 |
| AED Utilization | Once AED pads are placed on an aided — even if no shock is administered | P.G. 216-19 |
| CPR Administered | Once a member performs rapid chest compressions or mouth-to-mouth ventilation | P.G. 216-19 |
| QMHP | Qualified Mental Health Professional — licensed psychologist, certified social worker, or registered professional nurse eligible to direct a Section 9.58 removal | P.G. 216-22 |
| Mobile Crisis Outreach Teams | Hospital/community-based teams funded by DOHMH that assess, stabilize, and refer patients | P.G. 216-22 |
| ACT Teams | "Hospitals on wheels" — richly staffed mental health teams licensed by NYS Office of Mental Health; may order involuntary transport | P.G. 216-22 |

## Key Forms and Reports

| Form Name | Form Number | Used In |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| AIDED REPORT | Electronic (FORMS) | All aided cases |
| IDENTIFICATION TAG | PD317-091 | Dead bodies, body parts, fetuses delivered to morgue |
| COMPLAINT REPORT | PD313-152 | Deaths, suspicious circumstances, DOC deaths |
| COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET | PD313-152A | Dead body cases, CO alarm cases |
| MISSING/UNIDENTIFIED PERSONS REPORT | PD336-151 | Unidentified deceased or aided |
| Police Accident Report | MV104AN | Vehicle collisions, auxiliary police vehicle collisions |
| DANGEROUS ANIMAL/BITE REPORT | PD311-152 | ALL animal bite/dangerous animal incidents |
| RECEIPT FOR CREDENTIALS | PD616-011 | Taxicab driver cases |
| REPORT OF VIOLATION | PD672-151 | Taxicab driver cases (to TLC) |
| REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT | PD521-168 | Poison/substance cases sent to DOH Laboratories |
| MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER | PD244-150 | Communicable disease prisoners; AED/CPR on prisoners |
| Employee's Notice of Injury | WCD 23 (Law Department) | Auxiliary police LOD injury |
| WITNESS STATEMENT — INJURY TO MEMBER OF DEPARTMENT | PD429-065 | Auxiliary police LOD injury witnesses |
| UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT | PD370-152 | Serious auxiliary police injuries/deaths |
| THREAT, RESISTANCE OR INJURY (T.R.I.) INCIDENT REPORT | — | Auxiliary police force incidents |
| OMH 482 Form | OMH 482 | Mobile Crisis Team Authorization for Transport |
| Section 9.58 Identification Card | — | QMHP identification for involuntary removal |

## Critical Numbers and Time Limits

| Item | Value | Source |
|------|-------|--------|
| Second ambulance call | **20 minutes** after first call if ambulance does not arrive | P.G. 216-01 |
| ME follow-up call | **1 hour** if ME fails to respond | P.G. 216-04 |
| Morgue identification (homicide) | **0900 hours, next day** — officer who discovered body reports to morgue | P.G. 216-04 |
| Reports to Auxiliary Police Section | Within **10 days** | P.G. 216-13 |
| DOHMH Animal Bite Unit hours | **0900-1700, Monday-Friday** | P.G. 216-09 |
| After-hours animal bite notification | **Poison Control Center** | P.G. 216-09 |
| 9-8-8 Hotline availability | **24 hours/day, 7 days/week** in 200+ languages | P.G. 216-01 |
| Rabies wound definition | Open wound that **bled within last 24 hours** | P.G. 216-09 |

## Critical Notification Contacts

| Situation | Notify |
|-----------|--------|
| Poisoned/infected food or denatured alcohol | Operations Unit (via desk officer) |
| Homicide, suspicious death, suicide | Operations Unit, detective squad, Crime Scene Unit (via detective) |
| DOC suspicious death/suicide | Detective squad, CO/duty captain, patrol borough, **IAB** (get log number), Operations Unit |
| Scaffold injury/death | Department of Buildings |
| MTA bus injury/death | NYC Transit |
| Construction site, Parks property, factory/store injury/death | Operations Unit |
| Firefighter killed/injured at fire | DO NOT notify family without **fire officer** permission |
| DOC inmate death | DO NOT notify family unless **Correction supervisor** requests |
| Auxiliary police injury requiring hospital | Operations Unit (promptly) |
| Carbon monoxide alarm (City-owned building) | Department of Buildings (via Communications) |
| Section 9.58 removal validity in question | Legal Bureau |

## HHC Psychiatric Emergency Facilities

| Hospital | Borough |
|----------|---------|
| Bellevue Hospital Center | Manhattan |
| Metropolitan Hospital Center | Manhattan |
| Harlem Hospital Center | Manhattan |
| Lincoln Hospital | Bronx |
| Bronx Municipal Hospital Center | Bronx |
| North Central Bronx Hospital | Bronx |
| Kings County Hospital Center | Brooklyn |
| Woodhull Hospital | Brooklyn |
| Coney Island Hospital | Brooklyn |
| City Hospital Center at Elmhurst | Queens |
| Queens Hospital Center | Queens |

## Persons Authorized to Disturb a Dead Body

| Letter | Authorized Person |
|--------|-------------------|
| a | Paramedic, E.M.T., or doctor (caution: do not disturb evidence) |
| b | Medical Examiner or assistant |
| c | District Attorney or assistant |
| d | Members of Detective Bureau, detective squad, or Crime Scene Unit |
| e | Highway District member assigned as "collision technician" or "collision investigator" |
| f | ESU member, if body is on train tracks or in subway tunnel |

## AED Kit Contents

| Item | Quantity |
|------|----------|
| Operational AED device | 1 |
| Electrode pads with wire leads (sealed packages) | 2 packages (2 pads each) |
| Pocket mask | 1 |
| Gloves | 1 pair |
| Cloth/gauze compress | 1 |
| Razor | 1 |

## Investigating Supervisor Hierarchy — Auxiliary Police

| Priority | Role | Condition |
|----------|------|-----------|
| 1st | Lieutenant platoon commander | If available |
| 2nd | Patrol supervisor | If LPC unavailable |
| 3rd | CO/XO/duty captain | Serious physical injury, likely to die, or death |
