# Section 216 — Review Questions

## Question 1
A uniformed member responds to a residence where a person is conscious, properly identified, and requesting an ambulance for stomach pain. There are no dependent adults or uncared-for children, and no other police service is required. What documentation is needed?

- A) An AIDED REPORT and digital Activity Log entry
- B) Only a digital Activity Log entry
- C) An AIDED REPORT, COMPLAINT REPORT, and Activity Log entry
- D) No documentation is required for routine sick at home

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Only a digital Activity Log entry**

P.G. 216-01 — A "Routine Sick at Home" requires ONLY a digital Activity Log entry when the aided is at a residence AND is conscious and properly identified, no other police service is required, no dependent adults or uncared-for children are present, and no other investigation is needed. All four conditions are met here.
</details>

---

## Question 2
Which of the following persons is NOT authorized to disturb a dead body at a scene?

- A) A member of the Emergency Service Unit, if the body is on train tracks
- B) A member of Highway District assigned as a collision technician
- C) The patrol supervisor directing the investigation
- D) The Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The patrol supervisor directing the investigation**

P.G. 216-04 — Only six categories of persons may disturb a body: (a) paramedic/EMT/doctor, (b) ME or assistant, (c) DA or assistant, (d) Detective Bureau/squad/Crime Scene Unit, (e) Highway District collision technicians/investigators, (f) ESU on train tracks/subway tunnel. The patrol supervisor is not among them.
</details>

---

## Question 3
When a uniformed member responds to an aided case involving a suspected drug overdose (cocaine psychosis), what is the proper disposition of the aided?

- A) Bring the aided to the precinct stationhouse for processing
- B) Have the aided brought to an appropriate hospital facility
- C) Release the aided to a responsible family member
- D) Have the aided transported to a police detention facility for evaluation

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Have the aided brought to an appropriate hospital facility**

P.G. 216-01 — The aided is NOT to be brought to a police facility. The patrol supervisor determines if the aided should be removed to the appropriate hospital by RMP or await the ambulance. Drug overdose/cocaine psychosis aided persons go to the hospital, never to a police facility.
</details>

---

## Question 4
At a psychiatric ward, the attending psychiatrist refers a patient to the hospital emergency room for medical evaluation. What should the uniformed member do regarding their firearm?

- A) Keep the firearm unloaded for the entire duration
- B) Reload at the firearms safety station before leaving the psychiatric area, and unload upon return
- C) Secure the firearm with hospital security
- D) Leave the firearm unloaded and in the holster at all times while in the hospital

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Reload at the firearms safety station before leaving the psychiatric area, and unload upon return**

P.G. 216-07 — If the psychiatrist refers a patient to the hospital ER for medical evaluation and clearance, the member shall reload firearm(s) at the safety station before leaving the psychiatric admitting area, and unload upon return. Interim entries must be made in the Psychiatric Admitting Log.
</details>

---

## Question 5
A raccoon is observed in a park during mid-afternoon, approaching people in a friendly manner. How should this animal be treated?

- A) As a normal animal; raccoons sometimes adjust their schedules
- B) As potentially rabid until proven otherwise
- C) As a danger only if it displays aggressive behavior
- D) As requiring no police action unless it bites someone

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) As potentially rabid until proven otherwise**

P.G. 216-09 — Raccoons are primarily nocturnal animals; any raccoon seen active during the daytime should be considered rabid until proven otherwise. Any raccoon acting friendly or aggressively should be considered rabid.
</details>

---

## Question 6
Under what circumstances may a uniformed member discharge a firearm at a dog or other animal?

- A) When the animal has bitten a person
- B) When the animal is suspected of being rabid
- C) To protect themselves or another person from physical injury when there is no other reasonable means to eliminate the threat
- D) When directed to do so by a supervisor

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) To protect themselves or another person from physical injury when there is no other reasonable means to eliminate the threat**

P.G. 216-09 — Members shall not discharge their firearms at a dog or other animal except to protect themselves or another person from physical injury and there is no other reasonable means to eliminate the threat.
</details>

---

## Question 7
Who may authorize the release of information about a prisoner's communicable disease status?

- A) The commanding officer of the precinct
- B) The Deputy Commissioner — Legal Matters, with written consent
- C) The precinct desk officer, in an emergency
- D) The patrol supervisor on scene

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) The Deputy Commissioner — Legal Matters, with written consent**

P.G. 216-11 — Information concerning a prisoner's communicable disease status must be kept confidential. Any release may only be made with the written consent of the Deputy Commissioner — Legal Matters.
</details>

---

## Question 8
An auxiliary police officer sustains a serious physical injury while on duty. Who acts as the investigating supervisor?

- A) The patrol supervisor, precinct of occurrence
- B) The lieutenant platoon commander, precinct of occurrence
- C) The precinct commanding officer/executive officer/duty captain
- D) The commanding officer of the Auxiliary Police Section

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The precinct commanding officer/executive officer/duty captain**

P.G. 216-13 — The CO/XO/duty captain will be the investigating supervisor if the injured auxiliary police officer sustains a serious physical injury, is likely to die, or dies. The lieutenant platoon commander handles non-serious cases.
</details>

---

## Question 9
How must the Employee's Notice of Injury (WCD 23) be prepared for an injured auxiliary police officer?

- A) Typed by the desk officer
- B) Completed electronically in FORMS
- C) In the auxiliary police officer's own handwriting, if possible, in two copies
- D) Dictated to the patrol supervisor for transcription

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) In the auxiliary police officer's own handwriting, if possible, in two copies**

P.G. 216-13 — The patrol supervisor will have the auxiliary police officer prepare, in own handwriting if possible, two copies of Employee's Notice of Injury (WCD 23). If the member is unable, the patrol supervisor will prepare and submit the report.
</details>

---

## Question 10
An aided case occurs on a NYC Transit train. What is the primary concern regarding service interruption?

- A) Delays may cause commuter complaints
- B) Unnecessary interruption can increase temperatures significantly and may cause panic on crowded trains stopped in tunnels
- C) Service interruptions require MTA authorization
- D) Union regulations prohibit stopping trains for more than 10 minutes

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Unnecessary interruption can increase temperatures significantly and may cause panic on crowded trains stopped in tunnels**

P.G. 216-14 — Unnecessary interruption of train service can inconvenience thousands of passengers and endanger public welfare. In addition, unnecessary interruptions can increase temperatures significantly in trains and stations, and may cause panic on crowded trains stopped in tunnels. Service will be interrupted only when absolutely necessary.
</details>

---

## Question 11
A homicide victim's family needs to be notified. Who is responsible for making that notification?

- A) The desk officer at the precinct of occurrence
- B) The patrol supervisor on scene
- C) The assigned detective
- D) The Operations Unit

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The assigned detective**

P.G. 216-15 — The assigned detective will make notification to relatives or friends of a homicide victim.
</details>

---

## Question 12
An inmate dies in a Department of Correction facility. Regarding notification of the inmate's family, what is the correct procedure?

- A) The desk officer will immediately notify the family
- B) The detective squad will notify the family
- C) Notifications WILL NOT be made unless requested by the Correction supervisor in charge
- D) The Operations Unit will coordinate notification

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Notifications WILL NOT be made unless requested by the Correction supervisor in charge**

P.G. 216-16 — Notifications will NOT be made to relatives or friends of an inmate who dies in a DOC facility unless requested to do so by the Correction supervisor in charge.
</details>

---

## Question 13
When notified of a suspicious death in a DOC facility, which agency must the desk officer notify to obtain a log number?

- A) Operations Unit
- B) Crime Scene Unit
- C) Internal Affairs Bureau
- D) Detective Borough Command

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Internal Affairs Bureau**

P.G. 216-16 — The desk officer must notify the Internal Affairs Bureau and obtain a log number for DOC suspicious deaths and suicides.
</details>

---

## Question 14
The Department considers an AED to have been "utilized" when:

- A) A shock is delivered to the patient
- B) The AED is removed from its carrying case
- C) The AED pads are placed on the aided, even if no shock is administered
- D) The AED is turned on and the analysis is completed

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The AED pads are placed on the aided, even if no shock is administered**

P.G. 216-19 — Once an AED is placed on an aided, the Department considers that as utilized, even if a shock is not administered. When the AED pads are placed, the machine performs an analysis and advises what actions should be taken.
</details>

---

## Question 15
An AED should only be attached to a patient who meets which criteria?

- A) Unconscious adult, not breathing, no pulse
- B) Any unconscious person regardless of age
- C) Any person experiencing chest pain
- D) Unconscious adult or child, not breathing

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**A) Unconscious adult, not breathing, no pulse**

P.G. 216-19 — The AED will only be attached to an unconscious adult patient after it has been confirmed that the patient is not breathing and does not have a pulse. All three criteria must be met.
</details>

---

## Question 16
If AED/CPR is administered on a prisoner in Department custody, what form is completed?

- A) AIDED REPORT
- B) MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER (PD244-150)
- C) Both AIDED REPORT and MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER
- D) COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET (PD313-152A)

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER (PD244-150)**

P.G. 216-19 — If AED/CPR is administered by a uniformed member on a prisoner in custody, a MEDICAL TREATMENT OF PRISONER (PD244-150) form will be completed instead of an AIDED REPORT.
</details>

---

## Question 17
During the removal of a TB shelter client, what is the Department's primary responsibility?

- A) Force entry to the premises and remove the patient
- B) Safeguard the patient's property
- C) Assist in the arrest and preserve the peace
- D) Provide transportation to the hospital

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Assist in the arrest and preserve the peace**

P.G. 216-20 — The primary responsibility of the Department during TB shelter removals is to assist in the arrest and preserve the peace. Members are NOT to personally assist in forcing entry, securing premises, or safeguarding property. Those duties are performed by other agencies at DOHMH direction.
</details>

---

## Question 18
Upon responding to a residential carbon monoxide alarm, what should a uniformed member do?

- A) Enter the location immediately to check for victims
- B) Refrain from entering the location and request the patrol supervisor if alarm is confirmed
- C) Enter the location only if wearing a filter mask
- D) Enter the location after confirming the CO detector is not malfunctioning

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Refrain from entering the location and request the patrol supervisor if alarm is confirmed**

P.G. 216-21 — The uniformed member will refrain from entering the location and request the response of the patrol supervisor if the alarm is confirmed. Only ESU determines whether the location is safe for re-entry.
</details>

---

## Question 19
When a Mobile Crisis Team requests police assistance for an involuntary removal under Section 9.58, what two documents must the QMHP present?

- A) Hospital admission form and physician's order
- B) Section 9.58 identification card and OMH 482 form
- C) Mental Hygiene warrant and court order
- D) DOHMH removal order and patient consent form

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Section 9.58 identification card and OMH 482 form**

P.G. 216-22 — The uniformed member will request the physician or QMHP to present section 9.58 identification card along with signed and completed OMH 482 form (Mobile Crisis Outreach Team Authorization for Transport). Both must be photographed and uploaded to the AIDED REPORT.
</details>

---

## Question 20
If the validity of a Section 9.58 involuntary removal is questioned, whom should the uniformed member contact?

- A) Internal Affairs Bureau
- B) The district attorney's office
- C) The Legal Bureau
- D) The Operations Unit

<details>
<summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The Legal Bureau**

P.G. 216-22 — Uniformed members of the service are reminded to contact the Legal Bureau if the validity of a removal is in question.
</details>

---

# Section 217 — Vehicle Collisions

## Chapter Overview

Section 217 of the NYPD Patrol Guide governs procedures for **vehicle collisions**, including fatal and serious injury collisions, leaving the scene investigations, the Directed Accident Response Program (D.A.R.P.) for towing, city and NYCHA liability cases, aircraft accidents, and chemical test warrant applications for DWI suspects.

Sergeants must understand towing protocols, notification responsibilities, agency routing for city-involved incidents, and the chemical test warrant process — particularly the critical timeframes and escalation procedures.

## Learning Objectives

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

1. Explain the mechanical defect investigation procedures for fatal/serious injury collisions.
2. Define "City Involved" and route reports to the correct city agency.
3. Describe the procedures for investigating leaving-the-scene collisions, including VTL Section 602 authority.
4. Articulate the full D.A.R.P. tow dispatch process, including rotation rules, 30-minute wait, and disposition codes.
5. State the rules for unsolicited tow trucks at collision scenes.
6. Explain aircraft accident procedures, including the pilot's 10-hour reporting obligation.
7. Describe NYCHA-involved accident reporting and notification requirements.
8. Identify the correct reference marker procedures for highway, bridge, and tunnel collisions.
9. Define "Chemical Test Warrant" and explain when it can and cannot be applied for.
10. List the escalation timeline for contacting the ADA in chemical test warrant cases.

## Procedure Index

| File | Procedures Covered |
|------|-------------------|
| [section-217-collisions-general.md](section-217-collisions-general.md) | 217-01 (cross-ref), 217-02 (cross-ref), 217-03 |
| [section-217-city-agency-involved.md](section-217-city-agency-involved.md) | 217-04, 217-11 |
| [section-217-leaving-scene-aircraft.md](section-217-leaving-scene-aircraft.md) | 217-05, 217-06 (cross-ref), 217-08, 217-10 |
| [section-217-darp-towing.md](section-217-darp-towing.md) | 217-09 |
| [section-217-highways-chemical-test.md](section-217-highways-chemical-test.md) | 217-14, 217-16 |
| [key-terms.md](key-terms.md) | Definitions, forms, critical numbers |
| [review-questions.md](review-questions.md) | 15 multiple-choice exam prep questions |

## Study Tips

- Memorize all **six D.A.R.P. disposition codes** (10-99T4 through 10-99T9) — a frequent exam topic.
- Know the **agency routing table** for city-involved incidents (P.G. 217-04).
- Understand the **chemical test warrant** restrictions — especially that the warrant CANNOT be applied for if the operator is the only injured person.
- The **30-minute tow wait** rule and the prohibition against precinct commands making tow notifications are highly testable.
- Know the **reference marker** system: green, 9"x8", every 1/10 mile — and that bridge/tunnel markers must come from the Listing book, NOT posted markers.

---

# Section 217 — City and Agency Involved

## P.G. 217-04: Accidents and Collisions — City Involved

**Date Issued:** 10/18/16 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To assist the City in processing cases in which the City may be a plaintiff or defendant.

### Key Definition

**CITY INVOLVED** — Any reported incident involving personal injury and/or property damage which may involve the City in a civil action.

> **Exam Alert:** "City Involved" is broadly defined — any incident that **may** involve the City, not just confirmed liability situations.

### Procedure

When it is determined that the City may be involved in a civil suit:

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-4)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Prepare **Police Accident Report (MV104AN)** if collision, and check appropriate boxes under "DUPLICATE COPY REQUIRED FOR" |
| 2 | **OR** prepare **AIDED REPORT** in cases of personal injury where City may be involved |
| 3 | **OR** prepare **ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED (PD301-155)** only in cases where City may be involved and **no personal injury** resulted (property damage only) |
| 4 | Ensure AIDED REPORT contains indication that City is involved and name of City agency |

> **Exam Alert:** Three possible reports depending on circumstances: MV104AN (collision), AIDED REPORT (personal injury, non-collision), or PD301-155 (property damage only, no personal injury).

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 5 | Inform persons involved that a claim against the City must be filed with the **Comptroller of the City of New York** within **ninety (90) days** |
| 6 | Submit reports to desk officer |

> **Exam Alert:** Claims against the City must be filed with the **Comptroller** within **90 days**.

#### Desk Officer (Steps 7-8)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7 | Review and sign/approve reports |
| 8 | Forward reports: Police Accident Report in normal manner; ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED per instructions on form |
| 8a | Dispose of buff agency copy if Police Department is the **ONLY** agency involved |
| 8b | Determine City agency involved per the routing table below |

### Agency Routing Table

| Condition | Agency |
|-----------|--------|
| Traffic signs, stanchions, signal lights | Department of Transportation |
| Lampposts | Department of Transportation |
| Parkway, highway, stone wall, curb, fence, guide rail, post | DOT, Bureau of Highways, Legal Department |
| Property damage on Parks Department property; trees on parkland, city streets, or public property | Department of Parks |
| Damaged fire alarm box/post | FDNY, Actions and Claims Unit |
| City hydrants | Department of Environmental Protection **AND** FDNY, Actions and Claims Unit |
| School bus collision | Department of Education, Office of Pupil Transportation |
| Property damage from transit facilities (e.g., falling debris from elevated MTA line) | NYC Transit Law Department |
| Accidents on/near ferry, bridge, tunnel, dock, airport, or seaplane base | Agency concerned |

> **Exam Alert:** City hydrants require notification to **two** agencies: DEP **and** FDNY Actions and Claims.

> **Sergeant Focus:** It is City policy to **pursue claims in its favor**. Members must detail circumstances supporting City claims and obtain witness information. **Full cooperation** with the involved agency or Law Department is **mandatory**.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED | PD301-155 |
| AIDED REPORT | (Electronic) |
| Police Accident Report | MV104AN |

---

## P.G. 217-11: Accidents — New York City Housing Authority Involved

**Date Issued:** 04/13/21 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To assist the New York City Housing Authority in processing cases in which NYCHA may be a plaintiff or defendant.

### Key Definition

**N.Y.C.H.A. INVOLVED** — ALL accidents involving serious injury or death, or an aided case which may involve the Housing Authority in legal action.

> **Exam Alert:** NYCHA Involved covers **ALL** accidents with serious injury/death **or** aided cases with potential legal action — broader than just collisions.

### Procedure

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-3)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Prepare **FIELD REPORT** using FORMS |
| 1a | A legible **handwritten copy** must be completed if electronic access is not available |
| 2 | Ensure FIELD REPORT contains: |
| 2a | **Contributory conditions** (no lights in stairwell, rubbish on stairs, broken sidewalk, icy condition, etc.) **OR** |
| 2b | **Non-contributory conditions** (stairwell well lighted, dry, no obstructions, sidewalk clear, etc.) |
| 3 | Telephone **PSA desk officer** and report all pertinent details |

> **Exam Alert:** The FIELD REPORT must document **either** contributory **or** non-contributory conditions — one or the other must always be stated.

#### Desk Officer (Steps 4-5)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 4 | Make determination of severity and make notifications: |
| 4a | **Business hours (Mon-Fri):** Notify manager of NYCHA location AND forward copies to **NYCHA Law Department Tort Division, 75 Park Place, 11th Floor, NYC 10007** |
| 4b | **After hours/holidays/weekends:** Desk officer makes notifications on **next business day** |
| 5 | Make Command Log entry of notification, including name of person notified |

> **Exam Alert:** After-hours NYCHA notifications are deferred to the **next business day** — they are NOT made immediately.

### Additional Data

- Notifications for serious injury or death resulting from **criminal action** are **excluded** from this procedure
- Copies of all reports, including ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED if prepared, forwarded to Housing Authority Insurance Division for informational purposes
- NYCHA policy is to **pursue claims in its favor** — full cooperation is **mandatory**

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| FIELD REPORT | 313-1511 |
| ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED | PD301-155 |

---

# Section 217 — Collisions General

## P.G. 217-01: Vehicle Collisions — General Procedure

*(Text unavailable in source PDF — review original Patrol Guide)*

This procedure establishes the general framework for responding to and investigating vehicle collisions, including scene management, report preparation, and notifications.

---

## P.G. 217-02: Vehicle Collisions Which Result in Death, Serious Injury and Likely to Die, or Critical Injury

*(Text unavailable in source PDF — review original Patrol Guide)*

This procedure covers the specific steps for handling collisions resulting in death, serious injury (likely to die), or critical injury, including Collision Investigation Squad notification and specialized reporting requirements.

---

## P.G. 217-03: Vehicle Collisions — Death/Serious Injury/Critical Injury — Mechanical Defect Involved

**Date Issued:** 08/19/13 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To examine a vehicle involved in a collision which has resulted in death, serious injury and likely to die, or critical injury to ascertain if the vehicle is mechanically defective.

### Key Definition

**CRITICAL INJURY** — A critical injury will be determined based upon the on-scene assessment of Emergency Medical Services (EMS) personnel.

> **Exam Alert:** Critical injury is determined by **EMS on-scene assessment**, not by police or hospital personnel.

### Procedure

When a vehicle has been involved in a collision resulting in death, serious injury/likely to die, or critical injury and either the operator indicates or Highway Unit personnel suspect a mechanical defect:

#### Highway Unit Officer (Steps 1-5)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Prepare **MOTOR VEHICLE COLLISION AND MECHANISM REPORT (PD301-151)** |
| 1a | Prepare WITNESS STATEMENT — VEHICLE COLLISION (PD301-061), if appropriate |
| 2 | Prepare **Early Notification of Fatal Accident (MV-104EN)**, if appropriate, and **fax to NYS DMV within twenty-four (24) hours** |
| 3 | Prepare **Police Report for Fatal Motor Vehicle Accidents (MV-104D)**, if appropriate |
| 3a | Complete a **separate** Police Report for Fatal Motor Vehicle Accidents when more than one person is deceased or more than three vehicles are involved |
| 4 | Forward copies of Early Notification and Police Report to Collision Investigation Squad concerned |
| 5 | Report facts to desk officer, precinct of occurrence, and assigned member of Collision Investigation Squad |

> **Exam Alert:** The Early Notification of Fatal Accident must be faxed to NYS DMV within **24 hours**. A separate MV-104D is needed if more than one person is deceased or more than three vehicles are involved.

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 6-7)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 6 | Summon Department tow truck and have vehicle removed to Department facility |
| 7 | Prepare PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET (PD521-141A) |

#### Desk Officer, Precinct of Occurrence (Steps 8-10)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 8 | Enter rank, name, and shield number of Highway Unit officer in Command Log |
| 8a | Note in entry if mechanical defect is a possible factor in collision |
| 9 | Make reference to Command Log entry on Police Accident Report (MV104AN) |
| 10 | Have PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE (PD521-141) prepared from WORKSHEET, review and digitally sign |

#### Desk Officer/Supervisor at Department Facility (Steps 11-13)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 11 | **Do not permit removal** of vehicle if required as evidence or until the defect has been corrected |
| 12 | Obtain **signed statement** from person correcting mechanical defect and file in Property Receipt Book |
| 13 | Permit removal only by properly equipped tow truck if defect is not corrected, provided vehicle is not required as evidence |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The supervisor at the Department facility must ensure the vehicle is not released prematurely — evidence preservation and defect correction are both gates to release.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| MOTOR VEHICLE COLLISION AND MECHANISM REPORT | PD301-151 |
| WITNESS STATEMENT — VEHICLE COLLISION | PD301-061 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE | PD521-141 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET | PD521-141A |
| Police Accident Report | MV104AN |
| Early Notification of Fatal Accident | MV-104EN |
| Police Report for Fatal Motor Vehicle Accidents | MV-104D |

---

# Section 217 — Directed Accident Response Program (D.A.R.P.)

## P.G. 217-09: Directed Accident Response Program (D.A.R.P.)

**Date Effective:** 06/14/22 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 74 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To direct the response of designated tow trucks, through the Communications Section, to the scene of vehicular collisions, for vehicles which the reporting member deems unsafe to be driven, except that an operator of a vehicle with a gross vehicle weight of **fifteen thousand (15,000) pounds or more** may choose their own company.

### Scope

This procedure does **NOT** include collisions occurring in:
- Tunnels
- Bridges
- Arterial highways serviced by franchised towing companies under DOT permits

> **Exam Alert:** Administrative Code Section 20-524(c) **exempts governmental agencies** from D.A.R.P. — government vehicle operators may call their own tow service.

### Key Administrative Code Provisions

| Section | Requirement |
|---------|-------------|
| 20-518(a)(3) | Participants must maintain business premises under exclusive control, open to public, not shared with other towing companies |
| 20-518(b) | Vehicle removed at police direction must be towed by D.A.R.P. participant and stored at the facility listed on their license |

### Procedure

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-3)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Comply with P.G. 217-01, "Vehicle Collisions — General Procedure" |
| 2 | Ascertain if vehicle(s) can be safely driven, or if towing is required using **reasonableness** as a standard |
| 2a | Operators of **government vehicles** or vehicles with GVW of **15,000 lbs or more** may use their own tow company |
| 2b | A uniformed member may authorize removal if owner/operator is **incapacitated, not present, or refuses** to authorize the tow |
| 3 | Notify Communications Section dispatcher when towing service is needed |
| 3a | Include the **color, body type, and plate number(s)** of vehicle(s) requiring a tow |

> **Exam Alert:** Once the uniformed member makes a determination that a tow truck is needed, the **request may NOT be cancelled**.

> **Exam Alert:** **DO NOT TRANSMIT** the make, model, or year of the disabled vehicle(s) involved in a collision.

#### Communications Section Dispatcher (Steps 4-5)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 4 | Inform uniformed member of the name of the towing company responding |
| 5 | Dispatch patrol supervisor |

> **Exam Alert:** The D.A.R.P. operator at Communications maintains the tow company list and makes notifications on a **strict rotation basis**. **PRECINCT COMMANDS ARE NOT AUTHORIZED TO MAKE NOTIFICATIONS.**

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 6-9)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 6 | Allow **only** the tow truck dispatched by Communications to remove vehicle(s), **except:** |
| 6a | Motorist with vehicle GVW of 15,000+ lbs requesting private tow |
| 6b | Arrest evidence vehicles |
| 6c | Vehicles held as investigatory evidence |
| 6d | Vehicles subject to Rotation Tow Program as stolen or abandoned |
| 6e | Government vehicles |
| 7 | Remain on scene for **thirty (30) minutes** after radio dispatcher advises which tow company is responding |
| 8 | Inform motorist(s) of tow company name and supply D.A.R.P. operator telephone number |
| 8a | Instruct motorist to call after 30 minutes if tow has not arrived, prepared with plate number and collision location |

> **Exam Alert:** If the tow company does not arrive within **30 minutes**: (1) request Communications to **cancel** and assign next in rotation, (2) prepare **REPORT OF VIOLATION (PD672-151)** for the cancelled company, (3) advise dispatcher when resuming patrol. Communications enters disposition code **10-99T9**.

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 9 | Enter tow company information on Police Accident Report (MV104AN) **only when** the vehicle is towed and the tow company is known |

### Unsolicited Tow Trucks

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 9a | If unsolicited tow truck arrives or is found at scene with probable cause to believe it responded without a specific request: issue summons for **Admin Code Section 20-518(b)(2)** (unsolicited response), prepare REPORT OF VIOLATION |
| 9b | If probable cause of **solicitation** of involved parties: issue **additional** summons for **Admin Code Section 20-515(b)** (soliciting at collision scene). Also applies to persons soliciting from a private vehicle |

> **Exam Alert:** Members of the service **may NOT seize unlicensed tow trucks** for the sole purpose of being unlicensed by the Department of Consumer and Worker Protection.

### Patrol Supervisor (Steps 10-13)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 10 | Respond to location and supervise removal of involved vehicles |
| 11 | Supervise issuance of summonses to tow truck operators |
| 12 | Ensure preparation of REPORT OF VIOLATION when warranted |
| 12a | If witness advises a tow company removed a vehicle **prior to officer's arrival**, have REPORT OF VIOLATION prepared with witness information |
| 13 | Direct reporting officer to enter information regarding vehicle towed without owner/operator's knowledge into FINEST "IMPOUND" screen |
| 13a | Ensure notification is sent to registered owner of vehicle |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The patrol supervisor has direct responsibility for supervising vehicle removal, summons issuance, and ensuring the IMPOUND screen is updated for towed vehicles.

### Desk Officer (Steps 14-17)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 14 | If inquiry is made at stationhouse and FINEST "IMPOUND" screen has no information, call D.A.R.P. operator for vehicle location |
| 15 | Review and sign REPORT OF VIOLATION |
| 16 | Assign precinct serial number to REPORT OF VIOLATION |
| 17 | Forward REPORT OF VIOLATION to **Department of Consumer and Worker Protection, Licensing Enforcement Section, 42 Broadway, New York, NY 10004** |

### D.A.R.P. Disposition Codes

| Code | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **10-99T4** | Vehicle Collision (No tow required) |
| **10-99T5** | Vehicle Collision (D.A.R.P. tow service) |
| **10-99T6** | Vehicle Collision (Other tow service) |
| **10-99T7** | Vehicle Collision (D.A.R.P. and other tow service) |
| **10-99T8** | Vehicle Collision (Highway-authorized tow service) |
| **10-99T9** | Vehicle Collision (D.A.R.P. requested, waited 30 minutes, made second request, resumed patrol) |

> **Exam Alert:** Memorize all six codes. The only other acceptable code at a collision is **10-90Z** (Gone on Arrival) — used only when vehicle(s) are not at scene and no information is available about removal.

### REPORT OF VIOLATION Entries

| Violation | Reference |
|-----------|-----------|
| Unjustifiable refusal to accept towing assignment | Article XI, subdivision F (DARP) |
| Repeated unjustifiable refusals to accept towing assignments | Article XI, subdivision L (DARP) — list times and dates |

### Tow Authorization Requirements

The dispatched tow truck must possess and present a **"Tow Authorization" form** that includes a **Department of Consumer and Worker Protection license number**. Both tow trucks and operators must be licensed by DCWP.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| Police Accident Report | MV104AN |
| REPORT OF VIOLATION | PD672-151 |

---

# Section 217 — Highway Reports and Chemical Test Warrants

## P.G. 217-14: Preparation of Police Accident Reports (MV104AN) for Collisions Occurring on Highways, Bridges, and Tunnels

**Date Effective:** 10/24/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 108 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure that the appropriate reference marker numerals/letters are utilized when preparing a Police Accident Report (MV104AN).

### Procedure

Upon notification or observation of a collision on limited access highways, collector-distributor roadways (CDRs), entrance/exit ramps, bridges, and tunnels:

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-4)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Comply with P.G. 217-01, "Vehicle Collisions — General Procedure" |
| 2 | Locate the nearest **reference marker** to the collision site (for highways, CDRs, and ramps only) |
| 3 | Utilize the **Limited Access Highway Reference Marker Listing book** (maintained at each command) to determine the proper reference marker |
| 3a | For collisions on **bridges or in tunnels**: use the Listing book — reference markers actually posted on bridges/tunnels near the collision site **will NOT be entered** on Police Accident Reports |
| 3b | Use the Listing book when a reference marker is missing or cannot be seen due to inclement weather |
| 4 | Enter numerals/letters into the **twelve box** caption titled "Reference Marker" on the Police Accident Report |

> **Exam Alert:** For **bridge and tunnel** collisions, always use the **Listing book** — NEVER use markers physically posted at the scene.

#### Command Clerical Staff Member (Steps 5-6)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 5 | Record collision into the On-Line Accident Index |
| 6 | Enter reference marker numerals/letters on the "ADD ACCIDENT" screen under "IF HIGHWAY OR BRIDGE, ENTER REFERENCE MARKER" |

#### Quality of Life Officer (Steps 7-9)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7 | Review **all** Police Accident Reports for completeness and accuracy |
| 8 | Ensure "Reference Marker" caption is completed when required |
| 9 | Update On-Line Accident Index |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The Quality of Life Officer reviews all Police Accident Reports — supervisors should verify this review is happening consistently.

### Additional Data — Reference Marker Description

- **Size:** 9 inches × 8 inches
- **Color:** Green
- **Placement:** Every **one-tenth (1/10) of a mile** on limited access highways, CDRs, and entrance/exit ramps
- **Caption on report:** Twelve box "Reference Marker" caption on Police Accident Report

> **Exam Alert:** Reference markers are **green**, **9"×8"**, placed every **1/10 mile**.

---

## P.G. 217-16: Chemical Test Warrant Application

**Date Issued:** 02/04/15 | **Pages:** 5

### Purpose

To apply for a chemical test warrant.

### Key Definition

**CHEMICAL TEST WARRANT** — A Court Order and process directing medical personnel to draw blood from a person suspected of driving while under the influence of alcohol or drugs while causing serious physical injury or death to **another individual**, in order to determine the alcoholic or drug content of the person's blood.

A chemical test warrant will be applied for if a prisoner, arrested for DWI involving a collision resulting in serious physical injury or death to **another individual**:
- Has **refused** to submit to a chemical test, **OR**
- Is **unconscious** and has been under arrest for more than **two (2) hours** without being subjected to a chemical test

> **Exam Alert:** The warrant is for blood draws from DWI suspects who **refuse** testing or are **unconscious for more than 2 hours** without a test.

> **Exam Alert:** If the operator is the **ONLY** person with serious physical injury and no other person suffers serious injury or death, a chemical test warrant **CANNOT** be applied for.

### Procedure

#### Patrol Supervisor (Step 1)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Request Communications Section dispatcher to dispatch **Highway District** personnel to designated testing location or hospital |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The patrol supervisor initiates the Highway District request — this is the first step in the chemical test warrant process.

#### Desk Officer, Precinct of Arrest (Steps 2-4)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 2 | Contact **Operations Unit** and request notification to District Attorney's Office |
| 3 | Provide Operations Unit with: (a) arresting officer's name, tax registry number, shield, and command; (b) prisoner's pedigree; (c) desk telephone number of testing location |
| 4 | Make Telephone Record entry regarding notification |

#### Operations Unit Member (Steps 5-7)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 5 | Notify appropriate District Attorney's Office |
| 6 | Provide ADA with: arresting officer info, desk telephone of testing location, prisoner's pedigree |
| 7 | Notify desk officer, precinct of arrest, and provide ADA's name and telephone number |

#### Arresting Officer (Step 8)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 8 | Notify desk officer if **one hour** has passed and ADA has not initiated contact at testing location |

> **Exam Alert:** The **1-hour** mark triggers escalation if the ADA has not made contact.

#### Desk Officer (Step 9) — Escalation

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 9 | Contact **ADA supervisor** and Operations Unit if one hour has passed without ADA contact |
| 9a | Record phone call attempt(s) in Telephone Record |
| 9b | Contact ADA supervisor **every thirty (30) minutes** thereafter until ADA is contacted |

> **Exam Alert:** Escalation timeline: 1 hour → contact ADA supervisor; then every 30 minutes until resolved.

#### Arresting Officer (Steps 10-13)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 10 | Inform ADA of details of arrest |
| 11 | Comply with ADA instructions regarding arrest process |
| 12 | Complete all necessary arrest paperwork per P.G. 217-02 and P.G. 208-40 ("Intoxicated or Impaired Driver Arrest") |
| 13 | Complete **CHEMICAL TEST WARRANT APPLICATION (PD374-041)** |

### Articulation Requirements

To apply for the warrant, the member must articulate to the ADA and the judge that there is reasonable cause to believe the defendant:
- (a) Operated a vehicle and during said operation,
- (b) Killed or caused a serious physical injury to **another individual**,
- (c) Was intoxicated or impaired by alcohol or drugs,
- (d) Is under arrest, and
- (e) Has refused a chemical test, or is unconscious and under arrest for more than two hours without a test

#### I.D.T.U. Technician (Steps 14-17)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 14 | Respond to designated testing location or hospital |
| 15 | Prepare and sign **Report of Refusal to Submit to Chemical Test (NYSDMV AA-134)**, when applicable |
| 15a | Have arresting officer sign Report of Refusal |
| 16 | Distribute copies of Report of Refusal as indicated on form |
| 16a | Arresting officer must bring **two copies** for the ADA when the complaint is drawn |
| 17 | Supply arresting officer with chemical test evidence kit |

> **Exam Alert:** If prisoner refuses, maintain the **initial charge** — the arresting officer must articulate circumstances supporting the original DWI charge. After arraignment, the court forwards a **NOTICE OF SUSPENSION and/or NOTICE OF HEARING** for a hearing within **fifteen (15) days** of arraignment.

### Upon Approval of Chemical Test Warrant

#### Desk Officer, Testing Location (Steps 18-19)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 18 | Provide transportation for arresting officer and prisoner to designated hospital |
| 19 | Make Command Log entry: arresting officer name/shield, prisoner's pedigree, Department auto number, time of transport |

#### Arresting Officer (Steps 20-23)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 20 | Upon arrival, inform and submit chemical test warrant and evidence kit to hospital personnel |
| 21 | Notify ADA and Operations Unit if hospital personnel **do not comply** with the warrant |
| 22 | Comply with ADA instructions |
| 23 | Comply with P.G. 218-49, "Immediate Delivery of DNA Evidence to the Police Laboratory and the OCME," upon receiving evidence kit |

#### Desk Officer, Testing Location (Step 24)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 24 | Ensure prisoner is transported to borough court section facility or testing location to complete arrest processing |

### If Chemical Test Warrant Application Is Denied

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 25 | Arresting Officer | Inform desk officer of denial |
| 26 | Arresting Officer | Comply with ADA instructions regarding continuation of arrest processing |
| 27 | Desk Officer | Make Command Log entry regarding denial |
| 28 | Desk Officer | Ensure prisoner transported to borough court section facility |

### Additional Data

- If prisoner is unconscious at hospital, arresting officer requests Communications to dispatch Highway District with chemical test evidence kit. Samples taken by hospital personnel will be witnessed, sealed, and marked by Highway District personnel and delivered to Police Laboratory by arresting officer
- If more than **two hours** elapsed since arrest and unconscious prisoner has not been tested, a warrant **must** be obtained
- **Legal Bureau** available Monday–Friday, **0700–2300 hours**
- **After hours:** contact Operations Unit for Department Attorney notification

> **Exam Alert:** Legal Bureau hours: Mon–Fri, 0700–2300. After hours: Operations Unit.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| CHEMICAL TEST WARRANT APPLICATION | PD374-041 |
| Report of Refusal to Submit to Chemical Test | NYSDMV AA-134 |

---

# Section 217 — Leaving the Scene, Aircraft Accidents, and Department Property

## P.G. 217-05: Leaving the Scene of a Collision

**Date Issued:** 08/13/19 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To investigate reports of leaving the scene of vehicle collisions.

### Procedure

Upon arrival at a collision scene where the operator has fled without reporting, follow P.G. 217-01 and:

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Step 1)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Prepare **COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET (PD313-152A)**, unless the operator who left is apprehended and issued a summons for Leaving the Scene prior to preparation of the worksheet |

> **Exam Alert:** No COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET is needed if the operator is caught and summonsed **before** the worksheet is prepared.

#### If New York Registration Number Is Obtained (Steps 2-4)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 2 | Query **NYSPIN through FINEST** to determine if vehicle is reported stolen |
| 2a | **DO NOT** give complainant hard copy |
| 2b | Provide information utilizing **COLLISION INFORMATION EXCHANGE (PD301-157)** |
| 3 | Obtain name and address of registered owner |
| 4 | Give information to complainant |

> **Exam Alert:** Never give the complainant a hard copy of the NYSPIN query. Use the COLLISION INFORMATION EXCHANGE form instead.

#### If Vehicle Registered in Another State (Steps 5-6)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 5 | Query NYSPIN through FINEST to determine identity of registered owner |
| 5a | Request information through an adjoining command if command FINEST is inoperable |
| 6 | Provide complainant with information, if identity is obtained |

### Operational Considerations — Case Disposition

In determining whether to close a case or refer to the detective squad, consider:

| Factor | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| a. | Extent of damage |
| b. | Whether facts/degree of damage indicate operator **intentionally** left knowing damage was caused |
| c. | Availability of witnesses to testify |
| d. | Timeliness of complaint |
| e. | Whether report is for **prosecution** or **insurance** purposes |
| f. | Other relevant factors |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The patrol supervisor should weigh these six factors when deciding disposition. Intent and witness availability are the most critical factors favoring detective referral.

### Property Damage Only — Special Authority

In **property damage only** cases, a summons returnable to the Traffic Violations Bureau may be served **even though the member did not observe the offense** (Vehicle and Traffic Law, **Section 602**).

> **Exam Alert:** VTL Section 602 is an exception to the general rule requiring observation — members can summons for leaving the scene of a property-damage-only collision without witnessing it.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET | PD313-152A |
| COLLISION INFORMATION EXCHANGE | PD301-157 |

---

## P.G. 217-06: Department Vehicle Collisions

*(Text unavailable in source PDF — review original Patrol Guide)*

This procedure covers reporting and investigation requirements when Department vehicles are involved in collisions.

---

## P.G. 217-08: Aircraft Accidents

**Date Issued:** 08/28/19 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To determine the cause of, and record data concerning aircraft accidents.

### Procedure

On arrival at the scene of an aircraft accident, follow P.G. 216-01, "Aided Cases General Procedure," and:

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-5)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Request patrol supervisor to respond |
| 2 | Notify the desk officer |
| 3 | **Detain pilot and aircraft** |
| 4 | Safeguard evidence until examined by authorized personnel |
| 5 | Advise the owner or pilot of responsibility to report the accident to the desk officer within **ten (10) hours** — failure to do so is a **misdemeanor** |

> **Exam Alert:** The pilot must report to the desk officer within **10 hours**. Failure is a **misdemeanor**.

#### Desk Officer (Step 6)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 6 | Notify: |
| 6a | Detective squad concerned |
| 6b | Operations Unit |
| 6c | Duty captain |
| 6d | Borough office |

#### Supervising Officer in Command (Step 7)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7 | Prepare and forward report of unusual occurrence with **two extra copies** for **Chief of Special Operations** |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The supervising officer must ensure the unusual occurrence report includes the two additional copies for the Chief of Special Operations.

### Additional Data

An authorized investigator (**FAA or Aviation Unit**) determines the cause of the accident, supplies necessary information to the detective, and recommends the release of the pilot and plane when feasible.

> **Exam Alert:** The **FAA or Aviation Unit** — not the NYPD — determines the cause of the aircraft accident.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT | PD370-152 |

---

## P.G. 217-10: Accidents — Department Property

**Date Issued:** 07/01/20 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To report damage to Department property **other than Department vehicles**.

### Procedure

#### Uniformed Member of the Service (Step 1)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Report facts to desk officer and make digital Activity Log entry |

#### Desk Officer (Step 2)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 2 | Have **patrol supervisor** investigate facts and prepare report, including: |
| 2a | Date and time of occurrence |
| 2b | Details |
| 2c | Department property involved |
| 2d | Damage to other property |
| 2e | Owner of that property |
| 2f | Action taken |
| 2g | Witnesses |
| 2h | Brief description of injury |

> **Sergeant Focus:** The **patrol supervisor** investigates and prepares the report for Department property damage — this is a direct supervisory responsibility.

#### Patrol Supervisor (Step 3)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 3 | Submit report to command clerk |

#### Command Clerk (Step 4)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 4 | Type report on **Typed Letterhead (four copies)** |
| 4a | Submit to desk officer/counterpart |

#### Desk Officer (Steps 5-6)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 5 | Have patrol supervisor sign report |
| 6 | File one copy and forward three copies as follows: |

### Forwarding Table

| Property | Forward To |
|----------|-----------|
| Booths, buildings | Deputy Commissioner — Management & Budget, Facilities Management Division |
| Horses | Mounted Unit |
| Aircraft, launches | Office of the Chief of Special Operations |

> **Exam Alert:** Know the three-way routing: booths/buildings → Management & Budget; horses → Mounted Unit; aircraft/launches → Chief of Special Operations.

### Key Forms

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| Typed Letterhead | (4 copies) |

---

# Section 217 — Key Terms

## Key Definitions

| Term | Definition | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| **Critical Injury** | Determined based upon the on-scene assessment of Emergency Medical Services (EMS) personnel | P.G. 217-03 |
| **City Involved** | Any reported incident involving personal injury and/or property damage which may involve the City in a civil action | P.G. 217-04 |
| **N.Y.C.H.A. Involved** | ALL accidents involving serious injury or death, or an aided case which may involve the Housing Authority in legal action | P.G. 217-11 |
| **Chemical Test Warrant** | A Court Order directing medical personnel to draw blood from a DWI suspect in a collision causing serious physical injury or death to another individual, to determine blood alcohol/drug content | P.G. 217-16 |
| **D.A.R.P.** | Directed Accident Response Program — the system for dispatching designated tow trucks through Communications Section to collision scenes on strict rotation basis | P.G. 217-09 |
| **Reference Marker** | Green, 9"×8" marker located every 1/10 mile on limited access highways, CDRs, and entrance/exit ramps | P.G. 217-14 |

## Critical Numbers and Timeframes

| Number | Context | Source |
|--------|---------|--------|
| **24 hours** | Deadline to fax Early Notification of Fatal Accident (MV-104EN) to NYS DMV | P.G. 217-03 |
| **90 days** | Deadline for filing claims against the City with the Comptroller | P.G. 217-04 |
| **10 hours** | Pilot must report aircraft accident to desk officer (failure = misdemeanor) | P.G. 217-08 |
| **15,000 lbs** | GVW threshold — vehicles at or above this weight may use their own tow company | P.G. 217-09 |
| **30 minutes** | Wait time for D.A.R.P. tow before cancelling and requesting next in rotation | P.G. 217-09 |
| **1/10 mile** | Spacing of reference markers on limited access highways | P.G. 217-14 |
| **2 hours** | If unconscious DWI suspect under arrest for this long without chemical test, warrant must be obtained | P.G. 217-16 |
| **1 hour** | Escalation trigger if ADA has not contacted arresting officer for chemical test warrant | P.G. 217-16 |
| **30 minutes** | Follow-up interval for contacting ADA supervisor after initial 1-hour escalation | P.G. 217-16 |
| **15 days** | Hearing must occur within this period after arraignment for chemical test refusal | P.G. 217-16 |
| **0700–2300** | Legal Bureau availability, Monday–Friday | P.G. 217-16 |
| **VTL Section 602** | Authorizes summons for leaving scene (property damage only) without observing the offense | P.G. 217-05 |
| **Admin Code 20-518(b)(2)** | Violation for unsolicited tow truck response to collision scene | P.G. 217-09 |
| **Admin Code 20-515(b)** | Violation for soliciting towing at collision scene | P.G. 217-09 |

## D.A.R.P. Disposition Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **10-99T4** | Vehicle Collision — No tow required |
| **10-99T5** | Vehicle Collision — D.A.R.P. tow service |
| **10-99T6** | Vehicle Collision — Other tow service |
| **10-99T7** | Vehicle Collision — D.A.R.P. and other tow service |
| **10-99T8** | Vehicle Collision — Highway-authorized tow service |
| **10-99T9** | Vehicle Collision — D.A.R.P. requested, waited 30 min, second request, resumed patrol |
| **10-90Z** | Gone on Arrival — vehicle not at scene, no information on removal |

## City-Involved Agency Routing

| Condition | Agency |
|-----------|--------|
| Traffic signs, stanchions, signal lights, lampposts | Department of Transportation |
| Parkway, highway, wall, curb, fence, guide rail | DOT Bureau of Highways, Legal Dept |
| Parks Department property; trees on public property | Department of Parks |
| Fire alarm box/post | FDNY Actions and Claims Unit |
| City hydrants | DEP **and** FDNY Actions and Claims |
| School bus collision | DOE Office of Pupil Transportation |
| Transit facility debris (elevated MTA line) | NYC Transit Law Department |
| Ferry, bridge, tunnel, dock, airport, seaplane base | Agency concerned |

## Department Property Forwarding

| Property | Forward To |
|----------|-----------|
| Booths, buildings | Deputy Commissioner — Management & Budget, Facilities Management Division |
| Horses | Mounted Unit |
| Aircraft, launches | Office of the Chief of Special Operations |

## Key Forms

| MOTOR VEHICLE COLLISION AND MECHANISM REPORT | PD301-151 | P.G. 217-03 |
| WITNESS STATEMENT — VEHICLE COLLISION | PD301-061 | P.G. 217-03 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE | PD521-141 | P.G. 217-03 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET | PD521-141A | P.G. 217-03 |
| Police Accident Report | MV104AN | Multiple |
| Early Notification of Fatal Accident | MV-104EN | P.G. 217-03 |
| Police Report for Fatal Motor Vehicle Accidents | MV-104D | P.G. 217-03 |
| ACCIDENT REPORT — CITY INVOLVED | PD301-155 | P.G. 217-04 |
| COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET | PD313-152A | P.G. 217-05 |
| COLLISION INFORMATION EXCHANGE | PD301-157 | P.G. 217-05 |
| UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT | PD370-152 | P.G. 217-08 |

# Section 217 — Review Questions

## Multiple-Choice Practice Questions

---

### Question 1

According to P.G. 217-03, who determines whether an injury qualifies as a "critical injury" at a vehicle collision scene?

- A) The patrol supervisor
- B) The desk officer after reviewing hospital records
- C) Emergency Medical Services (EMS) personnel based on on-scene assessment
- D) The Highway Unit collision investigator

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C)** Per P.G. 217-03, a critical injury is determined based upon the **on-scene assessment of EMS personnel**.

</details>

---

### Question 2

Under P.G. 217-03, within what timeframe must the Early Notification of Fatal Accident (MV-104EN) be faxed to the NYS Department of Motor Vehicles?

- A) Twelve hours
- B) Twenty-four hours
- C) Forty-eight hours
- D) Before end of tour

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** The Early Notification of Fatal Accident must be faxed to NYS DMV within **twenty-four (24) hours**.

</details>

---

### Question 3

According to P.G. 217-04, persons involved in a city-involved accident must file claims with which office, and within what timeframe?

- A) Corporation Counsel within 60 days
- B) Comptroller of the City of New York within 90 days
- C) City Law Department within 30 days
- D) Commissioner of Finance within 120 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** Claims against the City must be filed with the **Comptroller of the City of New York** within **ninety (90) days**.

</details>

---

### Question 4

Under P.G. 217-04, damage to a city hydrant requires notification to which agency or agencies?

- A) Department of Environmental Protection only
- B) FDNY Actions and Claims Unit only
- C) Department of Environmental Protection AND FDNY Actions and Claims Unit
- D) Department of Transportation and FDNY

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C)** Hydrant damage requires dual notification: **DEP and FDNY Actions and Claims Unit**.

</details>

---

### Question 5

According to P.G. 217-05, under what authority may a uniformed member issue a summons for leaving the scene of a property-damage-only collision without having observed the offense?

- A) Penal Law Section 240
- B) Vehicle and Traffic Law, Section 602
- C) Administrative Code Section 20-518
- D) The member may not issue a summons without observing the offense

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** VTL **Section 602** authorizes issuing a summons for leaving the scene of a property-damage-only collision even without witnessing the offense.

</details>

---

### Question 6

Per P.G. 217-08, within how many hours must a pilot report an aircraft accident to the desk officer, and what is the consequence of failure?

- A) 6 hours; violation
- B) 10 hours; misdemeanor
- C) 12 hours; misdemeanor
- D) 24 hours; violation

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** The pilot must report within **ten (10) hours**; failure to do so is a **misdemeanor**.

</details>

---

### Question 7

Under P.G. 217-09, who is authorized to make tow company notifications under the Directed Accident Response Program?

- A) The desk officer at the precinct of occurrence
- B) The patrol supervisor at the scene
- C) The D.A.R.P. operator at the Communications Section
- D) Any uniformed member at the scene

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C)** The D.A.R.P. operator at the **Communications Section** maintains the list and makes notifications on strict rotation. **Precinct commands are NOT authorized** to make notifications.

</details>

---

### Question 8

According to P.G. 217-09, what information about the disabled vehicle must NOT be transmitted over the radio when requesting a D.A.R.P. tow?

- A) Color and body type
- B) Plate number
- C) Make, model, and year
- D) Location of collision

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C)** Members must **NOT transmit the make, model, or year** of the disabled vehicle. Only color, body type, and plate number are transmitted.

</details>

---

### Question 9

Under P.G. 217-09, if a D.A.R.P. tow company does not arrive within the required time, what should the uniformed member do?

- A) Wait an additional 30 minutes and then leave
- B) Request Communications to cancel and assign the next company in rotation; prepare a REPORT OF VIOLATION
- C) Contact the tow company directly and demand response
- D) Have the desk officer arrange an alternative tow

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** After **30 minutes**, the member requests Communications to **cancel** the initial company, assign the **next in rotation**, and prepares a **REPORT OF VIOLATION (PD672-151)** for the non-responding company.

</details>

---

### Question 10

According to P.G. 217-09, what is the D.A.R.P. disposition code for a vehicle collision where a D.A.R.P. tow was requested, the member waited 30 minutes, made a second request, and resumed patrol?

- A) 10-99T5
- B) 10-99T7
- C) 10-99T8
- D) 10-99T9

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**D)** Code **10-99T9** indicates: D.A.R.P. requested, waited thirty minutes, made second request, resumed patrol.

</details>

---

### Question 11

Per P.G. 217-09, which of the following vehicles is EXEMPT from D.A.R.P. towing requirements?

- A) Any vehicle with more than $5,000 in damage
- B) Government vehicles
- C) Vehicles registered out of state
- D) Vehicles less than one year old

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** **Government vehicles** are exempt under Administrative Code Section 20-524(c). Their operators may call their own tow service.

</details>

---

### Question 12

Under P.G. 217-10, if Department property (other than vehicles) is damaged, who investigates and prepares the report?

- A) The uniformed member who discovered the damage
- B) The detective squad
- C) The patrol supervisor
- D) The desk officer

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C)** The desk officer directs the **patrol supervisor** to investigate the facts and prepare the report.

</details>

---

### Question 13

According to P.G. 217-14, reference markers on limited access highways are what color and size?

- A) Blue, 12"×10"
- B) Green, 9"×8"
- C) Yellow, 8"×6"
- D) White, 10"×8"

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** Reference markers are **green**, measuring **9 inches × 8 inches**, placed every 1/10 of a mile.

</details>

---

### Question 14

Per P.G. 217-16, under what circumstances can a chemical test warrant NOT be applied for?

- A) When the collision occurred more than 24 hours ago
- B) When the operator is the only person who suffered serious physical injury
- C) When the operator's BAC is below 0.08
- D) When the operator is a juvenile

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** If the operator is the **only person** with serious physical injury and **no other person** suffers serious injury or death, a chemical test warrant **cannot** be applied for.

</details>

---

### Question 15

Under P.G. 217-16, if one hour has passed and the assistant district attorney has not contacted the arresting officer, what is the desk officer's next step?

- A) Cancel the chemical test warrant request
- B) Contact the ADA supervisor and Operations Unit; then contact ADA supervisor every 30 minutes
- C) Have the arresting officer proceed without ADA consultation
- D) Request a Department attorney from Legal Bureau

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B)** After one hour, the desk officer contacts the **ADA supervisor and Operations Unit**, then contacts the ADA supervisor **every thirty (30) minutes** thereafter until the ADA is reached.

</details>

---

# Section 218 — Property: General

## Overview

Section 218 governs all aspects of invoicing, processing, safeguarding, and disposing of property coming into police custody. This is one of the largest sections in the Patrol Guide, covering 44 procedures that address everything from general invoicing to specialized evidence handling (firearms, DNA, computers, fireworks), stolen vehicle processing, currency handling, vendor/peddler enforcement, and handgun license surrenders.

## Source

NYPD Patrol Guide, Section 218 (Property — General)
Source PDF: `public-pguide4-ocr.pdf`

## Learning Objectives

1. Understand general invoicing procedures and property categories (P.G. 218-01)
2. Know the process for returning property at command and voiding invoices (P.G. 218-02)
3. Understand vehicle safeguarding, inventory search rules, and the 48-hour limit (P.G. 218-12, 218-13)
4. Know stolen vehicle processing for NYC-recovered, out-of-city, and Port Authority scenarios (P.G. 218-14, 218-15, 218-16)
5. Master Rotation Tow Program procedures and the 30-day tow operator timeline (P.G. 218-21)
6. Understand firearms evidence marking, packaging, and chain of custody (P.G. 218-23)
7. Know found property, decedent's property, and currency processing rules (P.G. 218-26, 218-28, 218-35, 218-36)
8. Understand vendor/peddler property seizure and forfeiture procedures (P.G. 218-39, 218-40, 218-41)
9. Know handgun license surrender investigation requirements (P.G. 218-45)
10. Understand vehicle seizure at arrest and the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM requirement (P.G. 218-48)
11. Know DNA evidence immediate delivery chain: Command → Police Lab → OCME (P.G. 218-49)
12. Understand property retention timelines — 1 year for property, 15 days for vehicles (P.G. 218-55)

## Chapter Contents

| File | Procedures Covered | Topics |
|------|-------------------|--------|
| [section-218-invoicing-general.md](section-218-invoicing-general.md) | 218-01, 02, 03, 05, 06 | General invoicing, return of property, delivery to Property Clerk, recording numbers, temporary removal |
| [section-218-vehicles-custody.md](section-218-vehicles-custody.md) | 218-12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 48 | Safeguarding vehicles, inventory searches, stolen vehicles, photographing evidence vehicles, invoicing/delivery, Rotation Tow, boats, vehicle seizure at arrest |
| [section-218-evidence-processing.md](section-218-evidence-processing.md) | 218-11, 23, 31, 33, 49, 51, 53, 54 | Biohazard labels, firearms evidence, computer evidence, sexual offense kits, DNA delivery, suspect kits, fireworks, lab examination reports |
| [section-218-found-decedent-currency.md](section-218-found-decedent-currency.md) | 218-26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 34, 35, 36, 37, 38 | Found property, interstate companies, deceased person's property, parolee property, property taken from person, lottery property, evidence/non-evidence currency, red dye currency, forfeiture currency |
| [section-218-vendors-peddlers.md](section-218-vendors-peddlers.md) | 218-39, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44 | Vendor seizure/removal, general vendor forfeiture, unlicensed peddler forfeiture, Property Clerk seals, food vendor fees, removal/storage charges |
| [section-218-licenses-admin.md](section-218-licenses-admin.md) | 218-45, 55, 56, 57 | Handgun license suspension/revocation, retention of invoiced property, change of category, FORMS application failure |
| [key-terms.md](key-terms.md) | All | Definitions, timeframes, fees, forms |
| [review-questions.md](review-questions.md) | All | 20 multiple-choice practice questions |

## Study Tips

- **Property Categories** — Memorize all categories: Arrest Evidence, DNA Arrest Evidence, Investigatory, DNA Investigatory, Forfeiture, Decedent's Property, Found Property, Safekeeping, Peddler Property, Amnesty, Determine True Owner, Parking Enforcement, Photo Release, Rotation Tow, Other
- **48-hour rule** appears twice: vehicles at command (218-12/218-20) and VIN check delivery to Auto Pound (218-21)
- **Retention deadlines**: 1 year for property, 15 days for vehicles — failure to submit retention request = disposal
- **Currency handling** differs based on type: evidence vs. non-evidence vs. numismatic/sentimental vs. forfeiture vs. red dye
- **Inventory search consent**: If owner doesn't consent to opening locked items, a search warrant is needed (218-13)
- **FORMS Property** is the central system — know the FORMS failure procedures (218-57)

---

# Section 218 — Evidence Processing: Specialized Items

## P.G. 218-11: Use of Biohazard Labels

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To identify regulated waste coming into police custody.

### Definition

**REGULATED WASTE** — Liquid or semi-liquid blood or other potentially infectious materials; contaminated items that would release blood/infectious materials if compressed; items caked with dried blood capable of releasing during handling; contaminated sharps; pathological and microbiological wastes containing blood or other potentially infectious materials.

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Follow normal invoicing procedures |
| 2 | Member | Request Biohazard Label(s) from desk officer |
| 3 | Member | Affix label as close as feasible using string, wire, adhesive or other method preventing loss/removal |
| 4 | Member | Use one label for each item or container in same case |
| 5 | Desk Officer | Request additional Biohazard Labels from Quartermaster Section when needed |

> **Exam Alert:** Biohazard Labels are **fluorescent orange** with the word "Biohazard" on the label's face.

---

## P.G. 218-23: Processing Firearms and Firearm-Related Evidence

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 11

### Purpose

To ensure all firearms and firearm-related evidence is properly marked, packaged, sealed, and invoiced to maintain a continuous chain of custody.

### Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Firearm** | Rifle, shotgun, pistol, revolver, derringer, machine gun; starter's pistol, zip gun, air gun, CO2 gun; homemade/modified gun; simulated/toy/imitation firearm; non-metal-detectable firearm; ghost gun/untraceable; 3D-printed firearm; weapon with housing designed to expel a projectile by explosive action (including stabilizing/arm brace modifications) |
| **Cartridge** | Live ammunition — bullet (projectile) + shell casing as one unit |
| **Fired Bullet** | Part of cartridge fired through barrel (lead, metal-jacketed, coated lead) |
| **Shell Casing** | Metal casing remaining in/ejecting from firearm after bullet leaves barrel |

### Cartridge Categories (Critical for Packaging)

| Category | Description | Packaging Rule |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| a | Cartridge removed from **chamber** | Package and seal in ONE container; write "Cartridge Removed From Chamber" |
| b | Remaining cartridges removed from firearm (magazine/cylinder) | ALL in ONE container; write "Cartridge(s) Removed From Firearm" |
| c | Cartridges NOT from firearm, seized from person/location, NO crime scene | ONE container per person; ONE container per location; identify person/location |
| d | Cartridges NOT from firearm, recovered from **crime scene** | EACH cartridge in SEPARATE container |

### Marking Rules

| Item | Where to Mark | Do NOT Mark |
|------|--------------|-------------|
| Cartridges | Bullet portion | Metal shell casing portion |
| Fired Bullets | Bottom/base | Sides (near rifling marks) |
| Shell Casings | Inside | Exterior or bottom |
| Firearms | Initials + unique consecutive number | — |
| Magazines | Initials + unique consecutive number | — |
| Silencers | Initials + unique consecutive number | — |

> **Exam Alert:** Use a **scribe or sharp writing instrument** to scratch initials and a unique consecutive number on each item. Each number must be used only once per case.

### Crime Scene Evidence — Individual Packaging Rules

| Evidence Type | Packaging |
|--------------|-----------|
| Cartridges from crime scene | EACH in SEPARATE container — NEVER two or more together |
| Fired bullets from crime scene | EACH in SEPARATE container — NEVER two or more together |
| Shell casings from crime scene | EACH in SEPARATE container — NEVER two or more together |
| Cartridges from person (no crime scene) | ALL from same person in ONE container |
| Cartridges from location (no crime scene) | ALL from same location in ONE container |

### Sealing Containers

| Method | Procedure |
|--------|-----------|
| Standard container | Fasten with EVIDENCE TAPE; sign name across border between tape and container |
| NYPD Plastic Security Envelope | Remove paper strip exposing pre-applied adhesive; fold top aligning blue circles; sign on pre-printed line |

### WORKSHEET Listing Order

1. Firearm (item #1) — describe by make, type, serial number
2. Magazine (if any)
3. Cartridge from chamber
4. Remaining cartridges from firearm (same caliber = one item)
5. Silencer(s) — each listed separately
6. Firearm accessories affixed to firearm or needed for operation
7. Additional firearms and related items (repeat sequence)
8. Cartridges not from firearm (per person/per location)
9. Shell casings from crime scene (same caliber = one item)
10. Fired bullets from crime scene (all = one item)

### Desk Officer Responsibilities

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 21 | Verify firearm is unloaded (if unfamiliar, notify ESU to respond) |
| 21b | Verify proper marking, packaging, and sealing |
| 21c | Verify INVOICES and forms properly prepared |
| 22 | Enter notation in "Remarks" that firearm(s) have been unloaded/rendered safe |
| 23 | Review COMPLAINT REPORT — verify address, perpetrator info, evidence section, weapon section |
| 26 | Forward firearms requiring lab analysis to Police Laboratory |

> **Exam Alert:** Firearms requiring lab analysis must be delivered to the **patrol borough office by 0100 hours daily**. Delay requires **exigent circumstances** AND permission from **precinct CO/duty captain**.

> **Exam Alert:** Firearm alarm cancellation is made ONLY by the **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit** — recovering command does NOT cancel.

### Firearms Invoiced for Safekeeping

Delivered DIRECT to Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required):
- Property of retired, resigned, dismissed, suspended, sick, or hospitalized members
- Lawfully possessed property of a deceased person
- Obtained from licensed dealer when license revoked/suspended/expired
- Lawfully possessed and removed by court order/order of protection
- Obtained from permit holder during renewal/expiration/cancellation

### DNA Evidence on Firearms

- DO NOT package DNA evidence if **wet** — must be **air-dried** first
- DO NOT use plastic bags/envelopes for DNA evidence — use **paper bags/containers**
- Affix orange Biohazard label to each paper bag/container
- If marking would contaminate DNA evidence, mark the container instead (print name in BLOCK letters)

> **Exam Alert:** Firearm accessories NOT affixed to the firearm AND not needed for operation (holster, gun case, sling) are invoiced on a **separate INVOICE** from the firearm.

---

## P.G. 218-31: Processing Computer Evidence

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To process and safeguard computer-related evidence coming into Department custody.

### Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **CPU** | Box-shaped unit housing internal hard drive, memory, and central processor |
| **Monitor** | Television-type display instrument |
| **Keyboard** | Input device resembling a typewriter |
| **Mouse** | Pointing device connected to CPU by cable |
| **Printer** | Output device (dot matrix, ink-jet, laser) |
| **Peripherals** | Scanners, tape drives, modems, speakers, personal digital assistants, pocket organizers, laptops |

### Notifications

| Time | Contact |
|------|---------|
| Mon–Fri, 0600–2000 | Computer Crimes Squad |
| All other times | Office of the Chief of Detectives |

### Marking and Packaging

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 3a | Affix identifiable mark to hardware (monitor, CPU, printer, etc.) |
| 3b | Label items to indicate which peripherals were connected (Monitor 1, CPU 1, Keyboard 1) and tag all wires |
| 3c | Package similar floppy disks in **paper envelopes** — do NOT remove disks from drives; secure with masking tape |

> **Exam Alert:** DO NOT use Plastic Security Envelopes for floppy disks — **static electricity** can destroy data. DO NOT write on floppy disks with **ballpoint pens** — use labels or felt tip markers.

### Critical Prohibitions

- Members will **NOT search** hard drives, floppy disks, CD-ROMs, or data storage devices — Computer Crimes Squad ONLY
- Searching may **inadvertently alter or destroy** evidence
- Do not touch keyboard or execute programs at the scene
- If modem attached, **unplug telephone cord** from wall jack immediately
- To power off: **unplug from wall outlet** — do NOT use on/off switch; photograph monitor screen first
- Do NOT transport in vehicle trunks with radio transceivers
- Do NOT expose to devices emitting magnetic fields (portable radios, car speakers)

---

## P.G. 218-33: Processing Sexual Offense Collection Kits

**Date Effective:** 06/19/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 42 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To ensure sexual offense evidence collection kits and other evidence are properly forwarded to the OCME Evidence Unit or Property Clerk.

### Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Sexual Offense** | Offenses enumerated in Article 130, NYS Penal Law |
| **Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit** | Kit used by trained medical personnel in SAFE Program hospitals; contains slides, swabs, test tubes, envelopes |
| **Drug Facilitated Sexual Assault Kit** | Separate kit with two gray-topped blood tubes and urine container; used when drugs/alcohol suspected |
| **Sexual Offense Evidence** | Items containing bloodstains, serology, body fluids, biological evidence (clothing, bedding, undergarments) for DNA profiling |

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Ensure victim transferred to SAFE Program hospital |
| 2 | Member | Take possession of kits and evidence upon completion of medical exam (with victim consent) |
| 3 | Member | Ensure physician's and complainant's names entered on kit |
| 4 | Member | Remove evidence to precinct of occurrence; prepare WORKSHEET |
| 5–8 | Desk Officer | Direct preparation of INVOICE, REQUEST FOR LAB EXAM, COMPLAINT REPORT |
| 9 | Desk Officer | Direct delivery of kit and COMPLAINT REPORT copy to Police Laboratory |
| 10 | Desk Officer | Direct delivery of OTHER sexual offense evidence to Property Clerk (if kit was used at hospital) |
| 11 | Desk Officer | If NO kit used at hospital, deliver other evidence to Police Laboratory |
| 12 | Desk Officer | Comply with P.G. 218-49 for "high priority" cases |
| 14 | Desk Officer | Immediately notify NYPD OCME Liaison Unit of all unfounded cases |

> **Exam Alert:** THE SEAL on a Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit **SHALL NOT BE BROKEN** by any member of the Department EXCEPT authorized personnel.

> **Exam Alert:** Separate INVOICES required: one for the Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit, one for the Drug Facilitated Sexual Assault Kit, and one for other sexual offense evidence.

> **Exam Alert:** A member of the service (male or female) is NOT required to be present during the medical examination. **Chain of custody** during examination is the sole responsibility of attending medical personnel.

> **Exam Alert:** Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit should be used when the offense occurred within **120 hours** or at the discretion of medical personnel.

---

## P.G. 218-49: Immediate Delivery of DNA Evidence to Police Laboratory and OCME

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose
