## Sergeant Focus Areas
- Vehicle inspection accountability (daily tour checks + weekly Sunday inspections)
- Notification chain for lost/stolen property: Desk Officer → CO/Ops Coordinator → IAB + Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
- Disciplinary consultation with Department Advocate's Office for lost/stolen firearms
- ICO duties: monthly DAS audits (5 members), E-Z Pass monitoring, OMNY pass inspections, parking permit background checks
- LPR alarm response: ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN before law enforcement action
- Emergency power: disconnect non-essential equipment from red-plate receptacles; quarterly room surveys
- Restricted Parking Permit: CO has final authority; 5+ red light or 15+ school speed violations = ineligible

---

# Section 219 — Equipment, Supplies, and Assigned Property

## P.G. 219-17: Patrol Kits (Personal Protective Equipment)

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure patrol kits containing personal protective equipment (PPE) are maintained and available.

### Patrol Kit Contents

| Item |
|------|
| Disposable gowns |
| Eye/mouth shields |
| Heavy duty gloves |
| Shoe covers |
| Antiseptic wipes |
| Biohazard bag |

### Biohazard Waste

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Receptacle | **32-gallon** biohazard receptacle at command |
| Pickup | **Medical Division messenger** picks up regulated infectious waste |

> **Exam Alert:** Regulated infectious waste is picked up by the **Medical Division messenger**, not sanitation or other units.

---

## P.G. 219-18: Inspection of Supplies

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure that supplies delivered to the command are properly inspected.

### Key Points

- Inspect deliveries for proper **quantity and quality**
- **Accept or refuse** based on inspection findings
- Report discrepancies to the supplying unit

---

## P.G. 219-19: Department Locker Inspections

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure the proper inspection of Department-assigned lockers.

### Key Points

- Member **witnesses** their own locker inspection when present
- If member is **not present**, another member witnesses the inspection
- Findings documented as appropriate

> **Exam Alert:** A locker inspection must be **witnessed** — either by the assigned member or, if absent, by **another member**.

---

## P.G. 219-25: E-Z Pass Tags

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To account for the safekeeping and usage of E-Z Pass tags assigned to each command.

### Responsibilities

| Role | Duty |
|------|------|
| CO | Ensure lieutenant/patrol supervisor accounts for all tags |
| CO | Have ICO monitor E-Z Pass usage |
| Lt. Platoon Cmdr/Patrol Supervisor | Inspect all vehicles each Sunday after second platoon; verify tags affixed to center top windshield; record serial numbers on VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT |
| ICO | Maintain inventory; review usage for compliance; incorporate into monthly self-inspection |

### Mounting Rules

| Vehicle Type | Mounting Method |
|-------------|----------------|
| Standard | **Permanently affixed** with double-sided tape (top center windshield) |
| Vehicles routinely leaving city (e.g., Category I) | Secured with **velcro strips** (removable) |

### Transfer Restrictions

- Commands are **NOT authorized** to transfer E-Z Pass tags between vehicles
- All reassignments approved by CO, Fleet Services Division

### Authorized Toll Crossings (Partial List)

Bronx-Whitestone Bridge, George Washington Bridge, Bayonne Bridge, Goethals Bridge, Henry Hudson Bridge, Holland Tunnel, Queens Midtown Tunnel, Lincoln Tunnel, Throgs Neck Bridge, Outerbridge Crossing, Cross Bay Veterans Memorial Bridge, Verrazano-Narrows Bridge, Hugh L. Carey Tunnel, Robert F. Kennedy Bridge, Marine Parkway-Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge

### NOT Authorized Toll Crossings (Partial List)

New Jersey Turnpike, New York State Thruway, Garden State Parkway, New England Thruway, Governor Mario M. Cuomo (Tappan Zee) Bridge

### Lost/Stolen E-Z Pass

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Notify desk officer |
| 2 | Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT |
| 3 | Notify IAB; enter log number on report |
| 4 | Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit of serial number |
| 5 | CO investigates; forwards report to CO, Fleet Services Division |

> **Exam Alert:** E-Z Pass tags are **NOT authorized** for the NJ Turnpike, NY State Thruway, Garden State Parkway, New England Thruway, or Tappan Zee Bridge.

> **Exam Alert:** Commands may **NOT** transfer E-Z Pass tags between vehicles — Fleet Services Division must approve.

---

## P.G. 219-26: NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes

**Date Effective:** 01/06/26 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 3 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To provide for the issuance, control, and strict accountability of NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes to all uniformed members.

### Key Facts

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Issued by | Human Resources Division |
| Assigned to | Individual uniformed member (NOT the command) |
| Unique serial number | Each pass has its own number |
| Validity period | Generally **7 years** |
| Renewal | Coordinated by Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit |
| Carry requirement | At all times when shield and ID card required |
| Not valid on | **MTA express buses** |
| Usage monitoring | Each use **electronically recorded** by NYC Transit |

### CO Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Maintain account of all passes |
| Ensure all uniformed members have a valid pass |
| Enter serial number on member's FORCE RECORD |
| Have ICO monitor the program |
| Investigate damaged/defective passes |
| Investigate lost/stolen passes per P.G. 219-20 |

### ICO Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Enter serial number on FORCE RECORD |
| Inspect passes at roll calls and semi-annual uniform inspections |
| Investigate damaged/defective passes; prepare Typed Letterhead report |
| Hand deliver damaged/defective pass to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit |

### Long-Term Leave

- Member must **surrender** OMNY pass immediately prior to long-term leave (military, child care)
- Pass hand-delivered to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit
- Upon return, report prepared requesting re-issuance

### Lost/Stolen Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Notify desk officer |
| 2 | Desk Officer | Notify CO and operations coordinator |
| 3 | Operations Coordinator | Comply with P.G. 219-20; prepare COMPLAINT REPORT |
| 4 | Operations Coordinator | Notify IAB Command Center; enter log number |
| 5 | Operations Coordinator | Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit |
| 6 | Operations Coordinator | Notify **Transit Bureau Wheel** and Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit |

### Separation from Service

- All passes surrendered upon separation (retirement, resignation, dismissal)
- Notation on PROPERTY RECEIPT — DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)
- Pass hand-delivered to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit

> **Exam Alert:** OMNY passes are NOT valid on **MTA express buses**. All other subway/bus use is permitted.

> **Exam Alert:** Passes are assigned to the **individual member**, NOT the command. Commands may NOT assign or reassign passes.

> **Exam Alert:** Unauthorized person using an OMNY pass can be charged with **theft of service** and **impersonation of a Police Officer**.

---

## P.G. 219-27: Radiation Detection Pagers — Command-Assigned

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.110 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide for control, accountability, and issuance of radiation detection pagers assigned to commands.

### Key Points

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Assigned by | Counterterrorism Central Resource Division through Citywide Counterterrorism Coordinator |
| Training | **ONLY trained** supervisors/members may use pagers |
| Battery type | **AAA** batteries (obtained from Quartermaster Section) |
| Deployment | Every tour — pager(s) assigned to trained patrol supervisor/member |
| Transfer | Commands NOT authorized to transfer pagers to other commands |
| Inspection | Desk officer inspects/accounts for all pagers at start of each tour |
| Command Log | Serial numbers and operating status recorded |
| If alarm activates | Comply with P.G. 212-102 |
| Lost/stolen | Process per P.G. 219-20; notify Citywide Counterterrorism Office immediately |

### Repair/Replacement

- Desk officer investigates and prepares Typed Letterhead report
- Contact **Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator** for return/replacement instructions

---

## P.G. 219-29: NYPD Restricted Parking Permits

**Date Effective:** 05/25/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.64 | **Pages:** 7

### Definition

**NYPD Restricted Parking Permit (Misc. 23-N)** — Cardboard, approximately 4" × 10", with serial number, command code, vehicle license plate number, and expiration date.

### Eligibility Requirements

| Requirement |
|-------------|
| Valid NYS driver's license |
| Valid NYS vehicle registration |
| Valid insurance policy |
| Vehicle registered to member, blood/marriage relative, or registered NYS domestic partner |
| Owner's residence matches member's Department address of record |

### Ineligible Members/Vehicles

| Ineligible |
|------------|
| Member assigned Category I vehicle |
| Members at One Police Plaza with garage access (except Category II) |
| Members on Military & Extended Leave Desk or vacation/terminal leave pending retirement |
| Members under suspension |
| Auxiliary Police members |
| Vehicle with **5+ red light camera violations** or **15+ school speed camera violations** within 12 months |

### Application Schedule (By Borough)

| Borough | Member Submission Deadline | ICO Delivery Deadline |
|---------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| Manhattan | Last business day, 1st week of September | 2nd week of September |
| Bronx | Last business day, 2nd week of September | 3rd week of September |
| Staten Island/Brooklyn | Last business day, 3rd week of September | 4th week of September |
| Queens | Last business day, 4th week of September | 1st week of October |

### ICO Background Checks

| System | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| ZFINEST RALL | NYS Vehicle Registration by Plate Number |
| ZFINEST DCID | NYS Driver Inquiry (suspensions, revocations, scofflaw) |
| CPR Employee Inquiry Report | Department personnel records |
| DAS Summons History | Search by license plate |
| NYC Dept of Finance website | Outstanding parking/photo enforcement fines |

### Key Restrictions

- Permit may **NOT be photocopied or reproduced** — subject to disciplinary action
- Permit may NOT be **laminated**
- Valid ONLY for the vehicle whose license plate appears on the face
- **Non-transferable** — exclusive use of assigned member
- If vehicle changes, notify CO within **30 days**; may use substitute vehicle for **max 30 days**

### Parking Still Prohibited (With or Without Permit)

Double parking, fire hydrant/zone, bus stop/lane, sidewalk, crosswalks/driveways, obstructing traffic, no standing zones, snow emergency, no parking/taxi stand, bicycle lane, no permit zone

> **Exam Alert:** Vehicle with **5+ red light camera** or **15+ school speed camera** violations in 12 months = **ineligible**.

> **Exam Alert:** Restricted Parking Permits may NOT be **photocopied, reproduced, or laminated**.

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO has **final authority** to approve or deny all Restricted Parking Permit applications.

---

## P.G. 219-30: Radiation Detection Pagers — Personally Assigned

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.110 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide for control, accountability, and issuance of radiation detection pagers permanently assigned to individual uniformed members.

### Key Differences from Command-Assigned (219-27)

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Assigned by | Counterterrorism Central Resource Division after training completion |
| Assignment | **Permanently** to individual member (not the command) |
| Documentation | PROPERTY TRANSFER RECEIPT (PD521-145) — copy to CO and ICO |
| Serial number | Listed on member's FORCE RECORD |
| Transfer | Commands **NOT authorized** to transfer between members |
| Carry requirement | At **all times** when performing duty |
| Separation | Surrender to desk officer for return to Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator |

> **Exam Alert:** Personally assigned radiation pagers must be carried at **all times** while performing duty and are listed on the member's **FORCE RECORD**.

---

# Section 219 — Fuel, Heating, and Facility Operations

## P.G. 219-11: Computerized Fuel Dispensing

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide for the proper use of the computerized fuel dispensing system.

### Vehicle Identification Box (VIB)

The VIB is the in-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing. Fuel is dispensed using card-based authorization.

### Card Types

| Card | Color | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| Master | **Green** | Administrative control |
| CRALO | **Yellow** | Command Radio Auto Liaison Officer |
| Non-Fleet | **Orange** | Non-fleet vehicles |
| Private | **Red** | Private vehicles authorized for official use |
| Operator | **White** | Individual operator cards |

### Electric Vehicles

- **ChargePoint cards** issued for electric vehicle charging
- **Strictly prohibited** to charge personal vehicles using Department ChargePoint cards or stations

> **Exam Alert:** It is **strictly prohibited** to charge personal vehicles at Department charging stations.

> **Exam Alert:** Know the fuel card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator.

---

## P.G. 219-12: Fuel Delivery Supervision

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure proper supervision of fuel deliveries to Department facilities.

### Key Points

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Tank fill level | **90% ullage amount** (maximum fill capacity) |
| Monitoring | Electronic tank monitor system |
| Supervision | Delivery must be supervised by designated member |

> **Exam Alert:** Fuel tanks are filled to **90% ullage** — never completely full.

---

## P.G. 219-13: Heating Oil Delivery

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure proper procedures for receiving heating oil deliveries.

### Key Points

- **DCAS inspector** must sign the vendor delivery ticket **before** accepting delivery
- Do not accept delivery without DCAS inspector signature

> **Exam Alert:** A **DCAS inspector** must sign the vendor delivery ticket before heating oil delivery is accepted.

---

## P.G. 219-28: Emergency Power Outages

**Date Effective:** 01/18/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.4 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To address emergency power outages and ensure non-essential equipment is removed from emergency electrical receptacles.

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Desk Officer/Supervisory Officer | **Immediately** notify Building Maintenance Section (24/7) |
| 1a | Same | Record notification in Telephone Record |
| 2 | Same (if generator activated) | Survey command — ensure only **essential emergency equipment and critical computer systems** operate on emergency power |
| 3 | Same | Have all **non-essential equipment disconnected** from emergency electrical receptacles |

### Non-Essential Equipment (Disconnect Immediately)

- Air conditioners
- Vending machines
- Refrigerators
- Microwaves

### Emergency Receptacle Identification

- **Red cover plate** over the receptacle, OR
- **Raised lettering** on the outlet receptacle

### Fuel Tank Responsibilities

| Configuration | Responsibility |
|--------------|----------------|
| **Single fuel tank** (heating + generator) | Command maintains fuel level **above 50%** |
| **Two fuel tanks** (separate) | Command maintains heating tank; Building Maintenance Section maintains generator tank |

### CO Quarterly Survey

- Commanding officers conduct **quarterly survey** to ensure generator rooms, mechanical rooms, and electrical rooms are used **only for their intended purpose** — not as locker rooms, storage rooms, etc.
- Items in these rooms may create a **fire hazard**

> **Exam Alert:** Commands with a **single fuel tank** must maintain the level **above 50%**.

> **Exam Alert:** Only qualified **Building Maintenance Section** personnel should start or repair the emergency generator. No outside agencies without BMS supervisor approval (absent exigent circumstances).

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO must conduct a **quarterly survey** of generator/mechanical/electrical rooms to ensure proper use only.

---

# Section 219 — Information Technology and Communications Equipment

## P.G. 219-14: Computer Systems — Security and Integrity

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To establish security and integrity procedures for Department computer systems.

### Integrity Control Officer (ICO) Responsibilities

| Duty | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Monthly audit | Review **Domain Awareness System (DAS)** usage for **5 random members** per month |
| Password management | Ensure compliance with password policies |
| Access control | Monitor authorized access |

### Key Rules

- Department reserves the right to **review all computer activity** at any time
- Members have **no expectation of privacy** on Department computer systems
- Unauthorized access or use subject to disciplinary action

> **Exam Alert:** The ICO must conduct a **monthly DAS audit of 5 random members**.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Department reserves the right to review **all** computer activity — members have no privacy expectation on Department systems.

---

## P.G. 219-15: Portable Radios

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To provide for the issuance, use, and accountability of portable radios.

### Key Points

- Maintain RADIO ASSIGNMENT LOG at each command
- Radio must be carried in an **authorized carrying case**
- Lost radio = **negligence finding** if not in authorized carrying case at time of loss

### Notifications for Lost/Stolen Radio

| Notify |
|--------|
| Operations Unit |
| Electronics Section |
| Internal Affairs Bureau (IAB) |
| Stolen Property Inquiry Unit |

> **Exam Alert:** If a portable radio is lost and was **not in an authorized carrying case**, the member is deemed **negligent**.

> **Exam Alert:** Four notifications required for lost/stolen radio: **Operations Unit, Electronics Section, IAB, and Stolen Property Inquiry Unit**.

---

## P.G. 219-23: Solar Cellular Callboxes

**Date Effective:** 02/04/15 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure all components of a solar cellular callbox are in place and functioning properly.

### Callbox Components

1. Antenna
2. Solar panel
3. Pole
4. Sign — "Emergency Phone"
5. Callbox
6. Handset and cable (cord)

### Procedure

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Respond as directed by radio dispatcher |
| 2 | Verify all 6 components are in place |
| 3 | Make test call to Communications Section (verify operability, callbox number, location) |
| 4 | Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT for vandalism/damaged/missing components, or MV104AN for vehicle collision damage |

### Notifications

| Time | Contact |
|------|---------|
| Mon–Fri, 0800–1600 | Telecommunications Unit |
| All other times | ITB Wheel |

---

## P.G. 219-24: Cellular Telephones Assigned to Patrol Supervisor

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure proper utilization of and accountability for cellular telephones assigned to patrol supervisors.

### Speed Dial Numbers

| Button | Connects To |
|--------|------------|
| 1 | Telephone switchboard |
| 2 | Desk |
| 3 | Patrol borough |
| 4 | Operations Unit |
| 5 | 911 |

### Key Points

- Phones receive **all incoming calls** but only allow the **5 outgoing calls listed above**
- Desk officer ensures phones are operational each tour (batteries charged, car adapters available)
- Command Log entry: name, telephone number, and assignment of supervisor with phone
- Phones not assigned accounted for each tour with Command Log entry

---

## P.G. 219-31: License Plate Reader Devices

**Date Effective:** 11/16/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.112 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To provide for control, use, maintenance, and accountability of License Plate Reader (LPR) devices.

### Definition

**LICENSE PLATE READER (LPR)** — A specialized camera and computer that captures license plate numbers, converts them to text, and compares against a "hot list" (stolen, wanted, suspended registration/insurance, etc.).

### Critical Operational Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| NYSPIN verification | **Absolutely essential** — verify through NYSPIN before any law enforcement action |
| Not connected to NYSPIN | LPR is NOT automatically connected to NYSPIN |
| State of registration | LPR does NOT scan state of registration |
| Partial matches | LPR will activate on partial scan match |
| Hot list currency | Do NOT use if hot list not updated within **24 hours** |

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Inspect LPR for operability at beginning of tour; report to desk officer |
| 2 | Member | Activate LPR; record in Activity Log last hot list update time |
| 4 | Desk Officer | Account for each LPR device at beginning of each tour; record in Command Log |
| 5 | Desk Officer | Enter vehicle LPR assignment on CPR Roll Call System |
| 6 | Desk Officer | Contact ITB Service Desk for LPR repairs |
| 7 | Member (on alarm) | **Verify through NYSPIN** before any law enforcement action |
| 8 | Member | Take law enforcement action (arrest, summons, rotation tow) |

### Restrictions

- LPR used **only** for stolen/wanted files and furtherance of criminal investigations
- Any other use is **strictly prohibited**
- Camera lens cleaned only with glass cleaner applied with soft cloth
- LPR equipment must **not be moved** from mounting location
- Vehicle does not go out of service for LPR repair

> **Exam Alert:** **ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN** before any law enforcement action based on an LPR alarm — the device activates on **partial matches** and is NOT connected to NYSPIN.

---

## P.G. 219-32: Department Mobile Digital Devices

**Date Effective:** 03/26/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 15 | **Pages:** 4

### Definition

**MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE** — Any smartphone, tablet, or portable computer issued by the Department.

### Key Rules for Members

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Official use only | Use Department devices for **official business exclusively** |
| Personal devices | Do NOT use personal devices for official business |
| On-duty personal phone | NOT permitted during tour (except authorized meal or emergency) |
| Carry requirement | Carry Department smartphone while on duty |
| NYPD Phone Sticker | Affix NYPD PHONE STICKER (PD589-090) to phone case |
| No app installation | Do NOT install any applications unless approved by Deputy Commissioner, IT |
| No content deletion | Do NOT delete any content (texts, photos, videos, notes, voicemail) |
| Foreign travel | Request ITB Information Security Desk permission **5 business days** prior |

### Lost/Stolen/Damaged

- Notify supervisor and ITB Telecommunications Unit **immediately**
- Process per P.G. 219-20, "Loss or Theft of Department Property"
- Complete DEPARTMENT MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE REPAIR/REPLACEMENT/RETURN REQUEST (PD589-163)

### Off-Duty Considerations

- Members may secure Department smartphone at command when off-duty
- Members may carry phone off-duty and remove phone case (to not identify as MOS)

### Privacy Notice

- Members have **no right to privacy** in any feature of Department mobile devices
- All communications, photos, video may be accessed by Department **at any time** without prior knowledge
- Content may be Rosario material and/or discoverable in criminal prosecutions, civil lawsuits, CCRB/IAB investigations

### Operational Considerations

- Members are **permitted but not required** to photograph/video record encounters with public
- Shall NOT record situations where BWC use would be prohibited
- Shall NOT document demonstrations or political activity (unless per P.G. 212-71, 212-72)
- Crime Scene Unit remains solely responsible for documenting crime scenes

> **Exam Alert:** Members must **not delete any content** from Department mobile devices. All content is potentially discoverable.

> **Exam Alert:** Permission from ITB Information Security Desk required **5 business days** before taking a Department phone to a foreign country.

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO/designated supervisor must return Department smartphone to ITB when member has change in duty status (retired, suspended, modified).

---

# Section 219 — Loss or Theft of Department Property

## P.G. 219-20: Loss or Theft of Department Property

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To properly report and investigate the loss or theft of Department property.

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Immediately report loss/theft to desk officer |
| 2 | Desk Officer | Notify commanding officer and operations coordinator |
| 3 | Operations Coordinator | Conduct investigation |
| 4 | Operations Coordinator | Notify Internal Affairs Bureau (IAB) |
| 5 | Operations Coordinator | Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit — transmit alarm |
| 6 | Operations Coordinator | Prepare required reports |

### NYCT OMNY Police Pass Procedures

When an OMNY Police Pass is lost or stolen, additional notifications are required:
- **Transit Bureau Wheel**
- **Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit**

> **Exam Alert:** Lost/stolen Department property always requires **IAB notification** and a **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit alarm**.

> **Sergeant Focus:** The **Operations Coordinator** conducts the investigation into loss or theft of Department property.

---

## P.G. 219-21: Lost or Stolen Firearm, Shield, or Identification Card

**Date Effective:** 02/16/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.13 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To establish reporting and investigative procedures when a member's firearm, shield, or identification card is lost or stolen.

### Immediate Notification Chain

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | **Immediately** report loss/theft to desk officer |
| 2 | Desk Officer | Make Command Log entry |
| 3 | Desk Officer | Notify commanding officer |

### LOST/STOLEN REPORT (PD520-150)

| Part | Content |
|------|---------|
| Part A | Initial report of loss/theft |
| Part B | Details of item lost/stolen |
| Part C | Investigation details |
| Part D | Results of investigation, action taken or contemplated |

### Commanding Officer Actions

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 15 | Determine if disciplinary action should be taken |
| 16 | Initiate command discipline or formal charges, if warranted |
| 17 | Enter disposition on SUPERVISOR'S COMPLAINT REPORT/COMMAND DISCIPLINE ELECTION REPORT |
| 17a | Forward duplicate copy of LOST/STOLEN REPORT to Department Advocate (Schedule B cases only) |
| 18 | Prepare Part "D" of LOST/STOLEN REPORT |
| 19 | Distribute LOST/STOLEN REPORT as indicated on rear of form |

### Negligence Determination

- CO must determine if loss resulted from negligence
- If disciplinary action warranted, **consultation with Department Advocate's Office** required to determine appropriate disciplinary action

### Member — After Investigation

- Report to CO, Employee Resources Section to obtain new shield or ID card
- Return to permanent command; request clerical member to enter new shield number on FORCE RECORD

### Special Locations

| Location | Report To |
|----------|-----------|
| Police Headquarters (5th Precinct confines) | **Headquarters Security Unit** |
| Police Academy (13th Precinct confines) | **Police Academy Integrity Unit** |

- In both cases, a **captain or above** from the reporting member's command conducts the investigation
- COMPLAINT REPORT serial numbers obtained from the 5th or 13th Precinct, as appropriate

### Shield Replacement — Timing Issue

- If shield lost when Employee Resources Section is closed and member scheduled for uniform duty: member assigned to duty in uniform **without a shield** until ERS opens the next business day

### Auxiliary Police Shields

- Processed per P.G. 207-12, "Lost or Stolen Property/Identity Theft"
- Additional copies to Auxiliary Police Section
- Auxiliary shields recovered (not evidence) forwarded to CO, Auxiliary Police Section — **no INVOICE required**

### Forms and Reports

| Form | Number |
|------|--------|
| COMPLAINT REPORT | PD313-152 |
| FORCE RECORD | PD406-143 |
| IDENTIFICATION CARD | PD416-091 |
| LOST/STOLEN FIREARM/SHIELD/I.D. CARD REPORT | PD520-150 |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE | PD521-141 |
| SUPERVISOR'S COMPLAINT REPORT/COMMAND DISCIPLINE ELECTION REPORT | PD468-123 |

> **Exam Alert:** When disciplinary action is warranted for loss of a firearm, **consultation with the Department Advocate's Office** is required.

> **Exam Alert:** At Police Headquarters or Police Academy, a **captain or above** from the member's command conducts the investigation — not the local precinct.

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO must determine negligence and consult Department Advocate before initiating discipline for lost/stolen firearms.

---

# Section 219 — Vehicles and Fleet Operations

## P.G. 219-01: Department Vehicles — Inspection at Start of Tour

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To inspect Department vehicles at the start of each tour to ensure safe and proper operation.

### Vehicle Inspection Checklist

| Item | Check |
|------|-------|
| Gasoline | Adequate level |
| Brakes | Functioning properly |
| Tires | Proper inflation, condition |
| E-Z Pass tag | Present, affixed to windshield |
| Mobility tablet | Present, operational |
| License Plate Reader (LPR) | Operational (if equipped) |
| Lights/siren | Functioning |
| Radio | Operational |

### Required Vehicle Equipment (Patrol)

| Equipment |
|-----------|
| 3-foot polycarbonate shield |
| Ballistic window inserts |
| Patrol kit (PPE) |
| Life ring (commands with waterfront) |

### Patrol Supervisor Vehicle — Additional Equipment

| Equipment |
|-----------|
| Rapid Mobilization Plan |
| Crowbar |
| Sledgehammer |
| Crime scene evidence cones |

> **Sergeant Focus:** Patrol supervisor vehicles carry additional emergency/tactical equipment. Ensure these items are present and serviceable each tour.

---

## P.G. 219-02: Weekly Vehicle Inspections

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure weekly accountability and condition reporting for all Department vehicles assigned to the command.

### Key Points

- Inspections conducted **each Sunday** after the second platoon has been posted
- Lieutenant platoon commander/patrol supervisor performs the inspection
- Prepare VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT (PD571-153)
- Record E-Z Pass tag serial numbers on the report

> **Exam Alert:** Weekly inspections occur on **Sunday** after the **second platoon** is posted.

---

## P.G. 219-03: Vehicle Maintenance

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure Department vehicles receive timely maintenance and repairs.

### Key Points

- Use Fleet Services Division **online application** to request maintenance
- Roadside repair service available for breakdowns
- Mobility tablet support provided by **Information Technology Bureau (ITB)**

---

## P.G. 219-04: Car Wash

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To provide guidelines for the washing of Department vehicles.

### Key Points

- **Competitive bidding** required for car wash services
- Prepare CAR WASH AUTHORIZATION (PD171-051)
- EXPENSE REPORTS forwarded to **Audits and Accounts** by the **10th of each month**

> **Exam Alert:** Car wash expense reports are due to Audits and Accounts by the **10th of each month**.

---

## P.G. 219-05: Tire Repair

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To provide for the repair of tires on Department vehicles.

---

## P.G. 219-06: Tire Replacement

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To provide for the replacement of tires on Department vehicles.

---

## P.G. 219-07: Tire Station Removal

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To provide for the removal of tires at designated tire stations.

---

## P.G. 219-08: Tire Chains

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To provide for the installation and use of tire chains on Department vehicles.

### Key Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Availability period | **November 1 through March 15** |
| Installation threshold | NOT installed until **more than 4 inches** of snow |
| Front-wheel drive restriction | Tire chains are **NOT** installed on front-wheel drive vehicles |

> **Exam Alert:** Tire chains are NOT installed until there is **more than 4 inches of snow** and are NOT used on **front-wheel drive** vehicles.

---

## P.G. 219-09: Department Tow

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To provide for the proper use of Department tow vehicles.

### Authorized Uses for Department Tow

| Use |
|-----|
| Tow inoperable Department vehicle |
| Tow evidence vehicle |
| Clear path for parade/emergency |
| Assist Emergency Service Unit (ESU) |
| Lift heavy articles |

> **Exam Alert:** Department Tow has **limited authorized uses** — it is not a general-purpose tow service.

---

## P.G. 219-10: Unmarked Vehicle Utilization Record

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To account for the utilization of unmarked Department vehicles.

### Vehicle Categories

| Category | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **General Purpose** | Standard unmarked vehicles for routine operational use |
| **Special Purpose** | Vehicles assigned for specific operational needs |
| **Category I** | Vehicles not required to be stored at Department facility; permitted to be removed from the City on a regular basis; specifically designated by the Police Commissioner |

> **Exam Alert:** **Category I** vehicles are specifically designated by the **Police Commissioner** and may leave the city regularly. Members with Category I vehicles are **ineligible** for Restricted Parking Permits.

---

# Section 219 — Key Terms, Timeframes, and Forms

## Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Category I Vehicle** | Unmarked vehicle not required to be stored at Department facility; permitted to be removed from the City regularly; designated by the Police Commissioner |
| **Category II Vehicle** | Unmarked vehicle with specific designations (exempt from OPP garage parking ineligibility) |
| **General Purpose Vehicle** | Standard unmarked vehicle for routine operational use |
| **Special Purpose Vehicle** | Unmarked vehicle assigned for specific operational needs |
| **License Plate Reader (LPR)** | Specialized camera/computer that captures license plate numbers, converts to text, compares against "hot list"; NOT connected to NYSPIN; activates on partial matches |
| **Mobile Digital Device** | Any smartphone, tablet, or portable computer issued by the Department |
| **Vehicle Identification Box (VIB)** | In-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing |
| **NYPD Restricted Parking Permit (Misc. 23-N)** | Cardboard parking credential (~4"×10") with serial number, command code, license plate number, and expiration date |
| **NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Pass** | Transit pass for subway/bus access (not express bus); valid ~7 years; assigned to individual member by unique serial number |
| **Radiation Detection Pager** | Device assigned to commands or individual members for detecting radiological threats; requires specific training |
| **Ullage** | Remaining capacity in a fuel tank; tanks filled to 90% ullage |
| **Regulated Infectious Waste** | Biohazard materials picked up by Medical Division messenger |

## Fuel Card Colors

| Card | Color |
|------|-------|
| Master | **Green** |
| CRALO | **Yellow** |
| Non-Fleet | **Orange** |
| Private | **Red** |
| Operator | **White** |

## Critical Timeframes

| Timeframe | Rule | Source |
|-----------|------|--------|
| **Each tour** | Inspect Department vehicle; account for radiation detection pagers; account for LPR devices | 219-01, 219-27, 219-31 |
| **Each Sunday** | Weekly vehicle inspection after second platoon posted | 219-02 |
| **10th of month** | Car wash expense reports due to Audits and Accounts | 219-04 |
| **Nov 1 – Mar 15** | Tire chains available | 219-08 |
| **>4 inches snow** | Threshold for installing tire chains | 219-08 |
| **24 hours** | LPR hot list must be updated within this period to use device | 219-31 |
| **5 business days** | Request ITB permission before taking mobile device to foreign country | 219-32 |
| **30 days** | Notify CO of vehicle change for Restricted Parking Permit; max time to use substitute vehicle | 219-29 |
| **7 years** | General validity period for NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Pass | 219-26 |
| **Monthly** | ICO audits DAS usage for 5 random members | 219-14 |
| **Quarterly** | CO surveys generator/mechanical/electrical rooms | 219-28 |
| **50%** | Minimum fuel tank level for commands with single tank (heating + generator) | 219-28 |
| **90%** | Maximum fill level (ullage) for fuel deliveries | 219-12 |
| **12 months** | Period for counting red light (5+) or school speed (15+) camera violations for permit ineligibility | 219-29 |

## Restricted Parking Permit Ineligibility Triggers

| Trigger |
|---------|
| Assigned Category I vehicle |
| OPP garage access (except Cat II) |
| Military & Extended Leave / vacation-terminal leave pending retirement |
| Under suspension |
| Auxiliary Police |
| 5+ red light camera violations in 12 months |
| 15+ school speed camera violations in 12 months |
| Vehicle in judgment for outstanding fines |
| Invalid license, registration, or insurance |

## Parking Always Prohibited (Even With Permit)

| Violation |
|-----------|
| Double parking |
| Fire hydrant/zone |
| Bus stop/lane |
| Sidewalk |
| Crosswalks/driveways |
| Obstructing traffic |
| No standing zones |
| Snow emergency |
| Taxi stand |
| Bicycle lane |
| No Permit Zone |

## E-Z Pass — Authorized vs. Unauthorized Toll Crossings

| Authorized | Unauthorized |
|-----------|-------------|
| Bronx-Whitestone Bridge | NJ Turnpike |
| George Washington Bridge | NY State Thruway |
| Bayonne Bridge | Garden State Parkway |
| Henry Hudson Bridge | New England Thruway |
| Holland/Lincoln Tunnels | Tappan Zee Bridge |
| Queens Midtown Tunnel | _(list not all-inclusive)_ |
| Throgs Neck Bridge | |
| Verrazano-Narrows Bridge | |
| Hugh L. Carey Tunnel | |
| RFK Bridge | |

## Key Forms

| Form | Number | Used For |
|------|--------|----------|
| VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT | PD571-153 | Weekly vehicle inspection |
| CAR WASH AUTHORIZATION | PD171-051 | Car wash services |
| RADIO ASSIGNMENT LOG | — | Tracking portable radio assignments |
| LOST/STOLEN FIREARM/SHIELD/I.D. CARD REPORT | PD520-150 | Parts A–D for reporting loss/theft |
| SUPERVISOR'S COMPLAINT REPORT/COMMAND DISCIPLINE ELECTION REPORT | PD468-123 | Disciplinary findings |
| COMPLAINT REPORT | PD313-152 | Lost/stolen/damaged property |
| FORCE RECORD | PD406-143 | Member records (shield number, serial numbers) |
| PROPERTY TRANSFER RECEIPT | PD521-145 | Personally assigned radiation pagers |
| RESTRICTED PARKING PERMIT APPLICATION | PD671-041 | Parking permit request |
| NYPD PHONE STICKER | PD589-090 | Affixed to Department phone case |
| DEPARTMENT MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE REPAIR/REPLACEMENT/RETURN REQUEST | PD589-163 | Phone repair/replacement |
| PROPERTY RECEIPT — DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE | PD520-013 | Separation from service property return |

## Key Contact Units

| Unit | When to Contact |
|------|----------------|
| **Fleet Services Division** | Vehicle maintenance, E-Z Pass reassignment, defective tags |
| **Building Maintenance Section** (24/7) | Emergency power outages — immediately |
| **ITB Service Desk** | LPR repairs, FORMS outages |
| **ITB Telecommunications Unit** | Lost/stolen/damaged mobile devices; callbox issues (Mon–Fri 0800–1600) |
| **ITB Information Security Desk** | Foreign travel with Department phone (5 business days prior) |
| **ITB Wheel** | Callbox issues after hours |
| **Operations Unit** | Lost/stolen portable radio |
| **Electronics Section** | Lost/stolen portable radio |
| **Internal Affairs Bureau** | Lost/stolen Department property |
| **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit** | Lost/stolen Department property alarms |
| **Transit Bureau Wheel** | Lost/stolen OMNY pass |
| **Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit** | OMNY pass issues; damaged/defective/lost/stolen passes |
| **Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator** | Radiation pager repair/replacement |
| **Citywide Counterterrorism Office** | Lost/stolen radiation pager |
| **Chief of Department's Vehicle Identification Unit** | Restricted Parking Permit applications/returns |
| **Department Advocate's Office** | Consultation for discipline re: lost/stolen firearm |
| **Employee Resources Section** | Replacement shield/ID card |
| **Medical Division** | Regulated infectious waste pickup |
| **Quartermaster Section** | AAA batteries for radiation pagers |
| **DCAS Inspector** | Must sign heating oil delivery ticket |

---

# Section 219 — Review Questions

## Instructions

Select the best answer for each question. Answers are provided after each question.

---

**1.** Under P.G. 219-08, tire chains should NOT be installed on Department vehicles until there is more than how many inches of snow?

A) 2 inches
B) 3 inches
C) 4 inches
D) 6 inches

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 4 inches**

Tire chains are NOT installed until there is more than 4 inches of snow. Additionally, tire chains are NOT installed on front-wheel drive vehicles. (P.G. 219-08)

</details>

---

**2.** Which color fuel card is designated for the CRALO?

A) Green
B) White
C) Yellow
D) Orange

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Yellow**

Card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator. (P.G. 219-11)

</details>

---

**3.** How often must the Integrity Control Officer audit the Domain Awareness System (DAS) and how many members must be reviewed?

A) Weekly, 3 random members
B) Monthly, 5 random members
C) Quarterly, 10 random members
D) Semi-annually, 5 random members

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Monthly, 5 random members**

The ICO conducts a monthly DAS audit reviewing 5 random members. (P.G. 219-14)

</details>

---

**4.** If a portable radio is lost and the member was NOT carrying it in an authorized carrying case, the finding will be:

A) No fault
B) Accidental loss
C) Negligence
D) Misconduct

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Negligence**

If a portable radio is lost and was not in an authorized carrying case at the time, the member is deemed negligent. (P.G. 219-15)

</details>

---

**5.** Which of the following is NOT a required notification when a portable radio is lost or stolen?

A) Operations Unit
B) Electronics Section
C) Fleet Services Division
D) Stolen Property Inquiry Unit

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Fleet Services Division**

The four required notifications are: Operations Unit, Electronics Section, Internal Affairs Bureau, and Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. Fleet Services Division is not notified for radio losses. (P.G. 219-15)

</details>

---

**6.** Weekly vehicle inspections are conducted on what day and after which platoon has been posted?

A) Saturday, after the first platoon
B) Sunday, after the second platoon
C) Monday, after the first platoon
D) Friday, after the third platoon

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Sunday, after the second platoon**

The lieutenant platoon commander/patrol supervisor inspects all Department vehicles each Sunday after the second platoon has been posted. (P.G. 219-02)

</details>

---

**7.** Regulated infectious waste from patrol kits is picked up by:

A) Department of Sanitation
B) Medical Division messenger
C) FDNY Hazmat
D) Quartermaster Section

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Medical Division messenger**

The Medical Division messenger picks up regulated infectious waste from the 32-gallon biohazard receptacle at the command. (P.G. 219-17)

</details>

---

**8.** Before a law enforcement action is initiated based on a License Plate Reader (LPR) alarm, the operator must:

A) Notify the patrol supervisor
B) Contact the Real Time Crime Center
C) Verify the vehicle status through NYSPIN
D) Photograph the license plate

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Verify the vehicle status through NYSPIN**

It is absolutely essential that the LPR operator verify the current status of a vehicle through NYSPIN prior to initiating any law enforcement action. The LPR is NOT connected to NYSPIN and activates on partial scan matches. (P.G. 219-31)

</details>

---

**9.** NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes are NOT valid for use on which type of transit?

A) Subway
B) Local bus
C) MTA express bus
D) Staten Island Railway

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) MTA express bus**

OMNY Police Passes are not valid for use on MTA express buses. Besides this exception, they have no electronic restrictions and may be used on subways and public buses. (P.G. 219-26)

</details>

---

**10.** A vehicle with how many red light camera violations within a twelve-month period will be ineligible for a Restricted Parking Permit?

A) 3 or more
B) 5 or more
C) 10 or more
D) 15 or more

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 5 or more**

Vehicles with 5 or more red light camera violations OR 15 or more school speed camera violations within a twelve-month period are ineligible. (P.G. 219-29)

</details>

---

**11.** Commands with a single fuel tank (heating and generator) must maintain the fuel level above what percentage?

A) 25%
B) 50%
C) 75%
D) 90%

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 50%**

Commands with a single fuel tank must maintain the level above 50%. Commands with two separate tanks are responsible only for the heating tank; Building Maintenance Section maintains the generator tank. (P.G. 219-28)

</details>

---

**12.** When a member's firearm is lost and disciplinary action is warranted, the commanding officer must consult with:

A) Internal Affairs Bureau
B) Legal Bureau
C) Department Advocate's Office
D) Chief of Department

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Department Advocate's Office**

When disciplinary action is warranted for loss of or failure to safeguard a firearm, a consultation with the Department Advocate's Office is required to determine appropriate disciplinary action. (P.G. 219-21)

</details>

---

**13.** A member's firearm, shield, or ID card is lost within Police Headquarters (5th Precinct). The investigation will be conducted by:

A) The 5th Precinct commanding officer
B) A captain or above from the reporting member's command
C) The Headquarters Security Unit
D) Internal Affairs Bureau

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A captain or above from the reporting member's command**

At Police Headquarters or Police Academy, the loss is reported to the Headquarters Security Unit (or Police Academy Integrity Unit), but the investigation is conducted by a captain or above from the reporting member's command. (P.G. 219-21)

</details>

---

**14.** Which of the following is a permitted use of a Department tow vehicle?

A) Towing a civilian vehicle to a repair shop
B) Towing an inoperable Department vehicle
C) Removing vehicles for alternate side parking
D) Towing abandoned civilian vehicles to the pound

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Towing an inoperable Department vehicle**

Department Tow has limited authorized uses: inoperable Department vehicle, evidence vehicle, parade/emergency path clearance, assisting ESU, and lifting heavy articles. (P.G. 219-09)

</details>

---

**15.** How many business days prior to foreign travel must a member request ITB Information Security Desk permission to bring a Department mobile device?

A) 2 business days
B) 3 business days
C) 5 business days
D) 10 business days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 5 business days**

Members must request permission from the ITB Information Security Desk at least 5 business days prior to taking a Department mobile digital device to a foreign country. (P.G. 219-32)

</details>

---

# Section 220 — Citywide Incident Management

## Source
- **Patrol Guide Section:** 220
- **Source PDF:** public-pguide4.pdf (OCR)
- **Priority:** High

## Learning Objectives
- Understand CIMS (Citywide Incident Management System) — NYC's adaptation of NIMS/ICS
- Know Single Command vs Unified Command structures and the Primary Agency Matrix
- Identify Command Staff (PIO, Safety, Liaison) and General Staff (Operations, Planning, Logistics, Finance/Admin, I/I) roles
- Understand incident facilities (ICP, Operations Post, I/I Post, Staging Area) and their co-location rules
- Know resource categories (Single Resource, Resource Team, Special Response Team) and statuses (Assigned, Available, Out-of-Service)
- Recognize the CIMS incident sequence and Transfer of Command options
- Understand IMT activation authority and CBRN/HazMat command rules

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-220-structure-roles.md` | 220-01 through 220-04: Organizational structure, Primary Agency Matrix, Core Competencies, Agency Roles |
| `section-220-command-element.md` | 220-05: Command Element duties, Single/Unified Command, Span of Control, Management by Objectives |
| `section-220-command-staff.md` | 220-06 through 220-08: PIO, Safety Officer, Liaison Officer |
| `section-220-general-staff.md` | 220-09 through 220-13: Operations, Planning, Logistics, Finance/Admin, Intelligence/Investigations |
| `section-220-operations-procedures.md` | 220-14 through 220-18, 220-22, 220-27, 220-28: Supporting Agencies, Coordinating Agency, Communications, Transfer of Command, Incident Sequence, Demobilization, ICS Form 201, IMT Activation |
| `section-220-facilities-resources.md` | 220-19 through 220-21, 220-23 through 220-26: Incident Facilities, Staging Area, Air Operations, Check-In, Resources, Resource Assignments |
| `key-terms.md` | Comprehensive definitions, acronyms, command matrices, resource categories, priorities, timeframes |
| `review-questions.md` | 25 multiple-choice questions covering all major CIMS concepts |

## Sergeant Focus Areas

- **Transfer of Command**: An arriving sergeant has four options — assuming command is NOT mandatory; may elect to make no change if the current CE is managing effectively
- **Span of Control**: Law enforcement ratio is 1:8 to 1:10 (wider than general 1:3 to 1:7)
- **Squad composition**: 1 Sergeant + 8 Police Officers — the sergeant leads the basic CIMS tactical unit
- **Life Safety priority**: Always #1 — supersedes all other operations including investigation
- **Check-In**: Resources must check in BEFORE assignment to Operations Section
- **Safety Officer authority**: May directly stop unsafe activities when life is in imminent danger without chain of command approval
- **CBRN command**: NYPD assumes Single Command when crime/terrorism is involved; Unified Command otherwise
- **Clear text communications**: No 10-codes at CIMS incidents — use clear text and CIMS terminology

---

# Section 220 — Command Element

## P.G. 220-05: CIMS Command Element

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 15

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Command Element in CIMS.

### Command Element Responsibilities (Key Steps)

| Step | Responsibility |
|------|---------------|
| 1 | Assume overall incident management responsibility |
| 2 | Obtain briefing; assess situation; notify Desk Officer, Patrol Borough, Operations Unit, Communications |
| 3 | Ensure incident safety; intervene in/suspend unsafe operations |
| 5 | Establish priorities: **Life Safety (#1)**, Site Management, Investigative, Recovery/Restoration |
| 7 | Utilize "Management by Objectives" — determine Incident Objectives, Strategies, Tactical Direction |
| 12 | Select location and establish **Incident Command Post (ICP)** |
| 13 | Manage incident **from the ICP** |
| 14 | Establish appropriate organizational structure |
| 34 | Formulate/approve/authorize one single **Incident Action Plan** (verbal or written) |

### Incident Management Priorities (In Order)

| Priority | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **#1 — Life Safety** | Immediate priority; supersedes all other operations (absent extraordinary circumstances) |
| #2 — Site Management | Perimeter control, traffic/crowd control, security |
| #3 — Investigative Operations | May run concurrently with Life Safety (with Command Element approval) |
| #4 — Recovery/Restoration | Final mitigation; return to non-emergency; usually after Investigative Operations conclude |

### Incident Objectives Must Be

| Quality | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Attainable** | Considering existing limitations and constraints |
| **Measurable** | To determine if being achieved effectively |
| **Adaptable/Flexible** | To provide for alternative strategies |

### Incident Command Post (ICP) Requirements

| Requirement |
|-------------|
| Reasonable distance from incident |
| Safety, health, security, force protection |
| Easy/expeditious access and egress |
| Sufficiently large with room for expansion |
| Permits continuous operations |
| Adequate workspace and utility/communications |
| Generally within the **Site Access Control / Outer Perimeter** |

### ICP Notifications

1. Desk Officer, Precinct of occurrence
2. Patrol Borough Office
3. Communications Section dispatcher/supervisor
4. Operations Unit
5. NYPD Emergency Operations Center (if activated)
6. Other appropriate NYPD units
7. Other appropriate agencies/organizations

### One ICP Rule

- For **every incident** except nuclear weapon detonation, there is **only ONE single ICP**
- Nuclear weapon incidents require **borough-centric ICPs**

### Command Element Structures

| Structure | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Single Command** | One Primary Agency designates one Incident Commander with overall responsibility |
| **Unified Command** | Multiple Primary Agencies each designate an Agency Incident Commander; all co-locate at one ICP; jointly set objectives and create one IAP |

### Span of Control

| Context | Recommended Ratio |
|---------|-------------------|
| General | **1:3 to 1:7** |
| Law enforcement operations | **1:8 to 1:10** |

### Unity of Command

Every subordinate has **only one** designated supervisor.

### Chain of Command

Direction may only be given through the established Chain of Command (absent emergency circumstances).

### Deputy

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Qualifications | Same as the principal (knowledge, skills, experience) |
| Must be capable of | Assuming the principal's position |
| May be from | A different agency/jurisdiction |
| Role (flexible) | "Eyes and ears," make notifications, function as relief, assigned specific tasks |

### Transfer of Command

- Should be **face-to-face** if possible
- Incoming Command Element must receive **comprehensive briefing**
- When possible, Transfer of Command should occur at the **start of a new Operational Period**
- Notifications to all Primary Agencies, Supporting Agencies, Staff, Communications

### Demobilization

- Command Element orders demobilization when appropriate
- Ensure all required "After-Action" briefings, reports, meetings, critiques are completed

> **Exam Alert:** **Life Safety is the immediate #1 priority** — it supersedes all other incident management operations (absent extraordinary circumstances).

> **Exam Alert:** CIMS Span of Control: general = **1:3 to 1:7**; law enforcement = **1:8 to 1:10**.

> **Exam Alert:** There is only **ONE Incident Command Post** per incident (except nuclear weapon incidents).

> **Exam Alert:** In Unified Command, Agency Incident Commanders are at **equal authority** regardless of agency rank.

> **Sergeant Focus:** A Sergeant arriving first at a multi-agency incident may serve as the initial Command Element/Incident Commander until relieved through Transfer of Command by a higher-ranking officer.

---

# Section 220 — Command Staff

## P.G. 220-06: CIMS Public Information Officer

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Public Information Officer in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Manage public information activities (media inquiries, information development) |
| Obtain **approval from Command Element** before releasing information |
| Formulate/disseminate unified, consistent, concise, accurate official information |
| Conduct media briefings; distribute written media releases |
| Release Emergency Public Information, warnings, and protective measures |
| Establish/supervise media placement areas (sufficient space, no interference with operations) |
| Monitor media and forward useful information to Command Element/Planning/Intelligence |

### Designation Rules

| Structure | Designated By |
|-----------|--------------|
| Single Command | Incident Commander (but **Mayor's Press Office** has authority and may designate) |
| Unified Command | Agency Incident Commanders jointly (but **Mayor's Press Office** has authority and may designate) |

### Joint Information Center (JIC)

- Established when necessary for large, complex, multi-agency incidents
- Staffed by public affairs personnel from all Primary and Supporting Agencies
- JIC spokesperson designated by **Mayor's Press Office**
- In extremely unusual circumstances, multiple JICs may be established

> **Exam Alert:** The **Mayor's Press Office** has authority to designate the Public Information Officer and the JIC spokesperson.

---

## P.G. 220-07: CIMS Safety Officer

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 7

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Safety Officer in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Perform Incident Safety Analysis |
| Advise Command Element on safety, health, force protection, security |
| Develop/implement safety measures for personnel, vehicles, equipment, facilities |
| Implement PPE program |
| Monitor operations for compliance with Health and Safety Plan |
| **Immediately stop hazardous/unsafe activities** using chain of command |
| **Exercise emergency authority** to directly stop if life/safety in imminent danger |
| Review/approve the Medical Plan |

### Safety Officer Function — Legal Requirement

The Safety Officer function is **required by law** at a hazardous materials incident.

### Emergency Authority

The Safety Officer may **exercise emergency authority to directly prevent, stop, and/or correct** hazardous, unhealthy, or unsafe activities when life or safety of incident personnel is in **imminent danger** — then subsequently notify the Command Element.

### Designation Rules

| Structure | Designation |
|-----------|-------------|
| Single Command (non-CBRN) | Incident Commander designates **one** Safety Officer |
| Unified Command | Each Agency Incident Commander may designate an Agency Safety Officer (or jointly designate one) |
| Single Command (CBRN/HazMat) | NYPD, FDNY, DEP, DOHMH each designate an Agency Safety Officer (or jointly designate one) |

> **Exam Alert:** Safety Officer can **exercise emergency authority** to directly stop unsafe activities when life is in **imminent danger** — without waiting for chain of command approval.

> **Exam Alert:** Safety Officer function is **required by law** at hazardous materials incidents.

---

## P.G. 220-08: CIMS Liaison Officer

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Liaison Officer in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Serve as primary contact for Supporting Agencies |
| Coordinate with Supporting Agency/Agency Representatives |
| Ensure inter-agency coordination and cooperation |
| Facilitate communication between agencies |

### Designation Rules

| Structure | Designated By |
|-----------|--------------|
| Single Command | Incident Commander (but **NYCEM** has authority and may designate) |
| Unified Command | Agency Incident Commanders jointly (but **NYCEM** has authority and may designate) |

> **Exam Alert:** **NYCEM** (NYC Emergency Management) has authority to designate the Liaison Officer.

> **Exam Alert:** Command Staff positions are: **Public Information Officer** (Mayor's Press Office authority), **Safety Officer**, **Liaison Officer** (NYCEM authority).

---

# Section 220 — Incident Facilities and Resources

## P.G. 220-19: CIMS Incident Facilities

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 6

### Purpose

To define and describe CIMS Incident Facilities.

### Facility Types

| Facility | Description | Key Rules |
|----------|-------------|-----------|
| **Incident Command Post (ICP)** | Where Command Element performs command function; central coordination point | Only **ONE** per incident (except nuclear); generally within Outer Perimeter |
| **Operations Section Post** | Where Operations Section chief directs tactical operations | Generally **NOT co-located** with ICP; within Outer Perimeter |
| **Intelligence/Investigations Section Post** | Where I/I Section chief directs intelligence/investigative operations | Generally **NOT co-located** with ICP or Operations Post |
| **Incident Base** | Central location for logistic support and uncommitted resources | **Not activated in NYC** (absent extraordinary circumstances) |
| **Camp** | Temporary location for food, water, sanitation, lodging | **Not activated in NYC** (absent extraordinary circumstances) |
| **Other Facilities** | Mass Care, Triage, Temporary Morgue, Family Assistance, Mass Decontamination | As needed |

### ICP Location Requirements

| Requirement |
|-------------|
| Reasonable/appropriate distance from incident |
| Safety, health, security, force protection |
| Easy/expeditious access and egress |
| Sufficiently large, allows expansion |
| Permits continuous operations |
| Adequate workspace and utility/communications |
| Generally within **Site Access Control / Outer Perimeter** |

### ICP Key Rules

- Initially, ICP is wherever the Command Element is located
- Can be any facility: vehicle, trailer, tent, open area, room
- Identified by **name of the incident**
- Marked with flag, banner, beacon, sign, or distinctive marker
- Should **NOT be relocated** if possible (but may be if conditions change)

> **Exam Alert:** **Incident Base** and **Camp** are NOT activated in NYC absent extraordinary circumstances. These are NIMS ICS concepts for incidents outside NYC.

---

## P.G. 220-20: CIMS Incident Facilities — Staging Area

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 4

### Definition

**Staging Area** — A temporary location where resources are assembled, organized, and await assignment.

### Key Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Number | More than one Staging Area may be established |
| Naming | Each identified by **geographic name or number** |
| Manager | Each has a designated **Staging Area Manager** |
| Resource status | Resources at Staging Area are in **"Available" status** |
| Readiness | Resources must be ready for deployment within **3 minutes** |
| Location | Close enough for timely deployment but far enough for safety |
| Security | Adequate safety, health, security, force protection measures |

### Staging Area Manager Duties

- Manage resources in Available status
- Organize resources for deployment
- Track resource status
- Ensure Check-In is performed
- Coordinate with Operations Section chief

> **Exam Alert:** Resources in "Available" status at a Staging Area must be ready for deployment within **3 minutes**.

---

## P.G. 220-21: CIMS Incident Facilities — Air Operations

**Date Effective:** 08/28/19 | **Pages:** 5

### Key Concepts

- **Helibase** — Location for parking, fueling, and maintenance of helicopters
- **Helispot** — Temporary location for helicopter landing/takeoff
- Air operations managed by the **Air Operations Branch Director**

---

## P.G. 220-23: CIMS Air Operations

**Date Effective:** 08/28/19 | **Pages:** 4

### Key Points

- Detailed air operations procedures for helicopter deployment at incidents
- Coordination with Aviation Unit

---

## P.G. 220-24: CIMS "Check-In" Process

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To describe the "Check-In" process at an incident.

### What Check-In Accomplishes

| Function |
|----------|
| Ensure resource accountability |
| Track number, location, and status of resources |
| Assign and reassign resources |
| Locate personnel for emergency notifications |
| Prepare accurate time and overtime records |
| Plan for demobilization |
| Demobilize and release resources |

### Who Performs Check-In

| Priority | Performer |
|----------|-----------|
| 1st | Resources Unit |
| 2nd | Planning Section chief (if Resources Unit not activated) |
| 3rd | Command Element (if Planning Section not activated) |

### Check-In Locations (NYC)

| Location |
|----------|
| **Staging Area(s)** — Check-In with Resources Unit or Staging Area Manager |
| **Incident Command Post** — Check-In with Resources Unit |
| **Intelligence/Investigations Section Post** — When applicable |
| **Helibase(s)** — Absent extraordinary circumstances, not used in NYC |

### Check-In Forms

**DETAIL ROSTER / ASSIGNMENT SHEETS (PD406-141)** or other appropriate forms.

### Critical Rule

Resources must formally **Check-In before** reporting to Operations Section for assignment (absent extraordinary circumstances).

> **Exam Alert:** Resources must **Check-In before assignment** to the Operations Section. The 4 NYC Check-In locations are: Staging Area, ICP, I/I Section Post, and Helibase.

---

## P.G. 220-25: CIMS Resources

**Date Effective:** 07/19/16 | **Pages:** 5

### Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Resources** | Personnel, teams, equipment, supplies, vehicles, aircraft, watercraft, facilities |
| **Kind** | Broad class characterizing similar resources (e.g., marked patrol car, van, motorcycle) |
| **Type** | Level of capability: Roman numeral I (greatest) to IV (least) |

### Resource Categories

| Category | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Single Resource** | One vehicle/watercraft/aircraft + crew, single personnel, K-9 team, or pre-designated team (Squad, Mobile Field Force) |
| **Resource Team** | Same Kinds and Types of Single Resources assembled for a tactical assignment; must have designated Leader, transportation, common communications |
| **Special Response Team** | Any combination of different Kinds and Types assembled for a tactical assignment; must have Leader, transportation, common communications |

### NYPD Resource Examples

| Resource | Composition |
|----------|-------------|
| **Squad** | 1 Sergeant + 8 Police Officers |
| **Mobile Field Force** | 2 to 6 Squads |
| RMP | 1 vehicle + 2 Officers |
| Highway Motorcycle Squad | 1 Sergeant + 5 Officers on motorcycles |
| Mounted Squad | 1 Sergeant + 5 Mounted Officers on horses |
| ESU Entry Team | 1 Sergeant + 7–8 ESU Officers + vehicle(s) |
| ESU CAT Car | Nondescript Suburban + 1 Sergeant + 4 Officers |
| Bomb Squad Response Team | Van + 2 Officers + EDC + RMI |

### Resource Status Conditions

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Assigned** | Performing work tasks under supervisor direction |
| **Available** | Ready for assignment; deploy within **3 minutes** |
| **Out-of-Service** | Not able to be assigned or available (mechanical, rest, illness, injury, etc.) |

### Status Change Authority

Command Element, Operations Section chief(s), Branch Directors, Sector Supervisors, Group Supervisors, Resource Team/Special Response Team Leaders, I/I Section chief, Unit Leaders, Managers.

> **Exam Alert:** The 3 resource statuses are **Assigned, Available, Out-of-Service**. Available resources deploy within **3 minutes**.

> **Exam Alert:** A **Squad** = 1 Sergeant + 8 Officers. A **Mobile Field Force** = 2 to 6 Squads.

---

## P.G. 220-26: CIMS Resource Assignments

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 2

### Key Points

- Resources assigned to tactical operations via the Operations Section
- Resource assignments documented in the Incident Action Plan
- Span of Control principles apply to all resource assignments

---

# Section 220 — General Staff Sections

## P.G. 220-09: CIMS Operations Section Chief

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 14

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Operations Section chief in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Direct and coordinate **all tactical operations** at the incident |
| Implement tactical portion of the Incident Action Plan |
| Establish Operations Section Post |
| Manage/supervise Sectors, Groups, Branches |
| Maintain accountability for all resources assigned to Operations Section |

### Subordinate Elements

| Element | Supervised By | Managed By |
|---------|-------------|------------|
| **Branch** | Branch Director | Major functional or geographic areas |
| **Sector** | Sector Supervisor | Geographic area of operations |
| **Group** | Group Supervisor | Functional area of operations |

### Operations Section Post

- Separate from the ICP (generally NOT co-located)
- Located within the Site Access Control / Outer Perimeter
- Where Operations Section chief and staff direct tactical operations

### Unified Operations Section

Activated when:
- Unified Command — Command Element is managing the incident
- Single Command at a CBRN/HazMat incident

Each Primary Agency designates an **Agency Operations Section chief** based on Core Competencies.

> **Exam Alert:** The Operations Section directs **all tactical operations**. The Operations Section Post is generally **NOT co-located** with the ICP.

---

## P.G. 220-10: CIMS Planning Section Chief

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 9

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Planning Section chief in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Collect, evaluate, and disseminate incident information |
| Prepare/distribute the **Incident Action Plan** |
| Prepare the **Demobilization Plan** |
| Track resource status |
| Conduct planning meetings |
| Provide technical specialists |

### Subordinate Units

| Unit | Function |
|------|----------|
| **Resources Unit** | Track resource status; perform Check-In function |
| **Situation Unit** | Collect/analyze situation data; prepare situation displays |
| **Demobilization Unit** | Plan/execute orderly demobilization |
| **Documentation Unit** | Maintain incident files and records |

> **Exam Alert:** The **Planning Section** prepares the Incident Action Plan and the Demobilization Plan. The **Resources Unit** tracks resource status and performs the Check-In function.

---

## P.G. 220-11: CIMS Logistics Section Chief

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 9

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Logistics Section chief in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Provide **all service and support** for the incident |
| Manage communications infrastructure |
| Manage food, water, supplies, equipment |
| Manage facilities and transportation |
| Manage medical support for incident personnel (not victims) |

### Subordinate Structure

| Branch | Units |
|--------|-------|
| **Service Branch** | Communications Unit, Medical Unit, Food Unit |
| **Support Branch** | Supply Unit, Facilities Unit, Ground Support Unit |

> **Exam Alert:** Logistics provides **service and support** (NOT tactical operations). Medical Unit provides care for **incident personnel**, not victims.

---

## P.G. 220-12: CIMS Finance/Administration Section Chief

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 5

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Finance/Administration Section chief in CIMS.

### Key Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Track all incident costs |
| Manage financial/administrative functions |
| Process personnel time records |
| Manage procurement |
| Process claims |

### Subordinate Units

| Unit | Function |
|------|----------|
| **Time Unit** | Record personnel time |
| **Procurement Unit** | Manage contracts and purchasing |
| **Compensation/Claims Unit** | Process injury claims |
| **Cost Unit** | Track overall incident costs |

---

## P.G. 220-13: CIMS Intelligence/Investigations Section Chief

**Date Effective:** 02/18/15 | **Pages:** 7

### Purpose

To outline the duties of the Intelligence/Investigations Section chief in CIMS.

### Key Concept

When there is a **considerable investigative or intelligence component**, the Command Element should establish an Intelligence/Investigations Section as part of the General Staff. In unusual situations where it's not considerable, it may be organized as a Branch or Group within Operations, or even a Command Staff position.

### Subordinate Groups

| Group | Function |
|-------|----------|
| **Intelligence Group** | Intelligence collection and analysis |
| **Surveillance and Evidence Collection Group** | Surveillance operations, evidence collection |
| **Forensic Group** | Forensic analysis and processing |
| **Missing/Unidentified Persons Group** | Track missing or unidentified persons |
| **Investigative Support Group** | Support investigative operations |

### Intelligence/Investigations Section Post

- Generally **NOT co-located** with the ICP or Operations Section Post
- Located at a **secure location** within reasonable distance

> **Exam Alert:** The Intelligence/Investigations Section may be organized as a **General Staff Section** (considerable component), an **Operations Branch/Group** (unusual), or a **Command Staff position/Planning function** (extremely unusual).

---

# Section 220 — Incident Operations and Procedures

## P.G. 220-14: CIMS Supporting Agencies and Agency Representatives

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To define Supporting Agencies and Agency Representatives.

### Definition

**Supporting Agency** — An agency that is NOT a Primary Agency but has relevant resources, information, expertise, or jurisdictional authority.

**Agency Representative** — A representative designated by a Supporting Agency to function as the agency's spokesperson at the incident.

### Key Rules

- Agency Representative reports to the **Liaison Officer** (or Command Element if no Liaison Officer)
- Must have **authority** to make decisions on behalf of their agency
- All Supporting Agencies co-locate at the ICP when directed

---

## P.G. 220-15: CIMS Coordinating Agency

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 1

### Definition

**NYC Emergency Management (NYCEM)** is New York City's designated **Coordinating Agency**.

### Key Roles

- Facilitate, coordinate, and support inter-agency activities
- May respond to multi-agency incidents
- Participate in Unified or Single Command organization
- Facilitate and support **Recovery/Restoration Operations**
- Ensure ICP and Operations Section Post are established and staffed

> **Exam Alert:** **NYCEM** is the Coordinating Agency — they facilitate and support, but do NOT command the incident.

---

## P.G. 220-16: CIMS Integrated Communications

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 2

### Key Points

- Use **clear text** for inter-agency communications (no codes)
- Use **CIMS nomenclature/terminology**
- Observe strict radio and phone communications discipline
- Develop and implement a **Communications Plan** (component of the IAP)
- When appropriate, use Borough/Citywide interoperable radio frequencies
- NYC Agencies' Citywide interoperable **800 MHz** radio frequency

> **Exam Alert:** All incident personnel must use **clear text** (no 10-codes) for inter-agency communications and use **CIMS terminology**.

---

## P.G. 220-17: CIMS Transfer of Command

**Date Effective:** 02/04/15 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To describe the formal Transfer of Command process.

### Options Upon Arrival of Ranking Member

| Option | Action |
|--------|--------|
| 1 | Assume command (if no Command Element exists) |
| 2 | Assume command from current Command Element |
| 3 | Make **no change** to current Command Element |
| 4 | Designate a **third party** to assume command |

### When Transfer of Command Occurs

- Higher-ranking person arrives and elects to assume command
- Incident stabilized — transfer to less senior person
- New Operational Period — designated personnel assume command
- Command Element dismissed at end of tour

### Transfer of Command Checklist

| Item |
|------|
| CIMS organization implemented? CIMS terminology in use? |
| One single ICP established? |
| Face-to-face transfer if possible? |
| Incoming Command Element personally assesses situation with outgoing? |
| Comprehensive briefing provided? |
| Appropriate time chosen for formal transfer? |
| Incoming Command Element formally accepted command? |
| Notifications made? |

### Transfer of Command Briefing Should Include

Concise history, current situation, objectives/strategy/priorities, IAP, organizational structure, resource assignments, status of tactical/investigative operations, intelligence/threat information, incident facilities status, communications plan, constraints/limitations, current concerns, future predictions, delegation of authority issues.

> **Exam Alert:** A ranking member arriving at an incident has **four options**: assume command, assume from current CE, make no change, or designate a third party. Transfer should be **face-to-face** when possible.

> **Sergeant Focus:** An arriving sergeant may elect to **make no change** to the command element if the current officer is managing effectively — assuming command is not mandatory.

---

## P.G. 220-18: CIMS Incident Sequence

**Date Effective:** 08/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 66 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To describe the general CIMS Incident Sequence.

### Incident Sequence (In Order)

| Phase | Activities |
|-------|-----------|
| **1. Initial Response** | Agencies respond; first arrivals assess scene |
| **2. Life Safety Operations** | Immediate #1 priority; rescue, EMS |
| **3. Site Management Operations** | NYPD establishes perimeters; excludes non-essential personnel |
| **4. Investigative Operations** | May run concurrently with Life Safety (with CE approval); life safety concerns take precedence |
| **5. Recovery/Restoration Operations** | Final mitigation; return to non-emergency; may be delayed for investigation |
| **6. Demobilization** | Operations Section Post and ICP demobilize; incident concludes |

### Key Sequencing Rules

- **Life Safety** is always the immediate #1 priority
- Investigative Operations may run **concurrently** with Life Safety (life safety concerns take precedence)
