### ESU/Harbor Unit Special Weapons Uses

| Authorized Situation |
|---------------------|
| Barricaded criminal/hostage taker/EDP with firearm (or reasonable belief) |
| Armed hostage taker threatening life of hostage |
| Searches for armed perpetrators, warrant executions |
| VIP escorts and protection |
| Guarding dangerous criminals (with DOC) |
| Guarding payrolls, narcotics, or weapons transfers |
| Captain or above deems appropriate |
| Spontaneous incident requiring immediate action (last resort) |
| NOT for restraining EDP unless firearm involved |

> **Exam Alert:** Special weapons NEVER issued to member whose **qualifications are not current**. Shotgun is **mandated** for solo sector patrol vehicles.

---

## P.G. 221-11: Guidelines and Approval Process Pertaining to the Use of Distraction Devices

**Date Effective:** 06/26/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 52 | **Pages:** 2

### Definition

A **distraction device** is a less lethal hand-held apparatus generating startling sound and light energy when deployed into a confined area. Provides short-term tactical cover for high-risk entries.

### Authorization

- Only authorized for use by trained **Emergency Service Unit** members
- Requires direct knowledge and **specific approval** of **Chief of Department** (or designee), absent exigent circumstances

### Approval Process — Search Warrants

| Step | Who | Action |
|------|-----|--------|
| 1 | Captain or above | Contact **borough executive officer** |
| 2 | Borough XO | Review and confer with **Chief of Department** (or designee) for **final approval** |

### Approval Process — EDP/Perpetrator Search/Exigent Circumstances

| Step | Who | Action |
|------|-----|--------|
| 1 | Captain or above | Notify **duty inspector** |
| 2 | Duty inspector | Request approval of **citywide duty chief** |
| 3 | Duty chief | Contact **Chief of Department** (or designee) via Operations Unit for **final approval** |

### Exigent Circumstances Exception

Ranking ESU supervisor may request approval directly from **Chief of Special Operations** or **CO, ESU**. Once stabilized, the captain must **immediately notify the Chief of Department** via Operations Unit.

### Post-Incident Reporting

Captain concerned prepares Typed Letterhead report:
- Obtain **Distraction Device Utilization Control Number** from ESU Desk Officer
- Forward through channels to Chief of Department

> **Exam Alert:** Distraction devices require **Chief of Department** approval (or designee). Only used by trained **ESU** members. At every level, **all alternatives must be considered** before recommending approval.

---

# Section 221 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Force Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **De-Escalation** | Taking action to stabilize situation, reduce immediacy of threat; gain voluntary compliance |
| **Objectively Reasonable Standard** | Reasonableness based on totality of circumstances; perspective of similarly trained/experienced member |
| **Excessive Force** | Force greater than what a reasonable officer would use under same circumstances |
| **Active Resisting** | Physically evasive movements (bracing, tensing, pushing, verbal intent to resist) |
| **Active Aggression** | Threat or overt act of assault with present ability, indicating imminent assault/injury |
| **Passive Resistance** | Minimal physical action (standing motionless, going limp) |
| **Prohibited Methods of Restraint** | Sitting, kneeling, standing on chest/back compressing diaphragm |
| **Chokehold** | Any pressure to throat, carotid artery, or windpipe affecting breathing or blood flow |

## Force Incident Levels

| Level | Key Criteria | Investigating Entity |
|-------|-------------|---------------------|
| **Level 1** | Hand/foot strikes, take-downs, grappling, O.C. spray, mesh blanket, CEW **cartridge mode**, physical injury from police action | Immediate Supervisor |
| **Level 2** | Striking with object (baton, vehicle), canine bite, CEW **drive stun**, prohibited acts (excl. chokehold), alleged excessive force, attempted prisoner suicide, unconsciousness/lost tooth/stitches | CO/XO/Duty Captain + IAB |
| **Level 3** | Force readily capable of causing death/serious injury (not firearm), chokehold/prohibited restraint, excessive force + serious injury, attempted prisoner suicide + serious injury | Internal Affairs Bureau |
| **Level 4** | ALL firearms discharges, UMOS firearm fired by another, subject seriously injured/likely to die/dies in/prior to custody | Force Investigation Division |

## Immediate Supervisor Definition

Available supervisor NOT involved in the incident, from same command, at least **one rank higher** than all involved members. If none available, **duty captain** determines.

## Injury Classifications

| Term | Examples |
|------|---------|
| **Physical Illness/Injury** | Minor swelling, contusions, lacerations, abrasions |
| **Serious Physical Injury/Illness** | Broken/fractured bones, aneurysm, hospital admission required, heart attack, stroke, life-threatening |

## Less Lethal Weapons

| Weapon | Key Parameters |
|--------|---------------|
| **O.C. Pepper Spray** | Two 1-second bursts, minimum 3 feet, aim at eyes; NOT on passive resistance |
| **CEW (TASER 7)** | Cartridge mode = Level 1; Drive stun = Level 2; standard cycle 5 seconds; never on rear-cuffed; support side holster |
| **Tear Gas** | Borough commander approval; ESU only; dry cleaning does NOT remove residue |
| **Distraction Device** | Chief of Department approval; ESU only |

## CEW Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Function Test** | Mandatory test into firearms safety station in test mode under supervisor observation |
| **Laser Warning** | De-escalation — placing laser dot on subject for voluntary compliance |
| **Warning Arc** | Visible/audible arcing of electricity on TASER 7 |
| **Cartridge Mode** | Probes fired for neuromuscular incapacitation (Level 1) |
| **Drive Stun** | Direct/close contact with front electrodes (Level 2) |

## Special Weapons

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Special Weapons** | Shotguns, long guns, automatic weapons |
| **Special Weapons Record** | Three-section log: Qualification, Issuance, Maintenance |

## EDP Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **EDP** | Person apparently mentally ill/temporarily deranged, likely to result in serious injury to self or others |
| **Zone of Safety** | Distance from EDP > effective range of weapon (not firearm); minimum **20 feet** |
| **Crossfire** | Unintentional placement of person in position to be injured by firearms discharge |
| **Crossfire Awareness** | Being aware of threat/surroundings; communicate "crossfire" when aware |
| **CIT** | Crisis Intervention Team — specially trained members as contact officers |

## "Resisting Recidivist"

Prisoner arrested for charges against MOS (resisting arrest, assault 2nd, assault on PO, aggravated assault on PO) **more than once within 5 years**. Requires detective squad enhancement.

## Firearm Prohibitions Summary

| SHALL NOT |
|-----------|
| Discharge to endanger innocent persons |
| Discharge in defense of property |
| Discharge at fleeing felon with no imminent threat |
| Fire warning shots |
| Discharge at/from moving vehicle (unless deadly force by other means) |
| Discharge at animal (unless imminent injury, no retreat) |
| Cock a firearm (double action only) |

## Critical Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| PD324-140 (Firearms Discharge Report) | Within **48 hours** |
| Temporary assignment after discharge causing death/injury | Minimum **3 consecutive tours** |
| Post-incident member welfare follow-up | **24-48 hours** |
| Report to NYS Commission of Correction (SCOC M187) | Fax within **6 hours**; mail within **10 days** |
| CEW replacement cartridge | Within **72 hours** |
| CEW unintentional discharge retraining | Within **5 business days** |
| O.C. pepper spray effects | Do not dissipate for **15-30 minutes** |
| Vehicle checkpoint maximum duration | **2 hours** at one location |
| CEW standard cycle | **5 seconds** |
| CEW exposure risk threshold | Beyond **15 seconds** |
| Alcohol — prudent abstinence before tour | **4 hours** |
| Undercover alcohol limit | **2 beverages** per tour |

## Approval Authorities

| Action | Authority Required |
|--------|-------------------|
| Tear gas use | **Patrol Borough Commander** (or designee) |
| Distraction device use | **Chief of Department** (or designee) |
| Return to permanent command after firearms discharge | **First Deputy Commissioner** |
| Non-standard vehicle checkpoint | **Deputy Commissioner, Legal Matters** (written) |
| Cancel ESU response at EDP | **Sergeant or above** |
| Canine use (non-authorized tactical) | **Borough Commander** |
| Canine at non-peaceful demonstration | **Chief of Patrol** |

## Key Forms

| Form | Used For |
|------|---------|
| **T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORT** | One per force incident — regardless of number of MOS/subjects |
| **T.R.I. INTERACTION REPORT** | Each involved MOS completes for every reportable force/injury |
| **PD324-140** (UMOS Report of Firearms Discharge) | All firearms discharges — within 48 hours |
| **SCOC M187** (Report of Inmate Death) | Death in custody — NYS Commission of Correction |
| **PD469-150** (Supervisor's Fitness for Duty Report) | Alcohol testing documentation |
| **VEHICLE CHECKPOINT FORM** | Document checkpoint operations |
| **Special Weapons Record** | Log for issuance/qualification/maintenance of special weapons |

## NYPD Duty to Intervene

All MOS **must** intervene to stop excessive force by MOS or other law enforcement. Failure to intervene or report = **serious misconduct** → criminal/civil liability + Department discipline up to **dismissal**.

---

# Section 221 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. Which of the following is a firearm prohibition under P.G. 221-01?**

- A) Discharging a firearm at a fleeing felon who presents an imminent threat
- B) Firing warning shots
- C) Discharging a firearm to protect from imminent physical injury
- D) Discharging a firearm in self-defense

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Firing warning shots**

Warning shots are prohibited. Members SHALL NOT fire warning shots under any circumstances. (P.G. 221-01)

</details>

---

**2. A CEW discharge in cartridge mode is classified as what level force incident?**

- A) Level 1
- B) Level 2
- C) Level 3
- D) Level 4

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**A) Level 1**

CEW discharge in cartridge mode is a Level 1 force incident. CEW in drive stun mode is Level 2. (P.G. 221-03)

</details>

---

**3. Who investigates Level 3 force incidents?**

- A) Immediate Supervisor
- B) Commanding Officer/Executive Officer
- C) Internal Affairs Bureau
- D) Force Investigation Division

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Internal Affairs Bureau**

Level 3 force incidents (force readily capable of causing death/serious injury, chokehold, excessive force with serious injury) are investigated by the Internal Affairs Bureau. (P.G. 221-03)

</details>

---

**4. The immediate supervisor in a force incident must be:**

- A) Any available supervisor from the same command
- B) A supervisor not involved in the incident, at least one rank higher than all involved members
- C) The desk officer of the precinct of occurrence
- D) The duty captain in all cases

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A supervisor not involved in the incident, at least one rank higher than all involved members**

The immediate supervisor must be available, NOT involved in the incident, from the same command, and at least one rank higher than all involved members. (P.G. 221-03)

</details>

---

**5. After a firearms discharge causing death or injury, the involved UMOS is temporarily assigned for a minimum of:**

- A) One scheduled tour
- B) Three consecutive scheduled tours
- C) Five consecutive scheduled tours
- D) Seven calendar days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Three consecutive scheduled tours**

The involved member is temporarily assigned to the patrol borough office for a minimum of three consecutive scheduled tours (exclusive of sick time or RDOs). (P.G. 221-04)

</details>

---

**6. O.C. pepper spray should be applied using:**

- A) One continuous burst at two feet
- B) Two, one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet
- C) Three short bursts at five feet
- D) One long burst at arm's length

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Two, one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet**

O.C. pepper spray is held upright, aimed at the subject's eyes, using two one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet. (P.G. 221-07)

</details>

---

**7. What is the recommended minimum "zone of safety" distance from an EDP?**

- A) 10 feet
- B) 15 feet
- C) 20 feet
- D) 25 feet

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 20 feet**

A minimum distance of 20 feet is recommended for the zone of safety. The distance should be greater than the effective range of the weapon (other than a firearm). (P.G. 221-13)

</details>

---

**8. The UMOS Report of Firearms Discharge (PD324-140) must be completed within:**

- A) 24 hours
- B) 48 hours
- C) 72 hours
- D) End of tour

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 48 hours**

The PD324-140 must be completed within 48 hours of the incident. (P.G. 221-04)

</details>

---

**9. Who has the authority to approve the use of tear gas?**

- A) Precinct commanding officer
- B) Patrol borough commander or designee
- C) Chief of Department
- D) Emergency Service Unit supervisor

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Patrol borough commander or designee**

The ultimate decision concerning use of tear gas rests with the patrol borough commander or designee. Technical decisions are made by the ranking ESU member. (P.G. 221-09)

</details>

---

**10. Members of the service have a duty to intervene to stop excessive force. Failure to intervene may result in:**

- A) A verbal reprimand
- B) Command discipline only
- C) Criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to dismissal
- D) Transfer to another command

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to dismissal**

Failure to intervene in or report excessive force is serious misconduct that may result in criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to and including dismissal. (P.G. 221-02)

</details>

---

**11. A "Resisting Recidivist" is defined as a prisoner arrested for charges against MOS:**

- A) Three or more times in ten years
- B) More than once within five years
- C) Twice within one year
- D) Any repeat offense regardless of timeframe

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) More than once within five years**

A Resisting Recidivist is a prisoner arrested for resisting arrest, assault on PO, or similar charges more than once within five years (i.e., the second time in five years). The detective squad must enhance the arrest. (P.G. 221-06)

</details>

---

**12. The use of distraction devices requires approval from:**

- A) Patrol borough commander
- B) Duty inspector
- C) Chief of Department or designee
- D) Chief of Patrol

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Chief of Department or designee**

Distraction devices require direct knowledge and specific approval of the Chief of Department (or designee), absent exigent circumstances. (P.G. 221-11)

</details>

---

**13. Who may apply a spit hood to an EDP?**

- A) Any uniformed member of the service
- B) The patrol supervisor
- C) Only Emergency Service Unit personnel
- D) EMS personnel only

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Only Emergency Service Unit personnel**

Only ESU personnel may apply a spit hood. Only ESU personnel or a medical professional with minimum EMT certification can remove it. (P.G. 221-13)

</details>

---

**14. CEWs should be carried in a holster on which side?**

- A) Shooting side, same as firearm
- B) Support side, opposite the firearm
- C) Either side at the member's preference
- D) Ankle holster

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Support side, opposite the firearm**

The CEW holster must be attached to the duty belt on the support side, opposite the firearm. Cross-drawing is prohibited. (P.G. 221-08)

</details>

---

**15. An EDP must NEVER be transported to:**

- A) The nearest hospital
- B) A police facility
- C) A psychiatric ward
- D) An ambulance

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A police facility**

Under no circumstances will an EDP be transported to a police facility. EDPs are removed to a hospital via ambulance (or RMP if ambulance unavailable, at supervisor's direction). (P.G. 221-13)

</details>

---

**16. At the scene of a firearms discharge, the PBT alcohol test threshold is:**

- A) .04
- B) .05
- C) .08
- D) .10

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) .08**

If the PBT reading is less than .08 and no indicia of intoxication, no further testing is required. At .08 or greater, an Intoxilyzer test at the IDTU facility is required. (P.G. 221-23)

</details>

---

**17. Which of the following is NOT an authorized use of O.C. pepper spray?**

- A) Gaining control of a person actively resisting arrest
- B) Preventing an individual from injuring themselves
- C) Controlling a subject who is passively resisting
- D) Controlling a dangerous animal

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Controlling a subject who is passively resisting**

O.C. pepper spray, CEW, and impact weapons should NOT be used on persons who are passively resisting. (P.G. 221-07)

</details>

---

**18. Vehicle checkpoints should NOT exceed what duration at one location?**

- A) 1 hour
- B) 2 hours
- C) 3 hours
- D) 4 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 2 hours**

A vehicle checkpoint should not exceed two hours at one location, regardless of conditions. (P.G. 221-16)

</details>

---

**19. Department canine teams shall NOT be used for:**

- A) Tracking missing persons
- B) Crowd control or deterrent at peaceful demonstrations
- C) Searching buildings for break-in suspects
- D) Performing cadaver searches

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Crowd control or deterrent at peaceful demonstrations**

Canine teams will not be used for crowd control or as a deterrent at peaceful demonstrations. Use at non-peaceful demonstrations requires prior approval of the Chief of Patrol. (P.G. 221-18)

</details>

---

**20. When a UMOS discharges a firearm outside of New York City, the Operations Unit must be notified within:**

- A) 1 hour
- B) 6 hours
- C) 12 hours
- D) 24 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 6 hours**

If a firearm is discharged outside of NYC, the member must notify local police authorities immediately and the Operations Unit within 6 hours of discharge. (P.G. 221-04)

</details>

---

**21. A CEW standard cycle is how many seconds?**

- A) 3 seconds
- B) 5 seconds
- C) 10 seconds
- D) 15 seconds

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 5 seconds**

Members should use a CEW for one standard cycle of five seconds while constantly assessing. Exposure beyond 15 seconds may increase the risk of death or serious injury. (P.G. 221-08)

</details>

---

**22. The response of Emergency Service Unit to an EDP scene may only be canceled by:**

- A) Any member of the service
- B) The desk officer
- C) A uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above
- D) The precinct commanding officer only

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) A uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above**

The response of ESU may only be canceled by a uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above. (P.G. 221-13)

</details>

---

**23. Members SHALL NOT discharge firearms at or from a moving vehicle unless:**

- A) The vehicle is being used in a pursuit
- B) The vehicle is stolen
- C) Deadly physical force is being used by means other than a moving vehicle
- D) The vehicle is approaching a checkpoint

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Deadly physical force is being used by means other than a moving vehicle**

Members SHALL NOT discharge firearms at or from a moving vehicle unless deadly physical force is being used against MOS or another person by means OTHER than a moving vehicle. (P.G. 221-01)

</details>

---

**24. How many T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORTS are prepared per force incident?**

- A) One per involved member
- B) One per subject
- C) Only ONE per incident, regardless of number of MOS or subjects
- D) One per force level

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Only ONE per incident, regardless of number of MOS or subjects**

Only one T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORT will be prepared for an incident, regardless of the number of MOS involved or number of subjects/civilians/bystanders involved. (P.G. 221-03)

</details>

---

**25. For Level 3 and Level 4 force incidents, the immediate supervisor should handle body-worn camera footage by:**

- A) Reviewing all videos and documenting findings
- B) Uploading footage to FORMS T.R.I. application
- C) Safeguarding cameras and NOT viewing — await IAB/FID direction
- D) Forwarding footage to the commanding officer

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Safeguarding cameras and NOT viewing — await IAB/FID direction**

Body-worn camera footage concerning Level 3 and Level 4 force incidents will NOT be viewed by the immediate supervisor. Immediate supervisors will safeguard the cameras and await direction from IAB or FID. (P.G. 221-03)

</details>

---

# Section 304 — General Regulations

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 304
- **Source PDF:** A.G. 304-04 Fitness For Duty.pdf
- **Priority:** High

## Learning Objectives
- Understand fitness for duty requirements and alcohol-related misconduct consequences
- Know the mandatory termination triggers for firearm misuse while intoxicated
- Recognize the "Unfit For Duty While Armed" charge and Dismissal Probation requirements
- Know the three counseling resources: HELPLINE, POPPA, Counseling Services Unit

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 304-04 | Fitness For Duty | completed |
| A.G. 304-11 | (Not available) | blocked |
| A.G. 304-15 | (Not available) | blocked |
| A.G. 304-25 | (Not available) | blocked |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-304-fitness-for-duty.md` | A.G. 304-04: Fitness for duty, alcohol misconduct, termination triggers, counseling programs |
| `section-304-computer-use-policy.md` | A.G. 325-35: Department computer use policy, monitoring notice, prohibited uses |
| `key-terms.md` | Definitions, disciplinary consequences, counseling protections |
| `review-questions.md` | 8 multiple-choice questions covering fitness for duty concepts |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **Mandatory termination:** Firearm misuse while unfit due to alcohol; serious physical injury via motor vehicle while intoxicated
- **Counseling referral:** Know HELPLINE, POPPA, and Counseling Services Unit pathways
- **Dismissal Probation:** Required in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents
- **Counseling protections:** Participation does NOT jeopardize promotions; does NOT exempt from discipline for specific misconduct
- **Chemical testing refusal:** Charged under A.G. 304-06 "Prohibited Conduct"

---

# Section 304 — Department Computer Use Policy (A.G. 325-35)

## A.G. 325-35: Department Computer Use Policy and Monitoring Notice

**Date Effective:** 02/04/15 | **Pages:** 1

> **Note:** This procedure is from A.G. Section 325 (Department Property) but is included here with General Regulations due to its conduct/policy nature.

### Purpose

To inform members of the service of the Department computer use policy when accessing computer systems, and to notify all members that the Department reserves and will exercise the right to review, intercept, access, and disclose all material in the Department's computer systems at any time, with or without notice.

### Procedure

| Role | Step | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Commanding Officer | 1 | Inform member concerned of the Department Computer Use Policy and Monitoring Notice |
| Member | 2 | Review and comply with contents of the Policy and Monitoring Notice |

### Department Computer Use Policy — Key Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| All computer systems and equipment are intended for **official Department business only** |
| Use for **personal or non-Department business** is strictly prohibited — subject to disciplinary action |
| Use to disseminate **derogatory or discriminatory material** is strictly prohibited |
| Department reserves and will exercise the right to **review, intercept, access, and disclose** all material at any time, **with or without notice** |
| Access may occur **during or after** the user's assigned tours |

### Related Procedures

| Procedure | Title |
|-----------|-------|
| A.G. 322-04 | FINEST Communication System |
| P.G. 219-14 | Department Computer Systems |

> **Exam Alert:** Department computers are for **official business only**. The Department may review, intercept, access, and disclose all material **at any time, with or without notice** — including during or after tours.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Ensure members are informed of this policy. Personal use and dissemination of derogatory/discriminatory material are strictly prohibited and subject to disciplinary action.

---

# Section 304 — Fitness For Duty

## A.G. 304-04: Fitness For Duty

**Date Effective:** 04/21/22 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 45 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To establish fitness for duty requirements for all members of the service.

### Core Requirements

| Requirement |
|-------------|
| Be fit for duty at all times, except when on sick report |
| Do not consume intoxicants to the extent that member becomes unfit for duty |
| Members are reminded of absolute responsibility to remain fit for duty while in possession of firearms |

### Firearm Misconduct — Mandatory Termination

| Violation | Consequence |
|-----------|-------------|
| Misuse of firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption/intoxication | **Termination** from the Department |
| Exceptional cases | Determined by **Police Commissioner** on case-by-case basis |

### "Unfit For Duty While Armed" Charge

| Rule |
|------|
| Any misconduct involving member found unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption while armed with a firearm |
| Results in inclusion of charge **"Unfit For Duty While Armed"** in disciplinary proceedings |
| Strict punitive sanctions imposed upon substantiation — in addition to all other charges |

### Refusal of Chemical Testing

| Scenario | Consequence |
|----------|-------------|
| UMOS refuses to submit to chemical testing for alleged violation of VTL §1192 (DWI) | Charged with violating A.G. 304-06 "Prohibited Conduct" — engaging in conduct prejudicial to good order, efficiency, or discipline |

### Motor Vehicle Policy

| Scenario | Consequence |
|----------|-------------|
| UMOS causes **serious physical injury** while operating motor vehicle AND determined unfit for duty due to alcohol | **Terminated**, absent exigent circumstances |
| Any negotiated penalty for operating motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol | Must include period of **Dismissal Probation** |
| Ordered breath testing during probation — member found unfit or refuses | Additional disciplinary action, may include **termination** |

### Counseling and Assistance Programs

| Program | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **HELPLINE** | Confidential assistance for alcohol-related problems |
| **POPPA** (Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance) | Joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program |
| **Counseling Services Unit** | 24 hours/day, 7 days/week; assessment and referrals for treatment |

### Counseling Services Unit Protections

| Protection |
|-----------|
| Maintains strict confidentiality under Title 42 Federal guidelines |
| Does NOT act as part of Department's disciplinary system |
| Participation will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities |
| Current assignments will NOT ordinarily be changed due to participation |
| Participation does NOT exempt member from disciplinary action for specific acts of misconduct |

> **Exam Alert:** Misuse of firearm while unfit for duty due to alcohol = **mandatory termination** (exceptional cases by Police Commissioner only). Causing serious physical injury while driving + alcohol = **termination** absent exigent circumstances.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Supervisors should be familiar with HELPLINE, POPPA, and Counseling Services Unit referral pathways. Early detection and referral is the Department's objective. Participation in counseling does NOT jeopardize promotions.

---

# Section 304 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Fitness For Duty

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Fitness For Duty** | Being fit for duty at all times, except when on sick report; not consuming intoxicants to the extent of becoming unfit |
| **Unfit For Duty While Armed** | Disciplinary charge for misconduct involving member found unfit due to alcohol while armed with a firearm |
| **Dismissal Probation** | Period included in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents |

## Counseling Programs

| Program | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **HELPLINE** | Confidential assistance for alcohol-related problems |
| **POPPA** | Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance — joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance |
| **Counseling Services Unit** | 24/7 assessment and referral; maintains Title 42 Federal confidentiality; not part of disciplinary system |

## Disciplinary Consequences

| Violation | Consequence |
|-----------|-------------|
| Firearm misuse while unfit (alcohol) | **Termination** (exceptional cases: Police Commissioner) |
| Serious physical injury via motor vehicle + alcohol | **Termination** absent exigent circumstances |
| Refusal of chemical testing (VTL §1192) | Charged under A.G. 304-06 "Prohibited Conduct" |
| Found unfit or refuses breath testing during probation | Additional discipline, may include termination |

## Counseling Protections

| Protection | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| Confidentiality | Title 42 Federal guidelines |
| Promotions | Participation does NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities |
| Assignments | Current assignments NOT ordinarily changed |
| Discipline | Participation does NOT exempt from discipline for specific misconduct |
| Availability | Services available to members, families, and retirees |

---

# Section 304 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. A uniformed member of the service who misuses a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption will be:**

- A) Placed on modified assignment
- B) Suspended for 30 days
- C) Terminated from the Department
- D) Given command discipline

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Terminated from the Department**

Any misconduct involving a member's misuse of a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive consumption of and intoxication from alcohol will result in termination. Exceptional cases are determined by the Police Commissioner on a case-by-case basis. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**2. A UMOS causes serious physical injury to another person while operating a motor vehicle and is determined to be unfit for duty due to alcohol. What is the consequence?**

- A) Dismissal Probation only
- B) Suspension and retraining
- C) Termination, absent exigent circumstances
- D) Modified assignment for 30 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Termination, absent exigent circumstances**

The Department policy states that any UMOS who causes serious physical injury while operating a motor vehicle and is determined unfit for duty due to alcohol consumption will be terminated, absent exigent circumstances. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**3. A UMOS refuses to submit to chemical testing in connection with an alleged violation of VTL §1192 (DWI). The member will be charged with violating:**

- A) A.G. 304-04, "Fitness For Duty"
- B) A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct"
- C) A.G. 318-10, "Removal of Firearms"
- D) P.G. 221-23, "Alcohol Testing"

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct"**

A UMOS who refuses chemical testing for alleged DWI will be charged with violating A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct" — engaging in conduct prejudicial to the good order, efficiency, or discipline of the Department. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**4. The Counseling Services Unit maintains confidentiality standards under:**

- A) New York State Civil Service Law
- B) Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs
- C) Department Administrative Guide only
- D) HIPAA regulations

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs**

The Counseling Services Unit maintains strict standards of confidentiality as set forth in Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**5. A member participating in counseling programs for alcohol-related problems should be aware that:**

- A) Their promotional opportunities will be jeopardized
- B) They will automatically be transferred
- C) Participation will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities
- D) They will be placed on restricted duty

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Participation will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities**

Members participating in these programs will not jeopardize their promotional opportunities, and current assignments will not ordinarily be changed. However, participation does not exempt a member from disciplinary action for specific acts of misconduct. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**6. Any negotiated penalty for a UMOS determined to have been operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include:**

- A) A verbal reprimand
- B) A period of Dismissal Probation
- C) A 10-day suspension
- D) Command discipline only

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A period of Dismissal Probation**

Any negotiated penalty involving a UMOS operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include a period of Dismissal Probation. The member must also agree to submit to ordered breath testing during the probation period. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**7. POPPA is best described as:**

- A) A Department disciplinary unit
- B) A joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program
- C) A civilian oversight board
- D) An Internal Affairs Bureau division

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program**

POPPA (Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance) is a joint labor-management cooperative effort involving the implementation of a union-operated, alternative employee assistance program. Both HELPLINE and POPPA offer confidential services. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

**8. The Counseling Services Unit operates:**

- A) Monday through Friday, 0900-1700 hours
- B) 24 hours a day, seven days a week
- C) Only during business hours
- D) By appointment only

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 24 hours a day, seven days a week**

The Counseling Services Unit operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and will help assess the extent of the problem and make appropriate referrals for treatment. (A.G. 304-04)

</details>

---

# Section 305 — Uniforms and Equipment

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 305
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 305-05, A.G. 305-07
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know reimbursement procedures for lost or damaged uniforms and personal clothing
- Understand firearms carrying requirements — when armed/unarmed, holster requirements
- Identify prohibited holster types and carrying methods
- Know firearm modification, storage, and court entry rules

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 305-05 | Lost or Damaged Uniform | completed |
| A.G. 305-07 | Firearms General Regulations | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-305-lost-damaged-uniform.md` | A.G. 305-05: Reimbursement procedures, filing deadlines, reimbursement limits |
| `section-305-firearms-regulations.md` | A.G. 305-07: Carrying requirements, holster rules, prohibited modifications, court rules, foreign travel |

| `review-questions.md` | 10 multiple-choice questions covering uniforms and firearms regulations |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **Holster compliance:** Verify retention device, trigger guard coverage; no clip-on/belly band
- **Modification rules:** Only Firearms and Tactics Section armorers; qualification required after modification
- **Reimbursement:** 30-day filing deadline; direct forwarding for < $100, through channels for ≥ $100
- **Unarmed exceptions:** Know the 5 off-duty circumstances allowing member to be unarmed
- **Court entry:** Members unarmed at Family Court/Supreme Court Matrimonial unless arresting officer

---

# Section 305 — Firearms General Regulations

## A.G. 305-07: Firearms General Regulations

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 110 | **Pages:** 3

### Definition

**AUTHORIZED FIREARM** — Service pistols and off-duty pistols/revolvers that conform to specifications and standards set forth by the Firearms and Tactics Section, Police Academy. The Firearms and Tactics Section is custodian of all authorized on-duty and off-duty firearm parameters.

### Member Duties — Carrying Requirements

| Step | Duty |
|------|------|
| 1 | **Safeguard** firearms at all times |
| 2 | Be **armed at all times** when in NYC with service pistol or off-duty pistol/revolver, unless otherwise directed |
| 3 | Carry **only authorized firearms** when on duty |
| 3a | Off-duty pistol/revolver may be carried **in addition to** service pistol while on duty, provided only the service pistol is visible |
| 3b | CO permission required before carrying **special weapons** while performing duty |
| 4 | Ensure service/off-duty firearms are **fully loaded** with Department-issued ammunition when carried |

### When Member May Be Unarmed (Off Duty)

| Circumstance |
|-------------|
| Possession would create unnecessary risk of loss/theft (sporting activities, beach, pool) |
| On **vacation** |
| Engaged in authorized **off-duty employment** |
| Activity where it would be advisable NOT to carry |
| Likelihood that member will be **consuming alcohol** |

### Holster Requirements

| Requirement |
|------------|
| Must affix tightly to belt (or body for shoulder/ankle holster) with reliable retention device other than friction alone |
| Must cover trigger guard, muzzle, and front sight |
| Inside-waistband holster must have **reinforced mouth** for one-handed re-holstering |

### Prohibited Holsters and Carrying Methods

| Prohibited |
|-----------|
| "One size fits all" holsters |
| "Clip-on" holsters |
| "Belly band" holsters |
| Carrying firearms in briefcases, handbags, fanny packs, hip packs, tote bags, knapsacks, paper bags, or similar devices |

### Firearm Storage and Maintenance

| Rule |
|------|
| Do NOT store or leave firearms in **unattended motor vehicle** (except as authorized, e.g., P.G. 221-10) |
| Transport firearms other than authorized service/off-duty firearms **only** en route to/from practice range; must be armed with authorized firearm and other firearms must be **unloaded** |
| All repairs/modifications performed **solely by Firearms and Tactics Section armorers** |
| Record permitted modifications on **FORCE RECORD (PD406-143)** |
| Pass qualification course of fire before carrying modified firearm |
| Green **"Tritium" night sights** required on service pistols (optional for pre-9/12/94 members and off-duty pistols) |
| Record all revolvers and pistols on **FORCE RECORD** |
| Utilize **firearms safety station** when loading/unloading |

### Prohibited Modifications

All aftermarket/non-Department parts are prohibited, including: extended magazine releases, extended slide stop levers, magazines, sights, flared magazine wells. Also prohibited: polishing that alters the firearm, machining any part, attaching anything (slip-on grips, grip plugs, accessory rail attachments, rubber bands, belt clips), and engraving, adding decals, painting, or etching.

### Court Requirements

| Rule |
|------|
| Be **unarmed** when entering Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial Parts (unless arresting officer or on official business) |
| Safeguard firearms at location other than the court, OR deliver to desk officer of precinct where court is located |
| If present as arresting officer — report to court officer supervisor and sign Law Enforcement Officer log |

### Foreign Country Travel

| Rule |
|------|
| **Prohibited** from carrying on person, in luggage, or by other conveyance, any firearm into a **foreign country** (unless on official business or personally participating in sporting event requiring firearm) |
| Must comply with all applicable foreign country firearms rules/regulations |
| Failure to comply = disciplinary action and possible criminal penalties/weapon confiscation |

> **Exam Alert:** Members must be armed at all times in NYC unless specific off-duty exceptions apply. Firearms must NEVER be left in an **unattended motor vehicle**. All modifications must be performed by **Firearms and Tactics Section armorers** only.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Verify holster compliance (retention device, trigger guard coverage, no clip-on/belly band). Ensure members have passed qualification before carrying modified firearms. Know the Family Court/Supreme Court unarmed requirement.

---

# Section 305 — Lost or Damaged Uniform

## A.G. 305-05: Lost or Damaged Uniform

**Date Effective:** 06/24/21 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 63 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To reimburse uniformed members of the service for uniforms or equipment damaged or lost in the performance of police duty, as well as personal clothing or prescription eyeglasses/contact lenses damaged or lost during extraordinary police action (e.g., pursuit, physical altercation, rescues).

### Member Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Prepare report on **Typed Letterhead** addressed to Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget, within **30 days** from incident |
| 1a | State how/when loss or damage occurred |
| 1b | State date of purchase and cost of article; attach receipt if available |
| 2 | Attach statement of witnesses, if any |
| 3 | Ascertain if article can be repaired; obtain estimated cost for repair OR statement that item is damaged beyond repair |
| 4 | Present damaged article, **unrepaired**, to commanding officer with report and documentation |

### Note on Incapacitated Members

Report must be signed and submitted within **30 days** by member requesting reimbursement. If member is incapacitated, supervisory officer will state so in report, sign, and submit within 30 days.

### Commanding Officer Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 5 | Investigate and endorse report: facts as stated, damage/loss in performance of duty, article inspected, repairable/beyond repair, authorized civilian attire (if applicable), recommendation to approve/disapprove |
| 6 | Forward report to Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget: **Direct** if cost < $100; **Through channels** if cost ≥ $100 |

### Reimbursement Limits

| Item | Maximum |
|------|---------|
| Watch | $75.00 |
| Prescription eyeglasses/contact lenses | $100.00 |
| Jacket/Coat | $100.00 |
| Suit Jacket | $100.00 |
| Dress | $100.00 |
| Footwear | $75.00 |
| Dress pants | $75.00 |
| Skirt | $50.00 |
| Other pants (jeans, shorts) | $45.00 |
| Dress shirt/blouse | $40.00 |
| Other shirts (sweatshirt, t-shirt) | $20.00 |
| Other | As determined by Audits and Accounts Unit |

### Key Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| Department does NOT reimburse for personal articles (jewelry, rings, bracelets) except **watches** |
| All settlements are **non-transferable** and must be executed within **180 days** of approval |
| Personal clothing claims only for members authorized to perform duty in civilian clothes during **extraordinary police action** |

> **Exam Alert:** Report must be filed within **30 days**. Settlements must be executed within **180 days** of approval. Direct forwarding for costs under **$100**; through channels for $100 or more.

> **Sergeant Focus:** If member is incapacitated, the supervisory officer must sign and submit the report within 30 days. Verify that damaged article is presented unrepaired and that the member was performing police duty (or extraordinary police action for civilian clothing).

---

# Section 305 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Firearms Terminology

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Authorized Firearm** | Service pistols and off-duty pistols/revolvers conforming to Firearms and Tactics Section specifications |
| **Service Pistol** | Primary on-duty firearm |
| **Off-Duty Pistol/Revolver** | Authorized secondary firearm; may be carried in addition to service pistol on duty (not visible) |
| **Special Weapons** | Require CO permission before carrying on duty (see A.G. 305-11) |
| **FORCE RECORD (PD406-143)** | Record of all revolvers/pistols owned, permitted modifications |
| **Tritium Night Sights** | Green night sights required on service pistols (optional for pre-9/12/94 members and off-duty pistols) |
| **Firearms Safety Station** | Used when loading/unloading firearms |

## Holster Requirements

| Requirement | Detail |
|------------|--------|
| Retention | Reliable retention device other than friction alone |
| Coverage | Must cover trigger guard, muzzle, and front sight |
| Inside-waistband | Must have reinforced mouth for one-handed re-holstering |
| Prohibited types | "One size fits all," "clip-on," "belly band" |

## Prohibited Modifications

All aftermarket/non-Department parts; polishing that alters firearm; machining; attaching anything (slip-on grips, grip plugs, rail attachments, belt clips); engraving, decals, painting, etching.

## Uniform Reimbursement

| Term | Detail |
|------|--------|
| **Eligible items** | Uniforms/equipment damaged/lost in performance of duty; personal clothing/eyeglasses during extraordinary police action |
| **Filing deadline** | 30 days from incident |
| **Settlement deadline** | 180 days from approval |
| **Direct forwarding** | Cost < $100 |
| **Through channels** | Cost ≥ $100 |
| **Personal articles NOT reimbursed** | Jewelry, rings, bracelets (watches excepted at $75 max) |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Report for lost/damaged uniform | Within **30 days** |
| Settlement execution | Within **180 days** of approval |

---

# Section 305 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. A UMOS may be unarmed at their own discretion while off duty in all of the following situations EXCEPT:**

- A) On vacation
- B) Engaged in authorized off-duty employment
- C) Walking through a NYC neighborhood
- D) There is a likelihood of consuming alcohol

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Walking through a NYC neighborhood**

Members must be armed at all times when in NYC, unless specific exceptions apply: risk of loss/theft (sporting activities, beach), vacation, authorized off-duty employment, activity where it would be inadvisable to carry, or likelihood of consuming alcohol. Walking through NYC does not qualify. (A.G. 305-07)

</details>

---

**2. Which type of holster is prohibited under A.G. 305-07?**

- A) Shoulder holster
- B) Ankle holster
- C) Belly band holster
- D) Inside-waistband holster

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Belly band holster**

"Belly band" holsters, "clip-on" holsters, and "one size fits all" holsters are prohibited. Shoulder and ankle holsters are permitted if they meet retention requirements. (A.G. 305-07)

</details>

---

**3. Firearms may NOT be stored or left in:**

- A) A locked firearms safe at home
- B) An unattended motor vehicle
- C) The desk officer's safekeeping
- D) A firearms safety station

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) An unattended motor vehicle**

Members shall not store or leave firearms in an unattended motor vehicle, except as authorized (e.g., P.G. 221-10). (A.G. 305-07)

</details>

---

**4. All firearm repairs and modifications must be performed by:**

- A) Any certified gunsmith
- B) The member's commanding officer
- C) Firearms and Tactics Section armorers solely
- D) The member with CO approval

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Firearms and Tactics Section armorers solely**

All repairs and modifications to authorized firearms must be performed solely by Firearms and Tactics Section armorers. The member must pass a qualification course before carrying a modified firearm. (A.G. 305-07)

</details>

---

**5. A report for lost or damaged uniform must be filed within:**

- A) 10 days
- B) 30 days
- C) 60 days
- D) 90 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 30 days**

The report must be prepared on Typed Letterhead addressed to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget, within 30 days from the date of incident. (A.G. 305-05)

</details>

---

**6. Reimbursement for lost or damaged uniform items costing less than $100 should be forwarded to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget:**

- A) Through channels
- B) Direct
- C) Via Internal Affairs Bureau
- D) Through the Chief of Department

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Direct**

Items costing less than $100 are forwarded direct to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. Items costing $100 or more go through channels. (A.G. 305-05)

</details>

---

**7. The maximum reimbursement amount for a lost or damaged watch is:**

- A) $50.00
- B) $75.00
- C) $100.00
- D) $150.00

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) $75.00**

A maximum of $75.00 has been established as a reasonable amount for watch reimbursement. Members are reminded they must be equipped with a serviceable watch while performing patrol duty. (A.G. 305-05)

</details>

---

**8. A UMOS entering Family Court must be:**

- A) Armed with service pistol only
- B) Unarmed, unless present as arresting officer or on official business
- C) Armed with both service and off-duty weapons
- D) Armed but with weapon concealed

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Unarmed, unless present as arresting officer or on official business**

Members must be unarmed when entering Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial Parts, unless involved as arresting officer or on official business in a case. If armed as arresting officer, report to court officer supervisor and sign the Law Enforcement Officer log. (A.G. 305-07)

</details>

---

**9. All settlements for lost or damaged uniform reimbursement are non-transferable and must be executed within:**

- A) 90 days of approval
- B) 120 days of approval
- C) 180 days of approval
- D) One year of approval

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 180 days of approval**

All settlements are non-transferable and MUST be executed within 180 days of approval by the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. (A.G. 305-05)

</details>

---

**10. A UMOS carrying an authorized firearm into a foreign country (not on official business or sporting event) will face:**

- A) A verbal warning
- B) No consequences if firearm is properly secured
- C) Disciplinary action and possibly criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation
- D) Modified assignment

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Disciplinary action and possibly criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation**

Members are prohibited from carrying firearms into a foreign country unless on official business or personally participating in an event requiring use of a firearm. Failure to comply will result in disciplinary action and possibly subject the member to criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation. (A.G. 305-07)

</details>

---

# Section 318 — Disciplinary Matters

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 318
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 318-10, A.G. 318-11
- **Priority:** High

## Learning Objectives
- Know the procedure for removing firearms from an intoxicated UMOS
- Understand the definition of intoxication and "common sense" determination standard
- Master interrogation rights and protections for members under investigation
- Know the Garrity warning elements and their implications
- Understand prohibited interrogation tactics and scheduling rules

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 318-10 | Removal of Firearms from Intoxicated UMOS | completed |
| A.G. 318-11 | Interrogation of Members of the Service | completed |
| A.G. 318-06 | (Not available) | blocked |
| A.G. 318-07 | (Not available) | blocked |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-318-removal-firearms-intoxicated.md` | A.G. 318-10: PD469-150 preparation, investigation standard, firearms removal, report distribution (8 copies), mandatory counseling referral |
| `section-318-interrogation-mos.md` | A.G. 318-11: PD339-121, counsel rights, BWC viewing, prohibited tactics, Garrity warning, tape/transcript deadlines, member obligations |
| `key-terms.md` | Intoxication definition, key forms, interrogation rights, Garrity elements, prohibited tactics, timeframes |
| `review-questions.md` | 12 multiple-choice questions covering firearms removal and interrogation procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **PD469-150:** Both the referring supervisor AND CO/XO/duty captain prepare their own reports immediately
- **Determination standard:** Totality of circumstances, common sense, consider time elapsed
- **Mandatory actions:** Remove firearms, modified assignment/suspension, contact Counseling Services Unit (mandated)
- **IAB notification:** Obtain log number from IAB Command Center
- **Interrogation:** PD339-121 before questioning; no "off the record" questions; captain+ determines reasonable time for counsel
- **Garrity protection:** Statements cannot be used criminally but CAN be used departmentally

---

# Section 318 — Interrogation of Members of the Service

## A.G. 318-11: Interrogation of Members of the Service

**Date Effective:** 01/01/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 2 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To protect the rights of the member of the service (uniformed or civilian) in an official Department investigation.

### Pre-Questioning Requirements

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Provide member completed **NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)** for review and endorsement |
| 1a | Allow **reasonable amount of time** for review prior to endorsement |
| 2 | Permit member to obtain **counsel** if: (a) serious violation alleged, OR (b) sufficient justification presented even if violation is minor |

### Member Rights During Investigation

| Right |
|-------|
| Reasonable period of time to obtain and confer with counsel prior to questioning |
| Permitted to view their own **Body-Worn Camera recording** related to incident(s) under investigation prior to questioning |
| Representative of department **line organization** present at all times during interrogation |

### Interrogation Scheduling

| Rule |
|------|
| Routine, non-critical matters: schedule during **business hours** on a day when member is scheduled to work |
| Emerging investigations: conduct after all preliminary steps, conferrals completed, and member afforded reasonable time for counsel |
| Determination of "reasonable time" made by **captain (or above)** in charge of investigation |

### Interrogating Officer Must Inform Member Of

| Information |
|------------|
| Rank, name, and command of person in charge of investigation |
| Rank, name, and command of interrogating officer |
| Identity of all persons present |
| Whether member is **subject or witness** in the investigation |
| Nature of accusation |
| Identities of witnesses/complainants (address need not be revealed) — except confidential source or field associate (unless they witnessed the incident) |
| Information concerning all allegations |
| Department's policy regarding **false, misleading, and inaccurate statements** (A.G. 304-10) |

### Prohibited Interrogation Tactics

| SHALL NOT |
|-----------|
| "Off the record" questions |
| Offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, or other disciplinary punishment) |
| Promises of reward for answering questions |

### Interrogation Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| Must be **recorded** (mechanically or by Department stenographer) |
| Department Advocate determines if transcript required in non-criminal/minor violation cases |
| Regulate duration with breaks for **meal, personal necessity, telephone call** |
| Record all recesses |
| Assign member to **2nd Platoon** if possible (CO of member) |

### Charges and Specifications Timing

| Rule |
|------|
| Interrogations may be conducted before or after CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS (PD468-121) served |
| Post-service interrogation must be completed at least **10 days** prior to Department trial date |
| Exception: as directed by Deputy Commissioner - Trials |

### Tape/Transcript Requirements (Department Advocate)

| Item | Deadline |
|------|----------|
| Copy of tape (interrogation before charges) | No later than **20 days** after service of charges |
| Copy of tape (interrogation after charges) | No later than **5 days** after interrogation |
| Transcript (if prepared) | By **1000 hours** on trial date, in all cases |

### Member Obligations

| Duty |
|------|
| Answer questions **specifically directed and narrowly related** to official duties |
| Refusal = **suspension from duty** |
| Submit OVERTIME REPORT (PD138-064) if lost time accrues |

### Criminal Investigation Warning (Garrity Warning)

When member is under arrest, subject of criminal investigation, or criminal charges may result, the following warning must be given:

**Key points of the warning:**
- Member is being questioned as part of official investigation
- Entitled to all rights under NY State law, State Constitution, and US Constitution
- Right not to be compelled to incriminate yourself
- Right to legal counsel at each stage
- If member refuses to testify on questions related to official duties → subject to **departmental charges → possible dismissal**
- If member answers → statements cannot be used in **criminal proceedings** but CAN be used in **departmental charges**

### Confidentiality

| Rule |
|------|
| Questions and answers from interrogation are **confidential** |
| Not to be revealed or released outside Department without **written approval of Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters** |
| If subpoena duces tecum received → contact **Legal Bureau** immediately |

### Return to Full Duty

Members (uniformed and civilian) whose actions result in **death or serious physical injury** of a member of the public may only return to full-duty status pending a Department **administrative review**.

> **Exam Alert:** Member must be provided **PD339-121** before questioning. Counsel permitted for serious violations. Refusal to answer questions related to official duties = **suspension**. Statements can be used for **departmental charges** but NOT **criminal proceedings** (Garrity protection).

> **Sergeant Focus:** Know the 8 items the interrogating officer must inform the member about. No "off the record" questions, threats, or reward promises. Interrogation must be recorded. Post-charge interrogation must be completed **10 days** before trial. Assign member to **2nd Platoon**.

---

# Section 318 — Removal of Firearms from Intoxicated UMOS

## A.G. 318-10: Removal of Firearms from Intoxicated Uniformed Member of the Service

**Date Effective:** 09/06/22 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 93 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To determine if an on/off duty uniformed member of the service is unfit for duty due to intoxication.

### Definition

**INTOXICATION** — Unfitness for duty due to the influence of alcohol, narcotics, or other drug, or under circumstances in which surrounding events of a timely nature indicate the member may have been intoxicated during an earlier period directly related to the incident in question.

### Supervisory Member Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Direct that member remain at Department facility or other location pending arrival of CO/duty captain |
| 2 | Prepare, **immediately**, SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150) based upon observations |
| 3 | Notify CO/XO/duty captain to respond |

### CO/XO/Duty Captain Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 4 | Prepare, **immediately**, SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT based upon observations |
| 5 | Conduct investigation to determine if member is unfit for duty due to intoxication at time of alleged misconduct |
| 6 | **Remove firearms** when determined that member is intoxicated (see A.G. 318-13, "Removal and Restoration of Firearms") |
| 7 | Place member on **modified assignment** or **suspend from duty**, as appropriate |
| 8 | Advise member of availability of Counseling Service Programs |
| 9 | Have supervisory officer contact **Counseling Services Unit DIRECT** during normal business hours; at other times, request conferral through Sick Desk supervisor |
| 10 | Notify **Internal Affairs Bureau Command Center** and obtain a log number |

### Determination Standard

| Criteria |
|----------|
| Apply **common sense standards** to determine fitness for duty |
| Examine **totality of the circumstances** |
| Consider all credible relevant information |
| Consider: all SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORTS, witness statements (civilian and MOS), available scientific evidence (Breathalyzer, blood test, etc.) |
| Factor in **time elapsed** since alleged misconduct or first supervisory observation |

### Reporting Requirements

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 11 | Prepare **five copies** of all completed SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORTS (CO/XO/duty captain's and referring supervisor's) |
| 12 | Prepare **eight copies** of report on Typed Letterhead detailing observations/circumstances, with copies of all FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORTS, forward to: |

### Distribution of Reports (8 Copies)

| Recipient | Method |
|-----------|--------|
| First Deputy Commissioner | Original (**DIRECT**) |
| First Deputy Commissioner | Through channels |
| Chief of Department | **DIRECT** |
| Chief of Personnel | **DIRECT** |
| Chief of Internal Affairs | **DIRECT** |
| Deputy Commissioner, Trials | **DIRECT** |
| Department Advocate's Office | **DIRECT** |
| Member's commanding officer | **DIRECT** |

### Mandatory Counseling Referral

Prior to final adjudication of a disciplinary matter, in **all misconduct cases in which the use of alcohol is indicated**:
- Conferral with **Employee Assistance Unit** must be made
- Assessment and/or referral by **Counseling Services Unit** must be conducted
- Department Advocate's Office will ensure these steps are taken

### Note on Counseling Services

A supervisory officer is **mandated** in all cases to contact the Counseling Services Unit on behalf of a member who is placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed due to being unfit for duty. Counseling Service Program services are **NOT available** for illegal drug use/abuse problems.

> **Exam Alert:** Supervisory member prepares PD469-150 **immediately**. CO/XO/Duty Captain also prepares their own PD469-150. Firearms are removed per A.G. 318-13. **Eight copies** of the Typed Letterhead report are distributed to eight recipients.

> **Sergeant Focus:** The first supervisor on scene must (1) direct member to remain, (2) prepare PD469-150 immediately, (3) notify CO/XO/duty captain. Supervisory officer is **mandated** to contact Counseling Services Unit. IAB Command Center must be notified for log number.

---

# Section 318 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Intoxication

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Intoxication** | Unfitness for duty due to influence of alcohol, narcotics, or other drug, or circumstances indicating member may have been intoxicated during earlier period related to the incident |

## Key Forms

| Form | Used For |
|------|---------|
| **SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150)** | Prepared by both referring supervisor AND CO/XO/duty captain based on observations |
| **NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)** | Provided to member prior to interrogation for review and endorsement |
| **CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS (PD468-121)** | Formal disciplinary charges served on member |

## Interrogation Rights

| Right | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **PD339-121** | Must be provided before questioning |
| **Counsel** | Permitted for serious violations or sufficient justification |
| **BWC Review** | Member may view own BWC recording related to incident prior to questioning |
| **Line Organization** | Representative present at all times during interrogation |
| **Reasonable Time** | Captain or above determines what constitutes "reasonable time" for counsel |

## Garrity Warning Elements

| Element |
|---------|
| Official investigation by Police Department |
| Questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties |
| All rights under NY State law, State Constitution, US Constitution |
| Right not to be compelled to incriminate yourself |
| Right to legal counsel at each stage |
| Refusal to testify on official duties = departmental charges → possible dismissal |
| Statements CANNOT be used in criminal proceedings |
| Statements CAN be used in departmental charges |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Post-charge interrogation completion | At least **10 days** before Department trial |
| Tape provided after charge service | Within **20 days** |
| Tape provided after post-charge interrogation | Within **5 days** |
| Transcript to member | By **1000 hours** on trial date |

## Report Distribution — Intoxicated UMOS (8 Copies)

| Recipient | Method |
|-----------|--------|
| First Deputy Commissioner | Original (DIRECT) + Through channels |
| Chief of Department | DIRECT |
| Chief of Personnel | DIRECT |
| Chief of Internal Affairs | DIRECT |
| Deputy Commissioner, Trials | DIRECT |
| Department Advocate's Office | DIRECT |
| Member's CO | DIRECT |

## Prohibited Interrogation Tactics

| Prohibited |
|-----------|
| "Off the record" questions |
| Offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, disciplinary punishment) |
| Promises of reward for answering questions |

---

# Section 318 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. Upon observing a UMOS who appears unfit for duty due to intoxication, the supervisory member must prepare the SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150):**

- A) Within 24 hours
- B) By end of tour
- C) Immediately
- D) Within 48 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Immediately**

The supervisory member must prepare the PD469-150 immediately based upon observations of the member. The CO/XO/duty captain must also prepare their own PD469-150 immediately. (A.G. 318-10)

</details>

---

**2. When a UMOS is determined to be intoxicated, the CO/XO/duty captain should:**

- A) Issue a verbal warning and send member home
- B) Remove firearms, place on modified assignment or suspend, and advise of counseling
- C) Notify Internal Affairs Bureau only
- D) Contact the Police Commissioner directly

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Remove firearms, place on modified assignment or suspend, and advise of counseling**

The CO/XO/duty captain removes firearms (per A.G. 318-13), places member on modified assignment or suspends, advises of Counseling Service Programs, and mandates supervisory officer contact to Counseling Services Unit. (A.G. 318-10)

</details>

---

**3. The Typed Letterhead report for an intoxicated UMOS must be prepared in how many copies and forwarded to how many recipients?**

- A) Five copies, five recipients
- B) Six copies, six recipients
- C) Eight copies, eight recipients
- D) Ten copies, ten recipients

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Eight copies, eight recipients**

Eight copies are prepared and forwarded to: First Deputy Commissioner (original, DIRECT + through channels), Chief of Department, Chief of Personnel, Chief of Internal Affairs, Deputy Commissioner Trials, Department Advocate's Office, and member's CO — all DIRECT. (A.G. 318-10)

</details>

---

**4. Prior to questioning a member who is the subject of an official investigation, the interrogating officer must provide:**

- A) CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS (PD468-121)
- B) NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)
- C) SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150)
- D) Miranda Warning Card

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)**

Prior to questioning, the interrogating officer must provide the member a completed PD339-121 for review and endorsement, allowing reasonable time for review. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**5. A member who is the subject of an official investigation has the right to view which recording prior to questioning?**

- A) Any CCTV footage from the precinct
- B) Their own Body-Worn Camera recording related to the incident
- C) All BWC recordings from all officers at the scene
- D) No recordings may be viewed

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Their own Body-Worn Camera recording related to the incident**

The member will be permitted to view their own BWC recording related to the incident(s) under investigation prior to questioning at a time and place deemed appropriate by the supervisor in charge of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**6. A UMOS who refuses to answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties will be:**

- A) Given a verbal reprimand
- B) Placed on modified assignment
- C) Subject to suspension from duty
- D) Charged with insubordination only

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Subject to suspension from duty**

Members must answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties. Refusal shall result in suspension from duty. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**7. Under the Garrity warning, a member's statements during an official investigation:**

- A) Can be used in both criminal proceedings and departmental charges
- B) Cannot be used in criminal proceedings but CAN be used in departmental charges
- C) Cannot be used in either criminal proceedings or departmental charges
- D) Can be used in criminal proceedings but NOT departmental charges

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Cannot be used in criminal proceedings but CAN be used in departmental charges**

If the member answers, neither their statements nor any information gained by reason of such statements can be used against them in subsequent criminal proceedings. However, the statements may be used against them in relation to subsequent departmental charges. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**8. An interrogation conducted after CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS have been served must be completed at least how many days prior to the Department trial date?**

- A) 5 days
- B) 10 days
- C) 15 days
- D) 20 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 10 days**

An interrogation conducted after service of charges must be completed at least 10 days prior to the date of Department trial, except as directed by the Deputy Commissioner - Trials. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**9. The determination of what constitutes a "reasonable time" for a member to obtain counsel before interrogation is made by:**

- A) The member's commanding officer
- B) The desk officer
- C) The captain (or above) in charge of the investigation
- D) The member's union representative

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The captain (or above) in charge of the investigation**

In all cases, the determination as to what is a reasonable time will be made by the captain (or above) in charge of the investigation, considering the nature of the investigation, timeliness, and stage of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**10. A supervisory officer is mandated to contact the Counseling Services Unit when a member is:**

- A) Placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed due to unfitness
- B) Under criminal investigation only
- C) Transferred to another command
- D) Issued command discipline

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**A) Placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed due to unfitness**

A supervisory officer is mandated in all cases to contact the Counseling Services Unit on behalf of a member who is placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has their firearms removed due to being unfit for duty. (A.G. 318-10)

</details>

---

**11. Which of the following is a prohibited interrogation tactic?**

- A) Recording the interrogation mechanically
- B) Having a line organization representative present
- C) Promises of reward for answering questions
- D) Scheduling interrogation during business hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Promises of reward for answering questions**

Prohibited tactics include: "off the record" questions, offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, disciplinary punishment), and promises of reward for answering questions. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

**12. Questions and answers from an official investigation interrogation may be released outside the Department only with written approval of:**

- A) The Police Commissioner
- B) The Chief of Department
- C) The Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters
- D) The Chief of Internal Affairs

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters**

Questions and answers resulting from interrogation are confidential and not to be revealed or released outside the Department without prior written approval of the Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters. If a subpoena duces tecum is received, contact the Legal Bureau immediately. (A.G. 318-11)

</details>

---

# Section 319 — Civilian Personnel

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 319
- **Source PDF:** A.G. 319-08
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know the procedure for reporting and documenting civilian member on-duty injuries
- Identify the 5 required forms for civilian injury reporting
- Understand notification timelines for Civilian Employee Services Unit

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 319-08 | Civilian Member - Injury | completed |
| A.G. 319-15 | (Not available) | blocked |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-319-civilian-injury.md` | A.G. 319-08: Injury reporting, 5 required forms, notification timelines, Workers Compensation |
| `key-terms.md` | Forms list, key timeframes, Chief of Personnel authority |
| `review-questions.md` | 4 multiple-choice questions covering civilian injury reporting |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **7-day deadline:** Civilian member must report injury within 7 days
- **5 forms:** WCS-110, WCS-100, WCS-120, DP2002, Time Usage 49
- **Notification:** Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately (business hours) or 0900 next day; copies within 24 hours
- **Leave authority:** Chief of Personnel grants leave with pay for first week (Workers Compensation)

---

# Section 319 — Civilian Member Injury

## A.G. 319-08: Civilian Member - Injury

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 106 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To report injury of a civilian member while on duty.

### Civilian Member Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Report injury to supervisor at time of incident |
| 1a | Injury must be reported **no later than 7 days** after incident |

### Investigating Supervisor Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 2 | Investigate circumstances and ensure preparation of: |
| 2a | Employee Statement Form (**FISA Form WCS-110**) |
| 2b | Supervisor's/Agency Form - "Report of Injury" (**FISA Form WCS-100**) |
| 2c | Witness Statement Form (**FISA Form WCS-120**) |
| 2d | Employee Option Form (**DP2002**) |
| 2e | Time Usage 49 (Time Used Due to Job Injury - Workers Compensation) |
| 3 | Notify **Civilian Employee Services Unit** immediately by telephone during business hours, or at **0900 hours** following business day |
| 3a | Forward copies to Civilian Employee Services Unit within **24 hours** of notification |

### Key Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| Employee Notice of Injury and Employee Option Form obtained from Civilian Employee Services Unit |
| Chief of Personnel is empowered to grant **leave with pay** for first week's absence of a member covered by Workers Compensation who is physically disabled in performance of official duties |

> **Exam Alert:** Civilian member must report injury within **7 days**. Supervisor must notify Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately (or 0900 following business day) and forward copies within **24 hours**.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Know the 5 forms required: WCS-110 (Employee Statement), WCS-100 (Supervisor Report of Injury), WCS-120 (Witness Statement), DP2002 (Employee Option), and Time Usage 49. Chief of Personnel grants leave with pay for first week.

---

# Section 319 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Forms

| Form | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **Employee Statement Form (FISA WCS-110)** | Civilian member's statement of injury |
| **Supervisor's/Agency Form - Report of Injury (FISA WCS-100)** | Supervisor's report of civilian injury |
| **Witness Statement Form (FISA WCS-120)** | Witness account of civilian injury |
| **Employee Option Form (DP2002)** | Civilian member's option selection |
| **Time Usage 49** | Time Used Due to Job Injury - Workers Compensation |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Civilian member reports injury | No later than **7 days** after incident |
| Supervisor notifies Civilian Employee Services Unit | **Immediately** (business hours) or **0900** following business day |
| Forward copies to Civilian Employee Services Unit | Within **24 hours** of notification |

## Key Authority

| Authority | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| **Chief of Personnel** | Empowered to grant leave with pay for first week's absence (Workers Compensation, disabled in line of duty) |

---

# Section 319 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. A civilian member must report an on-duty injury no later than:**

- A) 24 hours after the incident
- B) 3 days after the incident
- C) 7 days after the incident
- D) 30 days after the incident

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 7 days after the incident**

The civilian member must report injury to supervisor at time of incident, and the injury must be reported no later than 7 days after the incident. (A.G. 319-08)

</details>

---

**2. The investigating supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit and forward copies within:**

- A) Immediately by telephone; copies within 12 hours
- B) Immediately by telephone during business hours; copies within 24 hours
- C) By end of tour; copies within 48 hours
- D) Within 24 hours; copies within 72 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Immediately by telephone during business hours; copies within 24 hours**

The supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately by telephone during business hours (or at 0900 following business day) and forward copies within 24 hours of notification. (A.G. 319-08)

</details>

---

**3. Who is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a civilian member covered by Workers Compensation?**

- A) The commanding officer
- B) The desk officer
