- C) The Chief of Personnel
- D) The Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The Chief of Personnel**

The Chief of Personnel is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a member covered by Workers Compensation who is physically disabled in the performance of official duties. (A.G. 319-08)

</details>

---

**4. How many forms must the investigating supervisor ensure are prepared when a civilian member is injured on duty?**

- A) 3
- B) 4
- C) 5
- D) 6

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 5**

Five forms: Employee Statement Form (FISA WCS-110), Supervisor's Report of Injury (FISA WCS-100), Witness Statement Form (FISA WCS-120), Employee Option Form (DP2002), and Time Usage 49. (A.G. 319-08)

</details>

---

# Section 329 — Career Development

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 329
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 329-06, A.G. 329-07
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know the retirement process including terminal leave computation and PPF reporting
- Understand LEOSA certification requirements for retired members
- Know the property return matrix for retiring and resigning members
- Understand resignation procedures, reinstatement rules, and vacation accrual impact

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 329-06 | Discontinuance of Police Service — Retirement or Vested Interest | completed |
| A.G. 329-07 | Discontinuance of Police Service — Resignation | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-329-retirement.md` | A.G. 329-06: Terminal leave computation, Tier 2/3 reporting, LEOSA, property return; A.G. 329-07: Resignation procedures, reinstatement rules, Operations Unit notifications |
| `key-terms.md` | Terminal leave, LEOSA, key forms, timeframes, terminal leave restrictions, resignation rules |
| `review-questions.md` | 12 multiple-choice questions covering retirement and resignation procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **Terminal leave:** 1 month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20; no leaves after terminal leave begins
- **Tier 2 vs Tier 3:** Tier 2 = 30 days advance to PPF, retire by 63; Tier 3 = no notice required, retire by 62
- **LEOSA:** Requires qualification within 12 months, 20+ years service or LOD injury, good standing
- **Resignation:** Bars reinstatement except Police Commissioner within 1 year; firearms to borough Property Clerk ONLY
- **Property accountability:** Benefits not returned until ALL property accounted for

---

# Section 329 — Retirement and Resignation

## A.G. 329-06: Discontinuance of Police Service — Retirement or Vested Interest

**Date Effective:** 03/03/26 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 22 | **Pages:** 6

### Purpose

To apply for discontinuance of police service (retirement or vested interest).

### Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Terminal Leave** | Leave with pay, computed at Police Pension Fund (PPF), granted to retiring UMOS upon request |
| **LEOSA Certification Card** | Wallet-sized card exempting retired law enforcement officers from local/state prohibitions on carrying concealed firearms; must be accompanied by valid IDENTIFICATION CARD marked "Retired" |

### Terminal Leave Computation

| Service | Leave Granted |
|---------|--------------|
| Per 10 years of service | One calendar month (28, 30, or 31 days depending on month) |
| Beyond 20 years | 3 days per completed year of service |
| Beyond 20 years (partial) | 1 day per completed 4 months of service |

### Terminal Leave NOT Granted When

| Condition |
|----------|
| Lump Sum payment option selected |
| Discontinuing with vested interest |
| Disciplinary charges are pending |
| Time spent on extended leave without pay (unless mandated by law) |

### Prior to Reporting to PPF — Member Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Notify operations coordinator |
| 2 | Email PPF at retirement@nycppf.org for paperwork and appointment |
| 3 | Confer with timekeeper to determine current leave balances |
| 4 | Prepare and forward **PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)** to CO/designee |
| 5 | Prepare digital Leave of Absence Request(s) to use accrued pre-separation leave |

### Reporting to PPF

| Tier | Requirement |
|------|-------------|
| **Tier 2** | Must report at least **30 calendar days** in advance of discontinuance |
| **Tier 3** | May report **without notice** at time of discontinuance |
| All members (terminal leave) | Requested (not required) to report to PPF **10 days** prior to commencement of terminal leave |

### Property Return — Member Delivers

| Property | Deliver To |
|----------|-----------|
| NYC Transit Police Pass, LIRR/Metro-North Police Passes | Desk officer → Employee Resources Section |
| NYPD Restricted Parking Permit, HQ Annex Parking Permit | Integrity control officer/counterpart |
| NYPD Vehicle Identification Plate (Misc. 740) | Chief of Department's Vehicle Identification Unit |
| Ballistic Tactical Helmet, Tactical Retreat Hood | Quartermaster Section, CBRN Unit |
| Body-Worn Camera | ITB Telecommunications Unit |
| Portable radio, laptop/tablet, cellular telephone, MIFI/VPN | ITB Telecommunications Unit |
| Shield and IDENTIFICATION CARD | Shield, ID, and Uniform Services Unit (after PPF conferral) |
| Authorized firearms (if not LEOSA qualified and not applying for handgun license) | Manhattan Property Clerk |
| Helmet and O.C. pepper spray with holster | Firearms and Tactics Section, Police HQ Range |
| Bullet resistant vest | Police Academy, Ballistic Vest Unit (optional — may retain) |

### LEOSA Certification Requirements

| Requirement |
|------------|
| Must have **qualified during regular Department range cycle within past 12 months** |
| Card expires **12 months** from date of last range requalification |
| At least **20 years of service** OR retiring due to **line of duty injury** |
| Separating in **"good standing"** |
| Tier 3: Not issued retired ID or LEOSA card until **30 days** after discontinuance (unless reported to PPF 30 days prior) |

### Terminal Leave Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| Members continue to accrue chart time while exhausting leave balances **except while on terminal leave** |
| No leaves of any kind may be granted after commencement of terminal leave |
| May NOT be placed on sick report unless from **line of duty action while on terminal leave** |
| Tier 2: Must retire no later than midnight of eve of **63rd birthday** |
| Tier 3: Must retire no later than midnight of eve of **62nd birthday** |
| Members on terminal leave are **excluded from excusal limitations** — available only for **emergency situations** |
| Members ordered to work on terminal leave compensated with **cash overtime only** |
| COs should NOT approve programmatic overtime, fill details, or non-emergency duties for these members (except with bureau chief/DC approval) |

### Firearm Retention (Post-1/15/13 Issued Firearms)

| Requirement |
|------------|
| Obtain **NICS check** through Federal Firearms Licensee (FFL) no more than **30 days** prior to separation |
| Deliver completed **ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS BY POLICE OFFICERS - REPORT TO N.Y. STATE POLICE (PD424-150)** to Firearms and Tactics Section prior to separation |

> **Exam Alert:** Tier 2 must report to PPF **30 calendar days** in advance. Terminal leave = 1 month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20. LEOSA card requires qualification within **12 months** and **20 years of service** or line of duty injury.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Know the property return matrix. Tier 2 mandatory retirement age is **63**; Tier 3 is **62**. Members on terminal leave are only available for **emergencies**. No leaves after terminal leave starts.

---

## A.G. 329-07: Discontinuance of Police Service — Resignation

**Date Effective:** 01/06/26 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 3 | **Pages:** 5

### Purpose

To facilitate the resignation of a uniformed member of the service.

### Definition

**RESIGNATION** — Voluntary separation from the Department other than retirement/vested interest.

### Key Rule — Reinstatement

Resignation **bars reinstatement** EXCEPT if approved by the **Police Commissioner** within **one year** of date of resignation.

### CO Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 2 | Instruct member that resignation bars reinstatement (except Police Commissioner approval within 1 year) |
| 3 | Request member complete RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW (PD452-151) |
| 4 | Interview member and record responses |
| 5 | Sign and distribute RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within **5 business days** |
| 6 | Have command clerk prepare PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013) |
| 7 | Sign PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE |

### Desk Officer Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 8 | Make Command Log entry of resignation |
| 9 | Obtain NYC Transit OMNY Police Pass, LIRR/Metro-North passes |
| 10 | Notify **Operations Unit** of resignation — provide 8 items of information |
| 11 | Notify Occupational Safety and Health Section if member indicated feeling unsafe |

### Operations Unit Notifications (Upon Learning of Resignation)

| Notified |
|---------|
| Communications Section (FINEST Message) |
| Internal Affairs Bureau |
| Payroll Section |
| Police Pension Fund |
| Human Resources Division |

### Last Working Day

| Rule |
|------|
| Assign member to **2nd platoon** on last working day |
| If last day falls on Saturday/Sunday → report to PPF on Friday; perform clerical duty in command on Saturday/Sunday |
| Report to PPF on last working day in **civilian clothes** |
| Deliver firearms to **borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY** |

### If Member Resigns Other Than In Person

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 43 | Notify Operations Unit |
| 44 | Notify permanent command |
| 45 | C.O., Personnel Orders Division: mail certified letter acknowledging receipt and acceptance |
| 46 | Attempt personal communication to determine location of property/firearms |
| 49 | Assign **sergeant and clerical member** to open locker and obtain Department property |

### Vacation Accrual Note

| Rule |
|------|
| Members in rank of PO through deputy chief receive entire vacation allotment on January 1 |
| Vacation time only **accrued** while in full pay status |
| Members who resign, are terminated, or take leave without pay — vacation accruals are **prorated** |
| Using vacation in excess of accrual rates prior to resigning → required to **forfeit pay** and/or reimburse Department |
| Resigning in good standing after 1 year of service → compensated for remaining accrued time |

> **Exam Alert:** Resignation bars reinstatement except by **Police Commissioner within 1 year**. Firearms delivered to **borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY**. If member won't report to PPF, CO accepts property. Assign **sergeant** to open locker for absent resignees.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Ensure 8 items of info provided to Operations Unit. Distribute RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within **5 business days**. Members must report to PPF in **civilian clothes**. Vacation is prorated upon resignation.

---

# Section 329 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Retirement Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Terminal Leave** | Leave with pay computed at PPF; 1 calendar month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20 + 1 day per 4 months |
| **LEOSA Certification Card** | Wallet-sized card exempting retired LEOs from local/state concealed carry prohibitions; must accompany retired ID card |
| **Vested Interest** | Discontinuance of police service with pension benefit eligibility but not full retirement |
| **Resignation** | Voluntary separation other than retirement/vested interest |
| **Dismissal Probation** | Period included in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents |
| **NICS Check** | National Instant Criminal Background Check — required to retain post-1/15/13 Department-issued firearms |

## Key Forms

| Form | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)** | Property return documentation for retiring/resigning members |
| **PENSION APPLICATION (PD440-045)** | Grants terminal leave |
| **RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW (PD452-151)** | Completed and signed by resigning member |
| **PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE (PD521-141)** | Lists firearms safeguarded |
| **ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS (PD424-150)** | Report to NY State Police for each firearm |
| **HANDGUN LICENSE APPLICATION (PD643-041)** | Typed and notarized; for NYC residents applying for handgun license upon retirement |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| Tier 2 reporting to PPF | At least **30 calendar days** in advance |
| Tier 3 reporting to PPF | May report **without notice** |
| Terminal leave PPF reporting | Requested **10 days** prior (not required) |
| LEOSA card expiration | **12 months** from last range requalification |
| NICS check | Within **30 days** prior to separation |
| RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW distribution | Within **5 business days** |
| Reinstatement after resignation | Only by **Police Commissioner** within **1 year** |
| Tier 2 mandatory retirement age | **63** |
| Tier 3 mandatory retirement age | **62** |
| Tier 3 retired ID/LEOSA issuance | **30 days** after discontinuance |
| Terminal leave discontinuance — vacation eligibility | Not for **30 days** after |

## Terminal Leave Restrictions

| Restriction |
|------------|
| No leaves of any kind after terminal leave begins |
| No sick report (unless LOD action while on terminal leave) |
| Excluded from excusal limitations |
| Available only for **emergency situations** |
| Cash overtime only if ordered to work |
| No programmatic overtime, fill details, or non-emergency duties (without bureau chief/DC approval) |

## Resignation Key Rules

| Rule |
|------|
| Bars reinstatement except by Police Commissioner within 1 year |
| Benefits/pension contributions NOT returned until ALL property accounted for |
| Police Commissioner decides if resignation is "without permission" |
| Firearms delivered to **borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY** |
| Report to PPF on last working day in **civilian clothes** |
| Sergeant and clerical member assigned to open locker of absent resignee |

---

# Section 329 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. Terminal leave for a uniformed member with 20 years of service is computed as:**

- A) 30 calendar days
- B) One calendar month per 10 years of service
- C) Two calendar months
- D) 60 calendar days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) One calendar month per 10 years of service**

One calendar month (28, 30, or 31 days depending on the month) of leave is granted for each 10 years of service. Beyond 20 years, 3 days per completed year and 1 day per completed 4 months. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**2. A Tier 2 uniformed member must report to the Police Pension Fund:**

- A) At least 10 calendar days in advance
- B) At least 30 calendar days in advance
- C) Without notice
- D) At least 60 calendar days in advance

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) At least 30 calendar days in advance**

Tier 2 members must report at least 30 calendar days in advance of discontinuance of service. Tier 3 members may report without notice. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**3. A LEOSA Certification Card expires:**

- A) 6 months from date of last range requalification
- B) 12 months from date of last range requalification
- C) 24 months from date of retirement
- D) It does not expire

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 12 months from date of last range requalification**

The LEOSA Certification Card will expire 12 months from the date of the member's last range requalification. The member must have qualified during a regular Department range cycle within the past 12 months. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**4. After commencement of terminal leave, which of the following may occur?**

- A) Member may be granted vacation
- B) Member may be placed on sick report for any illness
- C) No leaves of any kind may be granted
- D) Member may request comp time

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) No leaves of any kind may be granted**

No leaves of any kind may be granted after commencement of terminal leave, nor may the member be placed on sick report, unless such sick report results from action taken in the line of duty while on terminal leave. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**5. Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by:**

- A) The Chief of Department within 6 months
- B) The Police Commissioner within one year
- C) The First Deputy Commissioner within 2 years
- D) The Chief of Personnel within 90 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) The Police Commissioner within one year**

Resignation bars reinstatement except if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year of date of resignation. (A.G. 329-07)

</details>

---

**6. When a UMOS resigns, the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW form must be distributed within:**

- A) 24 hours
- B) 3 business days
- C) 5 business days
- D) 10 business days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 5 business days**

The commanding officer signs and distributes the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within five business days. (A.G. 329-07)

</details>

---

**7. A resigning member's firearms must be delivered to:**

- A) Manhattan Property Clerk
- B) Firearms and Tactics Section
- C) Borough Property Clerk's Office only
- D) The member's permanent command

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Borough Property Clerk's Office only**

The resigning member must deliver firearms with PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE and ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS report to the borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY. (A.G. 329-07)

</details>

---

**8. Tier 2 uniformed members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their:**

- A) 60th birthday
- B) 62nd birthday
- C) 63rd birthday
- D) 65th birthday

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 63rd birthday**

Tier 2 members are required by law to retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 63rd birthday. Tier 3 members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 62nd birthday. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**9. Members on terminal leave are available for duty only during:**

- A) Regular scheduled tours
- B) Emergency situations
- C) Programmatic overtime
- D) Detail assignments

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Emergency situations**

Members on terminal leave, or using accrued time attached to terminal leave, are excluded from excusal limitations and are only available for duty during emergency situations (natural disaster, etc.) or court must-appear notifications. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**10. A UMOS wishing to retain a Department-issued service firearm (issued after 1/15/13) must obtain a NICS check within how many days prior to separation?**

- A) 15 days
- B) 30 days
- C) 60 days
- D) 90 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 30 days**

Members must obtain a NICS check through a Federal Firearms Licensee (FFL) no more than 30 days prior to separation from the Department. (A.G. 329-06)

</details>

---

**11. If a UMOS resigns other than in person, who is assigned to open the member's locker?**

- A) The desk officer
- B) A sergeant and clerical member of the service
- C) The commanding officer personally
- D) Internal Affairs Bureau

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) A sergeant and clerical member of the service**

The desk officer assigns a sergeant and clerical member of the service to open the member's locker and obtain Department property. (A.G. 329-07)

</details>

---

**12. Upon learning of a member's resignation, the Operations Unit notifies all of the following EXCEPT:**

- A) Communications Section
- B) Internal Affairs Bureau
- C) Payroll Section
- D) The member's union representative

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**D) The member's union representative**

Operations Unit notifies: Communications Section (FINEST Message), Internal Affairs Bureau, Payroll Section, Police Pension Fund, and Human Resources Division. The union representative is not on this notification list. (A.G. 329-07)

</details>

---

# Section 330 — Medical, Health and Wellness

## Source
- **Administrative Guide Section:** 330
- **Source PDFs:** A.G. 330-03, A.G. 330-07, A.G. 330-09
- **Priority:** Medium

## Learning Objectives
- Know the complete line of duty injury investigation and reporting process
- Understand Sick Desk notification triggers for suspicious LOD claims
- Master the hospital privacy rules for injured members
- Know the Trauma Counseling Program scope, timeline, and confidentiality rules
- Understand infectious disease exposure procedures, universal precautions, and decontamination
- Know needle handling procedures and bleach solution protocols

## Procedures

| Procedure | Title | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| A.G. 330-03 | Line of Duty Injury or Death | completed |
| A.G. 330-07 | Trauma Counseling Program | completed |
| A.G. 330-09 | Exposure to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials | completed |

## Chapter Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `section-330-line-of-duty-injury.md` | A.G. 330-03: LOD injury investigation, Sick Desk triggers, hospital privacy, hospital response for serious injury/death, FECA |
| `section-330-trauma-counseling.md` | A.G. 330-07: Referral triggers, Trauma Team response, 48-hour follow-up, confidentiality, CO duties |
| `section-330-infectious-disease-exposure.md` | A.G. 330-09: Universal precautions, exposure procedures, bleach solution ratio, needle handling, Ryan White Act |
| `key-terms.md` | LOD definitions, trauma counseling terms, infectious disease/hazmat definitions, key forms, timeframes, bleach rules |
| `review-questions.md` | 15 multiple-choice questions covering all three procedures |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- **LOD Injury Report:** Sign off electronically before end of tour; include BWC/video findings
- **Out-of-City LOD:** Investigating supervisor must be captain or above
- **Sick Desk triggers:** Not witnessed, in/near police facility, first 2 hours of tour, off-duty request
- **Hospital privacy:** Only Medical Division address in hospital records — never member's personal info
- **Trauma Counseling:** 48-hour follow-up, strictly confidential, captain+ can request response
- **Infectious disease:** Assign officer who already had contact; patrol supervisor coordinates for multiple exposures
- **Decontamination:** Bleach ratio 1:10, freshly mixed, sink disposal only; one-handed needle scoop technique

---

# Section 330 — Exposure to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials

## A.G. 330-09: Exposure of Members of the Service to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** I.O. 99 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To provide instructions and minimize risk to members of the service (uniformed and civilian) who have contact with, or handle, an animal or person who may have an infectious disease, or who have contact with or handle hazardous materials.

### Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Infectious Disease** | Diseases transmitted by contact with infected animal's saliva/CNS tissue, or infected individual's blood or body fluids |
| **Hazardous Material** | Any chemical, biological, or radiological substance which is a health hazard (carcinogens, toxic agents, irritants, corrosives, flammable/combustible liquids, compressed gas, explosives) |
| **Exposure to Infectious Disease** | Airborne transmission or direct contact with individual suspected of having disease; contact with blood/body fluids; human/animal bite; hypodermic needle puncture; contaminated sharp instrument |
| **Exposure to Hazardous Material** | Exposure through inhalation, ingestion, skin contact, or parenteral contact |
| **Universal Precautions** | Approach to infection control: treat all human blood and certain body fluids as if infectious for HIV, Hepatitis B, or other bloodborne pathogens; treat all animal saliva and CNS tissue as if infectious for rabies |
| **Designated Officer** | Individual designated under 42 U.S.C. 300ff-86 to execute provisions of Ryan White Act regarding emergency response employees requesting exposure determination |

### Scene Management Note

To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has **already had contact** with the person suspected of having an infectious disease. If numerous individuals involved, the **patrol supervisor** coordinates information and documents names of involved MOS.

### Procedure — Upon Exposure

| Role | Step | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Member | 1 | Comply with guidelines in Additional Data |
| Member | 2 | Notify desk officer, precinct of occurrence |
| Desk Officer | 3 | Obtain **exposure report number** via Line of Duty Injury Reporting System |
| Desk Officer | 4 | Make Command Log entry (include exposure report number) |
| Desk Officer | 5 | Notify **designated Department surgeon** of facts |
| Desk Officer | 6 | Contact member and advise of necessary treatment |
| Member | 7 | Comply with directions of Department surgeon |
| Member | 8 | Make digital Activity Log entry (include exposure report number) |

### When Exposure Involves Actual Injury

- Member transported to hospital for treatment
- Comply with **A.G. 330-03** (Line of Duty Injury or Death)

### Tuberculosis Exposure (No Actual Injury)

- Department offers free Mantoux (PPD) skin testing
- If results positive → notify **Unit Safety Officer** to initiate entry on DOSH 900 log
- Code (e.g., maiden name pseudonym) may substitute for description in column F

### Infectious Disease Guidelines

| Guideline |
|----------|
| Confer with EMS, family, friends, neighbors, doctor, or agency about source individual's medical conditions |
| Wear **disposable gloves** if contact with blood/body fluids possible |
| Heavy-duty rubber gloves for routine capture/seizure of suspected rabid animals |
| **Wash hands** with soap and water (disinfectant soap if possible) after removing gloves |
| Immediately wash unprotected body parts if contact with blood/body fluids or animal bite/scratch |
| Machine wash (hot cycle) or dry clean uniform/civilian clothes that contact blood/body fluids |
| Use **extraordinary care** to avoid accidental needle puncture wounds |
| Affix **Biohazard Label** to any property, evidence, or waste that may be contaminated |

### Accidental Blood/Body Fluid Spills

| Rule |
|------|
| Supply of household bleach maintained at all precinct/PSA/transit district commands, ESU, borough court section facilities |
| Clean spills with **FRESHLY MIXED** solution: **1 part bleach : 10 parts water** |
| Bleach must NOT be mixed with any solution other than water |
| Must be freshly mixed for each use |
| Do NOT use bleach solution to clean **uniforms** (will cause damage) — use routine laundering or dry cleaning |
| Do NOT use bleach to cleanse **hands** |
| Dispose of bleach dilutions in **sink only** (not urinal or toilet — may contain chemical deodorizers causing toxic gas) |

### Hypodermic Needle Handling

| Rule |
|------|
| Place in hypodermic needle/syringe container using **one-handed "scoop" technique** |
| Move needle **away** from yourself (hold syringe, move toward container) |
| Do NOT hold container and move hand toward needle |
| Cap container if possible; if syringe extends beyond container, secure with tape |
| **NEVER** attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle from a hypodermic syringe |

### Ryan White Act — Designated Officer

| Rule |
|------|
| Emergency response employees may request determination of exposure through Department's designated officer at Medical Division, Hazmat Unit |
| If exposure determined → signed written request sent to medical facility where victim was transported |
| Designated officer must **immediately** notify member(s) upon receiving notification from medical facility |
| HIV-related information disclosed to member → **redisclosure prohibited** when not in conformance with state law |

> **Exam Alert:** Bleach solution ratio: **1 part bleach : 10 parts water**, freshly mixed. Hypodermic needles handled with **one-handed "scoop" technique** — NEVER remove, dislodge, or bend. Assign the officer who already had contact to continue handling the person.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Coordinate documentation of all involved MOS names when numerous individuals are exposed. Ensure exposure report number obtained via LOD Injury Reporting System. If actual injury → comply with A.G. 330-03. Know the bleach ratio and needle handling rules for roll call training.

---

# Section 330 — Line of Duty Injury or Death

## A.G. 330-03: Line of Duty Injury or Death

**Date Effective:** 02/03/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 8 | **Pages:** 5

### Purpose

To report and record line of duty injuries and deaths occurring within the City or outside of the City.

### Definition

**INVESTIGATING SUPERVISOR** — A supervisory UMOS in the rank of **sergeant or above** (patrol supervisor, platoon commander, duty captain, etc.) assigned to the injured UMOS's command. If unavailable, a supervisory UMOS within the precinct of occurrence.

### Injured Member Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Request supervisor to respond |
| 2 | Notify desk officer of: (a) circumstances of injury, (b) names/addresses of witnesses |
| 3 | Request witnesses to await arrival of supervisor |
| 4 | Remain at scene unless: (a) hospitalization/medical attention required, (b) further police action necessary |
| 5 | Notify local police authorities and **Operations Unit immediately** if reporting LOD injury from **outside of City** |

### Investigating Supervisor Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 6 | Respond to scene and request medical attention if necessary |
| 7 | Interview injured member and investigate circumstances |
| 8 | Interview witnesses and ensure preparation of: (a) WITNESS STATEMENT - INJURY TO MEMBER OF THE DEPARTMENT (PD429-065) for non-Department witnesses, (b) Line of Duty Injury Reporting System witness statement for MOS witnesses |
| 9 | Prepare Police Accident Report (MV104AN) if vehicle collision involved |
| 10 | Direct injured member to prepare AIDED REPORT (PD304-152) if no vehicle involved |
| 11 | Comply with P.G. 221-06 if any force used by UMOS or civilian and UMOS sustains injury |
| 12 | Prepare Line of Duty Injury Report if injured member is incapacitated |

### Out of City LOD Injuries

Investigating supervisor must be in the rank of **captain or above**.

### Line of Duty Injury Report

| Rule |
|------|
| Prepared using **Line of Duty Injury Reporting System** on Department intranet or smartphone |
| Include: witness information, documents related to injury (photographs, hospital discharge paperwork) |
| Submitted electronically to investigating supervisor |
| Investigating supervisor reviews for completeness/accuracy and signs off electronically **prior to completion of tour** (absent exceptional circumstances) |
| Include recommendation for approval/disapproval of LOD designation |
| Include findings of review of any available **BWC or other video surveillance** |

### Desk Officer Notifications

| Notify When |
|-------------|
| Injured member's command (if not assigned to precinct of occurrence) |
| Borough commander, CO/XO/duty captain, Operations Unit, Medical Examiner — if member **dies or likely to die** |
| Operations Unit — if member requires **hospital treatment** |
| Sick Desk supervisor when any of these conditions exist: |

### Sick Desk Notification Triggers

| Condition |
|----------|
| Incident causing injury was NOT specifically witnessed |
| Injury occurred within any **police facility** (building, office, etc.) |
| Injury occurred in immediate vicinity of police facility (parking lot, perimeter sidewalk) |
| Injury occurred during **first two hours of a tour** |
| Any request for LOD designation by an **off-duty** UMOS |

### CO Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 17 | Review all LOD Injury Reports via Reporting System application |
| 18 | Prepare Typed Letterhead to CO, Medical Division when: (a) injured member is **PPO**, (b) injury occurred while **off-duty**, (c) injury reported **after end of tour** |
| 19 | Prepare Typed Letterhead for LOD death or serious injury/likely to die → forward per P.G. 212-09 "Unusual Occurrence Reports" |
| 20 | Comply with A.G. 330-07, "Trauma Counseling Program" |
| 21 | Forward copies to PPF if LOD death or serious injury/likely to die |

### Hospital Privacy Rule

When treated at hospital for LOD injury, **ONLY** the following address/phone may be entered in hospital records:
- **Medical Division**, 1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368
- Member's command, home address, and telephone number will **NOT** be disclosed unless authorized supervisory officer determines requesting person is entitled

### When Member Is Hospitalized or Dies

Patrol supervisor ensures:
- Firearms, Department property (shield, ID, nameplate), and personal effects are **removed and safeguarded**
- Member struck by bullet on protective vest and apparently uninjured → must be examined by doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury

### Hospital Response — Serious Injury/Death in Line of Duty

Operations Unit notifies the following to respond to hospital:

| Respond | Also Respond |
|---------|-------------|
| Police Commissioner | Commanding Officer, Family Assistance Section |
| First Deputy Commissioner (if PC unavailable) | Lt, Family Assistance Section (borough concerned) |
| Chief of Department | Patrol borough CO |
| Chief of Detectives | CO/duty captain (prepares UOR) |
| Bureau chief of injured/deceased | Detective borough commander |
| Deputy Commissioner, Public Information | Detective assigned to investigate |
| Supervising Chief Surgeon | Crime Scene Unit detective/technician |
| Department Chaplain | Line organization representative |
| Duty inspector (control access to ER) | EAU representative |
| Patrol borough operations commander (if DI unavailable) | POPPA representative |

### Permitted to Speak to Injured/View Deceased

| Permitted |
|----------|
| Police Commissioner, First Deputy Commissioner, Chief of Department |
| Bureau chief concerned, Patrol borough CO (or rep) |
| Department Chaplain |
| Detective assigned to case, Crime Scene Unit detective/technician |
| Union representative |
| Policewomen's Endowment Association representative (if female-identifying PO) |
| ESU member (if transported by Department ambulance) |

### FECA (Federal Employees Compensation Act)

| Rule |
|------|
| Members may be entitled to medical and financial benefits under FECA if a **Federal statute** was violated and injury occurred while apprehending |
| Investigating supervisor must indicate on LOD Injury Report whether FECA applies |
| If FECA applies → CO forwards items to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within **90 days** |

### Delayed LOD Designation

| Rule |
|------|
| If designation cannot be made expeditiously → Typed Letterhead with reasons forwarded to Medical Division within **60 days** |
| Subsequent reports every **60 days** thereafter if necessary |

> **Exam Alert:** Investigating supervisor for out-of-city LOD injuries must be **captain or above**. Sick Desk must be notified when injury is during first **2 hours** of tour, in/near police facility, not witnessed, or off-duty request. Hospital records receive **Medical Division address only** — not member's personal info.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Sign off LOD Injury Report electronically **before end of tour**. Include BWC/video review findings. If member dies or likely to die, ensure firearms and Department property are safeguarded. Comply with P.G. 221-06 if any force was used. FECA indicator required on LOD report.

---

# Section 330 — Trauma Counseling Program

## A.G. 330-07: Trauma Counseling Program

**Date Effective:** 03/24/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 14 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide Trauma Counseling Services for members of the service (uniformed and civilian) involved in shooting incidents, disasters, or other violent occurrences resulting in death or injury.

### Scope — Referral Triggers

A member will be referred to the Trauma Counseling Program when involved in:

| Trigger |
|---------|
| Member is **shot or otherwise seriously injured** |
| Member **discharges a weapon** causing injury or death |
| Member causes, accidentally or otherwise, **serious physical injury or death** to another |
| Member directly involved where their **partner was killed or seriously injured** |
| Member directly involved in incidents or serious disasters where **multiple serious injuries and deaths** occurred |

### Trauma Counseling Team Response — Mandatory

The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to **all** incidents involving:
- Member of the service is shot, killed, or seriously injured in performance of duty
- Any incident pursuant to a request from a **captain or above**

### Procedure

| Role | Step | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Desk Officer | 1 | Notify **Operations Unit** and provide details |
| Operations Unit Supervisor | 2 | Notify **Sick Desk supervisor**, provide details, request Trauma Counseling Team response |
| Sick Desk Supervisor | 3 | Ascertain location for team response and name of contact supervisor at scene |
| Sick Desk Supervisor | 4 | Arrange transportation of Trauma Team |
| Ranking Supervisor at Scene | 5 | Ensure members needing counseling are **available for interview** upon team arrival |
| Ranking Supervisor at Scene | 6 | Provide suitable place for trauma interview |

### Trauma Counseling Team Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 7 | Respond to location and report to designated contact person |
| 8 | Respond to stationhouse of precinct of occurrence or other location to make contact with involved member(s) |
| 9 | Visit place of occurrence if necessary |
| 10 | Notify member they will be contacted for follow-up meeting |

### Follow-Up Schedule

| Timeline | Action |
|----------|--------|
| **48 hours** after event or initial contact | Follow-up meeting |
| **1 to 6 weeks** after incident | Additional counseling/interview sessions |

### Member Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 11 | Cooperate with Trauma Counseling Team and keep appointments |
| 12 | Report to **Behavioral Care and Support Section** or other location **48 hours** after incident or initial contact |
| 12a | Report for additional follow-up meetings as scheduled |

### CO/Duty Captain Duties

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 13 | Cooperate with Trauma Counseling Team and permit access to involved members |
| 14 | Verify that involved members have been referred and contacted in person |
| 15 | Have **UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT (PD370-152)** prepared listing involved members contacted by Trauma Team |
| 16 | Forward copies of UOR, T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORT (if prepared), and other reports to CO, Medical Division |

### Confidentiality

**ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL.**

### Additional Data

| Fact |
|------|
| Trauma Counseling Team staffed by Chief Surgeon, Director of Behavioral Care and Support Section, and licensed certified professionals of Medical Division |
| Team members will **NOT interfere** with ongoing investigations |
| Team members will NOT be assigned duties outside their Trauma Counselor functions when responding |
| Services also available for **personal or family tragedies** (violent criminal incidents, death in family, etc.) |
| Does NOT prevent voluntary use of or referral to any Employee Assistance Programs |
| Team members wear authorized nylon windbreaker jackets with **NYPD logo** on front and "N.Y.C. POLICE" on back |

> **Exam Alert:** Follow-up meeting at **48 hours** after event. Additional sessions within **1 to 6 weeks**. All conversations are **strictly confidential**. Trauma Team will NOT interfere with investigations.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Ensure involved members are available for Trauma Team interview. Verify members have been contacted in person. Prepare UOR listing contacted members. Captain or above can request Trauma Team response to any incident.

---

# Section 330 — Key Terms and Definitions

## Line of Duty Injury

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Investigating Supervisor** | Sergeant or above from injured member's command; if unavailable, from precinct of occurrence |
| **Out of City LOD** | Investigating supervisor must be **captain or above** |
| **Line of Duty Injury Report** | Prepared via LOD Injury Reporting System (intranet or smartphone) |
| **FECA** | Federal Employees Compensation Act — benefits when Federal statute violated during apprehension |

## Trauma Counseling

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Trauma Counseling Team** | Staffed by Chief Surgeon, Director of Behavioral Care and Support Section, and licensed professionals of Medical Division |
| **Behavioral Care and Support Section** | Location for 48-hour follow-up meeting |
| **POPPA** | Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance — separate from Trauma Counseling |
| **Employee Assistance Programs** | Multiple programs available; Trauma Counseling does not prevent voluntary use of any |

## Infectious Disease/Hazmat

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Infectious Disease** | Diseases transmitted by contact with blood, body fluids, animal saliva, or CNS tissue |
| **Hazardous Material** | Any chemical, biological, or radiological health hazard |
| **Universal Precautions** | Treat all blood and body fluids as infectious for HIV, HBV, and other bloodborne pathogens |
| **Designated Officer** | Individual designated under Ryan White Act (42 U.S.C. 300ff-86) for exposure determinations |

## Key Forms

| Form | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **WITNESS STATEMENT - INJURY TO MEMBER (PD429-065)** | Non-Department witness statement for LOD injury |
| **Police Accident Report (MV104AN)** | If vehicle collision involved in LOD injury |
| **AIDED REPORT (PD304-152)** | If no vehicle involved in LOD injury |
| **UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT (PD370-152)** | For LOD death/serious injury; also for Trauma Counseling incidents |
| **SCOC M187** | Report of Inmate Death to NYS Commission of Correction |
| **Biohazard Label** | Affixed to contaminated property, evidence, or waste |

## Key Timeframes

| Item | Timeframe |
|------|-----------|
| LOD Injury Report sign-off by investigating supervisor | **Before end of tour** |
| Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting | **48 hours** after event |
| Additional trauma counseling sessions | **1 to 6 weeks** after incident |
| FECA documentation forwarding | Within **90 days** of occurrence |
| Delayed LOD designation report | Within **60 days** (then every 60 days) |
| Exposure report number | Obtained via LOD Injury Reporting System |

## Sick Desk Notification Triggers (LOD Injury)

| Trigger |
|---------|
| Incident NOT specifically witnessed |
| Injury in or near any **police facility** |
| Injury during **first 2 hours** of tour |
| Off-duty LOD designation request |

## Bleach Solution for Blood/Body Fluid Spills

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Ratio | **1 part bleach : 10 parts water** |
| Preparation | FRESHLY MIXED for each use |
| Do NOT mix with | Any solution other than water |
| Do NOT use on | Uniforms (causes damage) or hands |
| Disposal | In sink only (not urinal/toilet — chemical deodorizers cause toxic gas) |

## Needle Handling

| Rule |
|------|
| One-handed "scoop" technique |
| Move needle AWAY from yourself |
| NEVER remove, dislodge, or bend a needle |
| Cap container if possible; tape if syringe extends |

---

# Section 330 — Review Questions

## Multiple Choice

**1. The investigating supervisor for a line of duty injury must sign off on the LOD Injury Report:**

- A) Within 24 hours
- B) Within 48 hours
- C) Prior to completion of tour
- D) By end of the following tour

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Prior to completion of tour**

The investigating supervisor reviews for completeness and accuracy, and signs off electronically prior to completion of tour, absent exceptional circumstances. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**2. For a line of duty injury occurring outside of New York City, the investigating supervisor must be in the rank of:**

- A) Sergeant or above
- B) Lieutenant or above
- C) Captain or above
- D) Inspector or above

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Captain or above**

The investigating supervisor for out-of-City line of duty injuries must be in the rank of captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**3. The desk officer must notify the Sick Desk supervisor for a LOD injury when:**

- A) The injury occurred during any part of the member's tour
- B) The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour
- C) The member was treated and released from the hospital
- D) The injury was witnessed by multiple members

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour**

Sick Desk notification is required when: incident was not specifically witnessed, injury in/near police facility, injury during first 2 hours of tour, or off-duty LOD designation request. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**4. When a UMOS is treated at a hospital for a LOD injury, which address should be entered in hospital records?**

- A) The member's home address
- B) The precinct of occurrence address
- C) The Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza address
- D) The member's permanent command address

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza address**

Only the Medical Division address (1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368) may be entered in hospital records. Member's command, home address, and telephone number will not be disclosed. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**5. When a UMOS is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured:**

- A) No further action is required
- B) The member may continue duty without medical evaluation
- C) The member must be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury
- D) The member is placed on sick report for 24 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The member must be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury**

When an injured member is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured, the member must be examined by a doctor to ascertain if a blunt body trauma or internal injury has occurred. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**6. The Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting should occur:**

- A) 24 hours after the event
- B) 48 hours after the event or initial contact
- C) One week after the event
- D) 30 days after the event

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 48 hours after the event or initial contact**

The member will be contacted for a follow-up meeting 48 hours after the event or initial contact. Additional counseling/interview sessions are scheduled within 1 to 6 weeks after the incident. (A.G. 330-07)

</details>

---

**7. All conversations with the Trauma Counseling Team are:**

- A) Reported to the commanding officer
- B) Recorded in the Command Log
- C) Strictly confidential
- D) Shared with Internal Affairs Bureau

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Strictly confidential**

ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL. (A.G. 330-07)

</details>

---

**8. The Trauma Counseling Team responds to all incidents involving a member being shot, killed, or seriously injured in the line of duty, AND:**

- A) Any incident at the discretion of the desk officer
- B) Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above
- C) Only when directed by Internal Affairs Bureau
- D) Only firearms discharge incidents

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above**

The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to all incidents involving MOS shot/killed/seriously injured in LOD, and any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above. (A.G. 330-07)

</details>

---

**9. The correct ratio for the bleach solution used to clean blood or body fluid spills is:**

- A) 1 part bleach to 5 parts water
- B) 1 part bleach to 10 parts water
- C) 1 part bleach to 20 parts water
- D) Undiluted bleach

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 1 part bleach to 10 parts water**

Accidental spills may be cleaned by applying a FRESHLY MIXED solution of 1 part household bleach to 10 parts water. The solution must be freshly mixed for each use and must not be mixed with any solution other than water. (A.G. 330-09)

</details>

---

**10. When handling hypodermic needles, members should use the:**

- A) Two-handed grip technique
- B) One-handed "scoop" technique
- C) Pliers and container method
- D) Gloved removal technique

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) One-handed "scoop" technique**

Members use a one-handed "scoop" technique, using the needle itself to pick up the container, moving the needle away from themselves. Members should NOT hold the container and move their hand toward the needle. Under no circumstances should a member attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle. (A.G. 330-09)

</details>

---

**11. When multiple individuals are exposed to an infectious disease at a scene, who coordinates documentation?**

- A) The desk officer
- B) The patrol supervisor at the scene
- C) The first member to arrive
- D) Emergency Medical Services

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) The patrol supervisor at the scene**

If there are numerous individuals involved, the patrol supervisor at the scene will be responsible for the coordination of information and documenting the names of the members of the service involved. (A.G. 330-09)

</details>

---

**12. Under A.G. 330-09, to avoid unnecessary exposure at a scene, control of an aided victim suspected of having an infectious disease should be assigned to:**

- A) The patrol supervisor
- B) EMS personnel only
- C) An officer who has already had contact with the person
- D) The next available officer

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) An officer who has already had contact with the person**

To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has already had contact with a person suspected of having an infectious disease. (A.G. 330-09)

</details>

---

**13. If a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, the ranking officer must forward a Typed Letterhead to Medical Division within:**

- A) 30 days
- B) 60 days
- C) 90 days
- D) 120 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 60 days**

When a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, a report on Typed Letterhead indicating reasons for the delay must be forwarded to Medical Division within 60 days. Subsequent reports are forwarded for each additional 60-day period. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**14. If an injury comes under the Federal Employees Compensation Act (FECA), the CO must forward documentation to Medical Division within:**

- A) 30 days
- B) 60 days
- C) 90 days
- D) 180 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 90 days**

If the injury comes under FECA, the member's CO will forward items (LOD Injury Report, witness statements, arrest history, criminal court complaint, firearms trace, completed DOL form BEC-721a) to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within 90 days of occurrence. (A.G. 330-03)

</details>

---

**15. The bleach solution for cleaning blood spills should be disposed of:**

- A) In any available drain
- B) In a urinal or toilet
- C) In a sink only
- D) In a hazardous waste container

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) In a sink only**

Disposal of bleach or bleach dilutions should be performed only in a sink (not a urinal or toilet, since they sometimes contain chemical deodorizers that may cause toxic gas when mixed with bleach). (A.G. 330-09)

</details>

---
